converted to an int64 without overflow.\\n64: \\tmaxInt64 = 9223372036854774784\\n65: \\t// The smallest SampleValue that can be converted to an int64 without underflow.\\n66: \\tminInt64 = -9223372036854775808\\n67: \\n68: \\t// Max initial size for the pooled points slices.\\n69: \\t// The getHPointSlice and getFPointSlice functions are called with an estimated size which often can be\\n70: \\t// over-estimated.\\n71: \\tmaxPointsSliceSize = 5000\\n72: \\n73: \\t// The default buffer size for points used by the matrix selector.\\n74: \\tmatrixSelectorSliceSize = 16\\n75: )\\n76: \\n77: type engineMetrics struct {\\n78: \\tcurrentQueries       prometheus.Gauge\\n79: \\tmaxConcurrentQueries prometheus.Gauge\\n80: \\tqueryLogEnabled      prometheus.Gauge\\n81: \\tqueryLogFailures     prometheus.Counter\\n82: \\tqueryQueueTime       prometheus.Observer\\n83: \\tqueryPrepareTime     prometheus.Observer\\n84: \\tqueryInnerEval       prometheus.Observer\\n85: \\tqueryResultSort      prometheus.Observer\\n86: \\tquerySamples         prometheus.Counter\\n87: }\\n88: \\n89: type (\\n90: \\t// ErrQueryTimeout is returned if a query timed out during processing.\\n91: \\tErrQueryTimeout string\\n92: \\t// ErrQueryCanceled is returned if a query was canceled during processing.\\n93: \\tErrQueryCanceled string\\n94: \\t// ErrTooManySamples is returned if a query would load more than the maximum allowed samples into memory.\\n95: \\tErrTooManySamples string\\n96: \\t// ErrStorage is returned if an error was encountered in the storage layer\\n97: \\t// during query handling.\\n98: \\tErrStorage struct{ Err error }\\n99: )\\n100: \\n101: func (e ErrQueryTimeout) Error() string {\\n102: \\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\\\"query timed out in %s\\\", string(e))\\n103: }\\n104: \\n105: func (e ErrQueryCanceled) Error() string {\\n106: \\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\\\"query was canceled in %s\\\", string(e))\\n107: }\\n108: \\n109: func (e ErrTooManySamples) Error() string {\\n110: \\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\\\"query processing would load too many samples into memory in %s\\\", string(e))\\n111: }\\n112: \\n113: func (e ErrStorage) Error() string {\\n114: \\treturn e.Err.Error()\\n115: }\\n116: \\n117: // QueryEngine defines the interface for the *promql.Engine, so it can be replaced, wrapped or mocked.\\n118: type QueryEngine interface {\\n119: \\tNewInstantQuery(ctx context.Context, q storage.Queryable, opts QueryOpts, qs string, ts time.Time) (Query, error)\\n120: \\tNewRangeQuery(ctx context.Context, q storage.Queryable, opts QueryOpts, qs string, start, end time.Time, interval time.Duration) (Query, error)\\n121: }\\n122: \\n123: var _ QueryLogger = (*logging.JSONFileLogger)(nil)\\n124: \\n125: // QueryLogger is an interface that can be used to log all the queries logged\\n126: // by the engine.\\n127: type QueryLogger interface {\\n128: \\tslog.Handler\\n129: \\tio.Closer\\n130: }\\n131: \\n132: // A Query is derived from a raw query string and can be run against an engine\\n133: // it is associated with.\\n134: type Query interface {\\n135: \\t// Exec processes the query. Can only be called once.\\n136: \\tExec(ctx context.Context) *Result\\n137: \\t// Close recovers memory used by the query result.\\n138: \\tClose()\\n139: \\t// Statement returns the parsed statement of the query.\\n140: \\tStatement() parser.Statement\\n141: \\t// Stats returns statistics about the lifetime of the query.\\n142: \\tStats() *stats.Statistics\\n143: \\t// Cancel signals that a running query execution should be aborted.\\n144: \\tCancel()\\n145: \\t// String returns the original query string.\\n146: \\tString() string\\n147: }\\n148: \\n149: type PrometheusQueryOpts struct {\\n150: \\t// Enables recording per-step statistics if the engine has it enabled as well. Disabled by default.\\n151: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4@ba16cf5a7437eb3b6b71b74557b7a67e1975ad0f/-/blob/promql/engine.go%3FL1-150\"}",

\t\"go.opentelemetry.io/otel\"\n38: \t\"go.opentelemetry.io/otel/attribute\"\n39: \t\"go.opentelemetry.io/otel/trace\"\n40: \n41: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/model/histogram\"\n42: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/model/labels\"\n43: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/model/timestamp\"\n44: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/model/value\"\n45: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/promql/parser\"\n46: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/promql/parser/posrange\"\n47: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/schema\"\n48: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/storage\"\n49: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/tsdb/chunkenc\"\n50: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/util/annotations\"\n51: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/util/logging\"\n52: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/util/stats\"\n53: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/util/zeropool\"\n54: )\n55: \n56: const (\n57: \tnamespace            = \"prometheus\"\n58: \tsubsystem            = \"engine\"\n59: \tqueryTag             = \"query\"\n60: \tenv                  = \"query execution\"\n61: \tdefaultLookbackDelta = 5 * time.Minute\n62: \n63: \t// The largest SampleValue that can be converted to an int64 without overflow.\n64: \tmaxInt64 = 9223372036854774784\n65: \t// The smallest SampleValue that can be converted to an int64 without underflow.\n66: \tminInt64 = -9223372036854775808\n67: \n68: \t// Max initial size for the pooled points slices.\n69: \t// The getHPointSlice and getFPointSlice functions are called with an estimated size which often can be\n70: \t// over-estimated.\n71: \tmaxPointsSliceSize = 5000\n72: \n73: \t// The default buffer size for points used by the matrix selector.\n74: \tmatrixSelectorSliceSize = 16\n75: )\n76: \n77: type engineMetrics struct {\n78: \tcurrentQueries       prometheus.Gauge\n79: \tmaxConcurrentQueries prometheus.Gauge\n80: \tqueryLogEnabled      prometheus.Gauge\n81: \tqueryLogFailures     prometheus.Counter\n82: \tqueryQueueTime       prometheus.Observer\n83: \tqueryPrepareTime     prometheus.Observer\n84: \tqueryInnerEval during processing.\n91: \tErrQueryTimeout string\n92: \t// ErrQueryCanceled is returned if a query was canceled during processing.\n93: \tErrQueryCanceled string\n94: \t// ErrTooManySamples is returned if a query would load more than the maximum allowed samples into memory.\n95: \tErrTooManySamples string\n96: \t// ErrStorage is returned if an error was encountered in the storage layer\n97: \t// during query handling.\n98: \tErrStorage struct{ Err error }\n99: )\n100: \n101: func (e ErrQueryTimeout) Error() string {\n102: \treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"query timed out in %s\", string(e))\n103: }\n104: \n105: func (e ErrQueryCanceled) Error() string {\n106: \treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"query was canceled in %s\", string(e))\n107: }\n108: \n109: func (e ErrTooManySamples) Error() string {\n110: \treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"query processing would load too many samples into memory in %s\", string(e))\n111: }\n112: \n113: func (e ErrStorage) Error() string {\n114: \treturn e.Err.Error()\n115: }\n116: \n117: // QueryEngine defines the interface for the *promql.Engine, so it can be replaced, wrapped or mocked.\n118: type QueryEngine interface {\n119: \tNewInstantQuery(ctx context.Context, q time.Duration) (Query, error)\n121: }\n122: \n123: var _ QueryLogger = (*logging.JSONFileLogger)(nil)\n124: \n125: // QueryLogger is an interface that can be used to log all the queries logged\n126: \\t\\\"embed\\\"\\n19: \\t\\\"errors\\\"\\n20: \\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n21: \\t\\\"io/fs\\\"\\n22: \\t\\\"math\\\"\\n23: \\t\\\"slices\\\"\\n24: \\t\\\"sort\\\"\\n25: \\t\\\"strconv\\\"\\n26: \\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n27: \\t\\\"testing\\\"\\n28: \\t\\\"time\\\"\\n29: \\n30: \\t\\\"github.com/grafana/regexp\\\"\\n31: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/common/model\\\"\\n32: \\t\\\"github.com/stretchr/testify/require\\\"\\n33: \\n34: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/model/exemplar\\\"\\n35: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/model/histogram\\\"\\n36: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/model/labels\\\"\\n37: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/model/timestamp\\\"\\n38: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/promql\\\"\\n39: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/promql/parser\\\"\\n40: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/promql/parser/posrange\\\"\\n41: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/storage\\\"\\n42: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/util/almost\\\"\\n43: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/util/annotations\\\"\\n44: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/util/convertnhcb\\\"\\n45: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/util/teststorage\\\"\\n46: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/util/testutil\\\"\\n47: )\\n48: \\n49: var (\\n50: \\tpatSpace       = regexp.MustCompile(\\\"[\\\\t ]+\\\")\\n51: \\tpatLoad        = regexp.MustCompile(`^load(?:_(with_nhcb))?\\\\s+(.+?)$`)\\n52: \\tpatEvalInstant = regexp.MustCompile(`^eval(?:_(fail|warn|ordered|info))?\\\\s+instant\\\\s+(?:at\\\\s+(.+?))?\\\\s+(.+)$`)\\n53: \\tpatEvalRange   = regexp.MustCompile(`^eval(?:_(fail|warn|info))?\\\\s+range\\\\s+from\\\\s+(.+)\\\\s+to\\\\s+(.+)\\\\s+step\\\\s+(.+?)\\\\s+(.+)$`)\\n54: \\tpatExpect      = regexp.MustCompile(`^expect\\\\s+(ordered|fail|warn|no_warn|info|no_info)(?:\\\\s+(regex|msg):(.+))?$`)\\n55: \\tpatMatchAny    = regexp.MustCompile(`^.*$`)\\n56: )\\n57: \\n58: const (\\n59: \\tdefaultEpsilon            = 0.000001 // Relative error allowed for sample values.\\n60: \\tDefaultMaxSamplesPerQuery = 10000\\n61: )\\n62: \\n63: type TBRun interface {\\n64: \\ttesting.TB\\n65: \\tRun(string, func(*testing.T)) bool\\n66: }\\n67: \\n68: var testStartTime = time.Unix(0, 0).UTC()\\n69: \\n70: // LoadedStorage returns storage with generated data using the provided load statements.\\n71: // Non-load statements will cause test errors.\\n72: func LoadedStorage(t testutil.T, input string) *teststorage.TestStorage {\\n73: \\ttest, err := newTest(t, input, false, newTestStorage)\\n74: \\trequire.NoError(t, err)\\n75: \\n76: \\tfor _, cmd := range test.cmds {\\n77: \\t\\tswitch cmd.(type) {\\n78: \\t\\tcase *loadCmd:\\n79: \\t\\t\\trequire.NoError(t, test.exec(cmd, nil))\\n80: \\t\\tdefault:\\n81: \\t\\t\\tt.Errorf(\\\"only 'load' commands accepted, got '%s'\\\", cmd)\\n82: \\t\\t}\\n83: \\t}\\n84: \\treturn test.storage.(*teststorage.TestStorage)\\n85: }\\n86: \\n87: // NewTestEngine creates a promql.Engine with enablePerStepStats, lookbackDelta and maxSamples, and returns it.\\n88: func NewTestEngine(tb testing.TB, enablePerStepStats bool, lookbackDelta time.Duration, maxSamples int) *promql.Engine {\\n89: \\treturn NewTestEngineWithOpts(tb, promql.EngineOpts{\\n90: \\t\\tLogger:                   nil,\\n91: \\t\\tReg:                      nil,\\n92: \\t\\tMaxSamples:               maxSamples,\\n93: \\t\\tTimeout:                  100 * time.Second,\\n94: \\t\\tNoStepSubqueryIntervalFn: func(int64) int64 { return durationMilliseconds(1 * time.Minute) },\\n95: \\t\\tEnableAtModifier:         true,\\n96: \\t\\tEnableNegativeOffset:     true,\\n97: \\t\\tEnablePerStepStats:       enablePerStepStats,\\n98: \\t\\tLookbackDelta:            lookbackDelta,\\n99: \\t\\tEnableDelayedNameRemoval: true,\\n100: \\t})\\n101: }\\n102: \\n103: // NewTestEngineWithOpts creates a promql.Engine with opts and returns it.\\n104: func NewTestEngineWithOpts(tb testing.TB, opts promql.EngineOpts) *promql.Engine {\\n105: \\ttb.Helper()\\n106: \\tng := promql.NewEngine(opts)\\n107: \\ttb.Cleanup(func() {\\n108: \\t\\trequire.NoError(tb, ng.Close())\\n109: \\t})\\n110: \\treturn ng\\n111: }\\n112: \\n113: // RunBuiltinTests runs an acceptance test suite against the provided engine.\\n114: func RunBuiltinTests(t TBRun, engine promql.QueryEngine) {\\n115: \\tRunBuiltinTestsWithStorage(t, engine, newTestStorage)\\n116: }\\n117: \\n118: // RunBuiltinTestsWithStorage runs an acceptance test suite against the provided engine and storage.\\n119: func RunBuiltinTestsWithStorage(t TBRun, engine promql.QueryEngine, newStorage func(testutil.T) storage.Storage) {\\n120: \\tt.Cleanup(func() {\\n121: \\t\\tparser.EnableExperimentalFunctions = false\\n122: \\t\\tparser.ExperimentalDurationExpr = false\\n123: \\t})\\n124: \\tparser.EnableExperimentalFunctions = true\\n125: \\tparser.ExperimentalDurationExpr = true\\n126: \\n127: \\tfiles, err := fs.Glob(testsFs, \\\"*/*.test\\\")\\n128: \\trequire.NoError(t, err)\\n129: \\n130: \\tfor _, fn := range files {\\n131: \\t\\tt.Run(fn, func(t *testing.T) {\\n132: \\t\\t\\tcontent, err := fs.ReadFile(testsFs, fn)\\n133: \\t\\t\\trequire.NoError(t, err)\\n134: \\t\\t\\tRunTestWithStorage(t, string(content), engine, newStorage)\\n135: \\t\\t})\\n136: \\t}\\n137: }\\n138: \\n139: // RunTest parses and runs the test against the provided engine.\\n140: func RunTest(t testutil.T, input string, engine promql.QueryEngine) {\\n141: \\tRunTestWithStorage(t, input, engine, newTestStorage)\\n142: }\\n143: \\n144: // RunTestWithStorage parses and runs the test against the provided engine and storage.\\n145: func RunTestWithStorage(t testutil.T, input string, engine promql.QueryEngine, newStorage func(testutil.T) storage.Storage) {\\n146: \\trequire.NoError(t, runTest(t, input, engine, newStorage, false))\\n147: }\\n148: \\n149: // testTest allows tests to be run in \\\"test-the-test\\\" mode (true for\\n150: // testingMode). This is a special mode for testing test code execution itself.\\n151: func testTest(t testutil.T, input string, engine promql.QueryEngine) error {\\n152: \\treturn runTest(t, input, engine, newTestStorage, true)\\n153: }\\n154: \\n155: func runTest(t testutil.T, input string, engine promql.QueryEngine, newStorage func(testutil.T) storage.Storage, testingMode bool) error {\\n156: \\ttest, err := newTest(t, input, testingMode, newStorage)\\n157: \\n158: \\t// Why do this before checking err? newTest() can create the test storage and then return an error,\\n159: \\t// and we want to make sure to clean that up to avoid leaking goroutines.\\n160: \\tdefer func() {\\n161: \\t\\tif test == nil {\\n162: \\t\\t\\treturn\\n163: \\t\\t}\\n164: \\t\\tif test.storage != nil {\\n165: \\t\\t\\ttest.storage.Close()\\n166: \\t\\t}\\n167: \\t\\tif test.cancelCtx != nil {\\n168: \\t\\t\\ttest.cancelCtx()\\n169: \\t\\t}\\n170: \\t}()\\n171: \\n172: \\tif err != nil {\\n173: \\t\\treturn err\\n174: \\t}\\n175: \\n176: \\tfor _, cmd := range test.cmds {\\n177: \\t\\tif err := test.exec(cmd, engine); err != nil {\\n178: \\t\\t\\t// TODO(fabxc): aggregate command errors, yield diffs for result\\n179: \\t\\t\\t// comparison errors.\\n180: \\t\\t\\treturn err\\n181: \\t\\t}\\n182: \\t}\\n183: \\n184: \\treturn nil\\n185: }\\n186: \\n187: // test is a sequence of read and write commands that are run\\n188: // against a test storage.\\n189: type test struct {\\n190: \\ttestutil.T\\n191: \\t// testingMode distinguishes between normal execution and test-execution mode.\\n192: \\ttestingMode bool\\n193: \\n194: \\tcmds []testCommand\\n195: \\n196: \\topen    func(testutil.T) storage.Storage\\n197: \\tstorage storage.Storage\\n198: \\n199: \\tcontext   context.Context\\n200: \\tcancelCtx context.CancelFunc\\n201: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4@ba16cf5a7437eb3b6b71b74557b7a67e1975ad0f/-/blob/promql/promqltest/test.go%3FL1-200\"}",

NewTestEngineWithOpts(tb, promql.EngineOpts{\n90: \t\tLogger:                   nil,\n91: \t\tReg:                      nil,\n92: \t\tMaxSamples:               maxSamples,\n93: \t\tTimeout:                  100 * time.Second,\n94: \t\tNoStepSubqueryIntervalFn: func(int64) int64 { return durationMilliseconds(1 * time.Minute) },\n95: \t\tEnableAtModifier:         true,\n96: \t\tEnableNegativeOffset:     true,\n97: \t\tEnablePerStepStats:       enablePerStepStats,\n98: \t\tLookbackDelta:            lookbackDelta,\n99: \t\tEnableDelayedNameRemoval: true,\n100: \t})\n101: }\n102: \n103: // NewTestEngineWithOpts creates a promql.Engine with opts and returns it.\n104: func NewTestEngineWithOpts(tb testing.TB, opts promql.EngineOpts) *promql.Engine {\n105: \ttb.Helper()\n106: \tng := promql.NewEngine(opts)\n107: \ttb.Cleanup(func() {\n108: \t\trequire.NoError(tb, ng.Close())\n109: \t})\n110: \treturn ng\n111: }\n112: \n113: // RunBuiltinTests runs an acceptance test suite against the provided engine.\n114: func RunBuiltinTests(t TBRun, engine promql.QueryEngine) {\n115: \tRunBuiltinTestsWithStorage(t, engine, newTestStorage)\n116: }\n117: \n118: // RunBuiltinTestsWithStorage runs an acceptance test suite against the provided engine and storage.\n119: func RunBuiltinTestsWithStorage(t TBRun, engine promql.QueryEngine, newStorage func(testutil.T) storage.Storage) {\n120: \tt.Cleanup(func() {\n121: \t\tparser.EnableExperimentalFunctions = false\n122: \t\tparser.ExperimentalDurationExpr = false\n123: \t})\n124: \tparser.EnableExperimentalFunctions = true\n125: \tparser.ExperimentalDurationExpr = true\n126: \n127: \tfiles, err := fs.Glob(testsFs, \"*/*.test\")\n128: \trequire.NoError(t, err)\n129: \n130: \tfor _, fn := range files {\n131: \t\tt.Run(fn, func(t *testing.T) {\n132: \t\t\tcontent, err := fs.ReadFile(testsFs, fn)\n133: \t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n134: \t\t\tRunTestWithStorage(t, string(content), engine, newStorage)\n135: \t\t})\n136: \t}\n137: }\n138: \n139: // RunTest parses and runs the test against the provided engine.\n140: func RunTest(t testutil.T, input string, engine promql.QueryEngine) {\n141: \tRunTestWithStorage(t, input, engine, newTestStorage)\n142: }\n143: \n144: // RunTestWithStorage parses and runs the test against the provided engine and storage.\n145: func RunTestWithStorage(t testutil.T, input string, engine promql.QueryEngine, newStorage func(testutil.T) storage.Storage) {\n146: \trequire.NoError(t, runTest(t, input, engine, newStorage, false))\n147: }\n148: \n149: // testTest allows tests to be run in \"test-the-test\" mode (true for\n150: // testingMode). This is a special mode for testing test code execution itself.\n151: func testTest(t testutil.T, input string, engine promql.QueryEngine) error {\n152: \treturn runTest(t, input, engine, newTestStorage, true)\n153: }\n154: \n155: func runTest(t testutil.T, input string, engine promql.QueryEngine, newStorage func(testutil.T) storage.Storage, testingMode bool) error {\n156: \ttest, err := newTest(t, input, testingMode, newStorage)\n157: \n158: \t// Why do this before checking err? newTest() can create the test storage and then return an error,\n159: \t// and we want to make sure to clean that up to avoid leaking goroutines.\n160: \tdefer func() {\n161: \t\tif test == nil {\n162: \t\t\treturn\n163: \t\t}\n164: \t\tif test.storage != nil {\n165: \t\t\ttest.storage.Close()\n166: \t\t}\n167: \t\tif test.cancelCtx != nil {\n168: \t\t\ttest.cancelCtx()\n169: \t\t}\n170: \t}()\n171: \n172: \tif err != nil {\n173: \t\treturn err\n174: \t}\n175: \n176: \tfor _, cmd := range test.cmds {\n177: \t\tif err := test.exec(cmd, engine); err != nil {\n178: \t\t\t// TODO(fabxc): aggregate command errors, yield diffs for result\n179: \t\t\t// comparison errors.\n180: \t\t\treturn err\n181: \t\t}\n182: \t}\n183: \n184: \treturn nil\n185: }\n186: \n187: // test is a sequence of read and write commands that are run\n188: // against a test storage.\n189: type test struct {\n190: \ttestutil.T\n191: \t// testingMode distinguishes between normal execution and test-execution mode.\n192: \ttestingMode bool\n193: \n194: \tcmds []testCommand\n195: \n196: \topen    func(testutil.T) storage.Storage\n197: \tstorage storage.Storage\n198: \n199: \tcontext   context.Context\n200: \tcancelCtx context.CancelFunc\n201: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4@ba16cf5a7437eb3b6b71b74557b7a67e1975ad0f/-/blob/promql/promqltest/test.go%3FL1-200"
  }
}

nil\\n155: }\\n156: \\n157: // PrometheusVersion contains build information about Prometheus.\\n158: type PrometheusVersion struct {\\n159: \\tVersion   string `json:\\\"version\\\"`\\n160: \\tRevision  string `json:\\\"revision\\\"`\\n161: \\tBranch    string `json:\\\"branch\\\"`\\n162: \\tBuildUser string `json:\\\"buildUser\\\"`\\n163: \\tBuildDate string `json:\\\"buildDate\\\"`\\n164: \\tGoVersion string `json:\\\"goVersion\\\"`\\n165: }\\n166: \\n167: // RuntimeInfo contains runtime information about Prometheus.\\n168: type RuntimeInfo struct {\\n169: \\tStartTime           time.Time `json:\\\"startTime\\\"`\\n170: \\tCWD                 string    `json:\\\"CWD\\\"`\\n171: \\tHostname            string    `json:\\\"hostname\\\"`\\n172: \\tServerTime          time.Time `json:\\\"serverTime\\\"`\\n173: \\tReloadConfigSuccess bool      `json:\\\"reloadConfigSuccess\\\"`\\n174: \\tLastConfigTime      time.Time `json:\\\"lastConfigTime\\\"`\\n175: \\tCorruptionCount     int64     `json:\\\"corruptionCount\\\"`\\n176: \\tGoroutineCount      int       `json:\\\"goroutineCount\\\"`\\n177: \\tGOMAXPROCS struct {\\n186: \\tStatus    status   `json:\\\"status\\\"`\\n187: \\tData      any      `json:\\\"data,omitempty\\\"`\\n188: \\tErrorType string   `json:\\\"errorType,omitempty\\\"`\\n189: \\tError apiFuncResult struct {\\n195: \\tdata      any\\n196: \\terr       *apiError\\n197: \\twarnings  annotations.Annotations\\n198: \\tfinalizer func()\\n199: }\\n200: \\n201: type apiFunc func(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult\\n202: \\n203: // TSDBAdminStats defines the tsdb interfaces used by the v1 API for admin operations as well as statistics.\\n204: type TSDBAdminStats interface {\\n205: \\tCleanTombstones() error\\n206: \\tDelete(ctx context.Context, mint, maxt int64, ms ...*labels.Matcher) error\\n207: \\tSnapshot(dir string, withHead bool) error\\n208: \\tStats(statsByLabelName string, limit int) (*tsdb.Stats, error)\\n209: \\tWALReplayStatus() (tsdb.WALReplayStatus, error)\\n210: \\tBlockMetas() ([]tsdb.BlockMeta, error)\\n211: }\\n212: \\n213: type QueryOpts interface {\\n214: \\tEnablePerStepStats() bool\\n215: \\tLookbackDelta() time.Duration\\n216: }\\n217: \\n218: // API can register a set of endpoints in a router and handle\\n219: // them using the provided storage and query engine.\\n220: type API struct {\\n221: \\tQueryable         storage.SampleAndChunkQueryable\\n222: \\tQueryEngine       promql.QueryEngine\\n223: \\tExemplarQueryable storage.ExemplarQueryable\\n224: \\n225: \\tscrapePoolsRetriever  func(context.Context) ScrapePoolsRetriever\\n226: \\ttargetRetriever       func(context.Context) TargetRetriever\\n227: \\talertmanagerRetriever func(context.Context) AlertmanagerRetriever\\n228: \\trulesRetriever        func(context.Context) RulesRetriever\\n229: \\tnow                   func() time.Time\\n230: \\tconfig                func() config.Config\\n231: \\tflagsMap              map[string]string\\n232: \\tready                 func(http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc\\n233: \\tglobalURLOptions      GlobalURLOptions\\n234: \\n235: \\tdb                  TSDBAdminStats\\n236: \\tdbDir               string\\n237: \\tenableAdmin         bool\\n238: \\tlogger              *slog.Logger\\n239: \\tCORSOrigin          *regexp.Regexp\\n240: \\tbuildInfo           *PrometheusVersion\\n241: \\truntimeInfo         func() (RuntimeInfo, error)\\n242: \\tgatherer            prometheus.Gatherer\\n243: \\tisAgent             bool\\n244: \\tstatsRenderer       StatsRenderer\\n245: \\tnotificationsGetter func() []notifications.Notification\\n246: \\tnotificationsSub    func() (<-chan notifications.Notification, func(), bool)\\n247: \\t// Allows customizing the default mapping\\n248: \\toverrideErrorCode OverrideErrorCode\\n249: \\n250: \\tremoteWriteHandler http.Handler\\n251: \\tremoteReadHandler  http.Handler\\n252: \\totlpWriteHandler   http.Handler\\n253: \\n254: \\tcodecs []Codec\\n255: }\\n256: \\n257: // NewAPI returns an initialized API type.\\n258: func NewAPI(\\n259: \\tqe promql.QueryEngine,\\n260: \\tq storage.SampleAndChunkQueryable,\\n261: \\tap storage.Appendable,\\n262: \\teq storage.ExemplarQueryable,\\n263: \\tspsr func(context.Context) ScrapePoolsRetriever,\\n264: \\ttr func(context.Context) TargetRetriever,\\n265: \\tar func(context.Context) AlertmanagerRetriever,\\n266: \\tconfigFunc func() param string) stats.QueryStats {\n151: \tif param != \"\" {\n152: \t\treturn stats.NewQueryStats(s)\n153: \t}\n154: \treturn nil\n155: }\n156: \n157: // PrometheusVersion contains build information about Prometheus.\n158: type PrometheusVersion struct {\n159: \tVersion   string `json:\"version\"`\n160: \tRevision  string `json:\"revision\"`\n161: \tBranch    string `json:\"branch\"`\n162: \tBuildUser string `json:\"buildUser\"`\n163: \tBuildDate string `json:\"buildDate\"`\n164: \tGoVersion string `json:\"goVersion\"`\n165: }\n166: \n167: // RuntimeInfo contains runtime information about Prometheus.\n168: type RuntimeInfo struct {\n169: \tStartTime           time.Time `json:\"startTime\"`\n170: \tCWD                 string    `json:\"CWD\"`\n171: \tHostname            string    `json:\"hostname\"`\n172: \tServerTime          time.Time `json:\"serverTime\"`\n173: \tReloadConfigSuccess bool      `json:\"reloadConfigSuccess\"`\n174: \tLastConfigTime      time.Time `json:\"lastConfigTime\"`\n175: \tCorruptionCount     int64     `json:\"corruptionCount\"`\n176: \tGoroutineCount      int       `json:\"goroutineCount\"`\n177: \tGOMAXPROCS          int       `json:\"GOMAXPROCS\"`\n178: \tGOMEMLIMIT          int64     `json:\"GOMEMLIMIT\"`\n179: \tGOGC                string    `json:\"GOGC\"`\n180: \tGODEBUG             string    `json:\"GODEBUG\"`\n181: \tStorageRetention    string    `json:\"storageRetention\"`\n182: }\n183: \n184: // Response contains a response to a HTTP API request.\n185: type Response struct {\n186: \tStatus    status   `json:\"status\"`\n187: \tData      any      `json:\"data,omitempty\"`\n188: \tErrorType string   `json:\"errorType,omitempty\"`\n189: \tError     string   `json:\"error,omitempty\"`\n190: \tWarnings  []string `json:\"warnings,omitempty\"`\n191: \tInfos     []string `json:\"infos,omitempty\"`\n192: }\n193: \n194: type apiFuncResult struct {\n195: \tdata      any\n196: \terr       *apiError\n197: \twarnings  annotations.Annotations\n198: \tfinalizer func()\n199: }\n200: \n201: type apiFunc func(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult\n202: \n203: // TSDBAdminStats defines the tsdb interfaces used by the v1 API for admin operations as well as statistics.\n204: type TSDBAdminStats interface {\n205: \tCleanTombstones() error\n206: \tDelete(ctx context.Context, mint, maxt int64, ms ...*labels.Matcher) error\n207: \tSnapshot(dir string, withHead bool) error\n208: \tStats(statsByLabelName string, limit int) (*tsdb.Stats, error)\n209: \tWALReplayStatus() (tsdb.WALReplayStatus, error)\n210: \tBlockMetas() ([]tsdb.BlockMeta, error)\n211: }\n212: \n213: type QueryOpts interface {\n214: \tEnablePerStepStats() bool\n215: \tLookbackDelta() time.Duration\n216: }\n217: \n218: // API can register a set of endpoints in a router and handle\n219: // them using the provided storage and query engine.\n220: type API struct {\n221: \tQueryable         storage.SampleAndChunkQueryable\n222: \tQueryEngine       promql.QueryEngine\n223: \tExemplarQueryable storage.ExemplarQueryable\n224: \n225: \tscrapePoolsRetriever  func(context.Context) ScrapePoolsRetriever\n226: \ttargetRetriever       func(context.Context) TargetRetriever\n227: \talertmanagerRetriever func(context.Context) AlertmanagerRetriever\n228: \trulesRetriever        func(context.Context) RulesRetriever\n229: \tnow                   func() time.Time\n230: \tconfig                func() config.Config\n231: \tflagsMap              map[string]string\n232: \tready                 func(http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc\n233: \tglobalURLOptions      GlobalURLOptions\n234: \n235: \tdb                  TSDBAdminStats\n236: \tdbDir               string\n237: \tenableAdmin         bool\n238: \tlogger              *slog.Logger\n239: \tCORSOrigin          *regexp.Regexp\n240: \tbuildInfo           *PrometheusVersion\n241: \truntimeInfo         func() (RuntimeInfo, error)\n242: \tgatherer            prometheus.Gatherer\n243: \tisAgent             bool\n244: \tstatsRenderer       StatsRenderer\n245: \tnotificationsGetter func() []notifications.Notification\n246: \tnotificationsSub    func() (<-chan notifications.Notification, func(), bool)\n247: \t// Allows customizing the default mapping\n248: \toverrideErrorCode OverrideErrorCode\n249: \n250: \tremoteWriteHandler http.Handler\n251: \tremoteReadHandler  http.Handler\n252: \totlpWriteHandler   http.Handler\n253: \n254: \tcodecs []Codec\n255: }\n256: \n257: // NewAPI returns an initialized API type.\n258: func NewAPI(\n259: \tqe promql.QueryEngine,\n260: \tq storage.SampleAndChunkQueryable,\n261: \tap storage.Appendable,\n262: \teq storage.ExemplarQueryable,\n263: \tspsr func(context.Context) ScrapePoolsRetriever,\n264: \ttr func(context.Context) TargetRetriever,\n265: \tar func(context.Context) AlertmanagerRetriever,\n266: \tconfigFunc func() config.Config,\n267: \tflagsMap map[string]string,\n268: \tglobalURLOptions GlobalURLOptions,\n269: \treadyFunc func(http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc,\n270: \tdb TSDBAdminStats,\n271: \tdbDir string,\n272: \tenableAdmin bool,\n273: \tlogger *slog.Logger,\n274: \trr func(context.Context) RulesRetriever,\n275: \tremoteReadSampleLimit int,\n276: \tremoteReadConcurrencyLimit int,\n277: \tremoteReadMaxBytesInFrame int,\n278: \tisAgent bool,\n279: \tcorsOrigin *regexp.Regexp,\n280: \truntimeInfo func() (RuntimeInfo, error),\n281: \tbuildInfo *PrometheusVersion,\n282: \tnotificationsGetter func() []notifications.Notification,\n283: \tnotificationsSub func() (<-chan notifications.Notification, func(), bool),\n284: \tgatherer prometheus.Gatherer,\n285: \tregisterer prometheus.Registerer,\n286: \tstatsRenderer StatsRenderer,\n287: \trwEnabled bool,\n288: \tacceptRemoteWriteProtoMsgs []config.RemoteWriteProtoMsg,\n289: \totlpEnabled, otlpDeltaToCumulative, otlpNativeDeltaIngestion bool,\n290: \tctZeroIngestionEnabled bool,\n291: \tlookbackDelta time.Duration,\n292: \tenableTypeAndUnitLabels bool,\n293: \toverrideErrorCode OverrideErrorCode,\n294: ) *API {\n295: \ta := &API{\n296: \t\tQueryEngine:       qe,\n297: \t\tQueryable:         q,\n298: \t\tExemplarQueryable: eq,\n299: \n300: // QueryFunc processes PromQL queries.\\n44: type QueryFunc func(ctx context.Context, q string, t time.Time) (promql.Vector, error)\\n45: \\n46: // EngineQueryFunc returns a new query function that executes instant queries against\\n47: // the given engine.\\n48: // It converts scalar into vector results.\\n49: func EngineQueryFunc(engine promql.QueryEngine, q storage.Queryable) QueryFunc {\\n50: \\treturn func(ctx context.Context, qs string, t time.Time) (promql.Vector, error) {\\n51: \\t\\tq, err := engine.NewInstantQuery(ctx, q, nil, qs, t)\\n52: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n53: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n54: \\t\\t}\\n55: \\t\\tres := q.Exec(ctx)\\n56: \\t\\tif res.Err != nil {\\n57: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, res.Err\\n58: \\t\\t}\\n59: \\t\\tswitch v := res.Value.(type) {\\n60: \\t\\tcase promql.Vector:\\n61: \\t\\t\\treturn v, nil\\n62: \\t\\tcase promql.Scalar:\\n63: \\t\\t\\treturn promql.Vector{promql.Sample{\\n64: \\t\\t\\t\\tT:      v.T,\\n65: \\t\\t\\t\\tF:      v.V,\\n66: \\t\\t\\t\\tMetric: labels.Labels{},\\n67: \\t\\t\\t}}, nil\\n68: \\t\\tdefault:\\n69: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, errors.New(\\\"rule result is not a vector or scalar\\\")\\n70: \\t\\t}\\n71: \\t}\\n72: }\\n73: \\n74: // DefaultEvalIterationFunc is the default implementation of\\n75: // GroupEvalIterationFunc that is periodically invoked to evaluate the rules\\n76: // in a group at a given point in time and updates Group state and metrics\\n77: // accordingly. Custom GroupEvalIterationFunc implementations are recommended\\n78: // to invoke this function as well, to ensure correct Group state and metrics\\n79: // are maintained.\\n80: func DefaultEvalIterationFunc(ctx context.Context, g *Group, evalTimestamp time.Time) {\\n81: \\tg.metrics.IterationsScheduled.WithLabelValues(GroupKey(g.file, g.name)).Inc()\\n82: \\n83: \\tstart := time.Now()\\n84: \\tg.Eval(ctx, evalTimestamp)\\n85: \\ttimeSinceStart := time.Since(start)\\n86: \\n87: \\tg.metrics.IterationDuration.Observe(timeSinceStart.Seconds())\\n88: \\tg.updateRuleEvaluationTimeSum()\\n89: \\tg.setEvaluationTime(timeSinceStart)\\n90: \\tg.setLastEvaluation(start)\\n91: \\tg.setLastEvalTimestamp(evalTimestamp)\\n92: }\\n93: \\n94: // The Manager manages recording and alerting rules.\\n95: type Manager struct {\\n96: \\topts                 *ManagerOptions\\n97: \\tgroups               map[string]*Group\\n98: \\tmtx                  sync.RWMutex\\n99: \\tblock                chan struct{}\\n100: \\tdone                 chan struct{}\\n101: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4@ba16cf5a7437eb3b6b71b74557b7a67e1975ad0f/-/blob/rules/manager.go%3FL1-100\"}",

\tmaps0 \"maps\"\n22: \t\"net/url\"\n23: \t\"path/filepath\"\n24: \t\"slices\"\n25: \t\"strings\"\n26: \t\"sync\"\n27: \t\"time\"\n28: \n29: \t\"github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus\"\n30: \t\"github.com/prometheus/common/model\"\n31: \t\"github.com/prometheus/common/promslog\"\n32: \t\"golang.org/x/sync/semaphore\"\n33: \n34: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/model/labels\"\n35: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/model/rulefmt\"\n36: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/notifier\"\n37: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/promql\"\n38: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/promql/parser\"\n39: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/storage\"\n40: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/util/strutil\"\n41: )\n42: \n43: // QueryFunc processes PromQL queries.\n44: type QueryFunc func(ctx context.Context, q string, t time.Time) (promql.Vector, error)\n45: \n46: // EngineQueryFunc returns a new query function that executes instant queries against\n47: // the given engine.\n48: // It converts scalar into vector results.\n49: func EngineQueryFunc(engine promql.QueryEngine, q storage.Queryable) QueryFunc {\n50: \treturn func(ctx context.Context, qs string, t time.Time) (promql.Vector, error) {\n51: \t\tq, err := engine.NewInstantQuery(ctx, q, nil, qs, t)\n52: \t\tif err != nil {\n53: \t\t\treturn nil, err\n54: \t\t}\n55: \t\tres := q.Exec(ctx)\n56: \t\tif res.Err != nil {\n57: \t\t\treturn nil, res.Err\n58: \t\t}\n59: \t\tswitch v := res.Value.(type) {\n60: \t\tcase promql.Vector:\n61: \t\t\treturn v, nil\n62: \t\tcase promql.Scalar:\n63: \t\t\treturn promql.Vector{promql.Sample{\n64: \t\t\t\tT:      v.T,\n65: \t\t\t\tF:      v.V,\n66: \t\t\t\tMetric: labels.Labels{},\n67: \t\t\t}}, nil\n68: \t\tdefault:\n69: \t\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"rule result is not a vector or scalar\")\n70: \t\t}\n71: \t}\n72: }\n73: \n74: // DefaultEvalIterationFunc is the default implementation of\n75: // GroupEvalIterationFunc that is periodically invoked to evaluate the rules\n76: // in a group at a given point in time and updates Group state and metrics\n77: // accordingly. Custom GroupEvalIterationFunc implementations are recommended\n78: // to invoke this function as well, to ensure correct Group state and metrics\n79: // are maintained.\n80: func DefaultEvalIterationFunc(ctx context.Context, g *Group, evalTimestamp time.Time) {\n81: \tg.metrics.IterationsScheduled.WithLabelValues(GroupKey(g.file, g.name)).Inc()\n82: \n83: \tstart := time.Now()\n84: \tg.Eval(ctx, evalTimestamp)\n85: \ttimeSinceStart := time.Since(start)\n86: \n87: \tg.metrics.IterationDuration.Observe(timeSinceStart.Seconds())\n88: \tg.updateRuleEvaluationTimeSum()\n89: \tg.setEvaluationTime(timeSinceStart)\n90: \tg.setLastEvaluation(start)\n91: \tg.setLastEvalTimestamp(evalTimestamp)\n92: }\n93: \n94: // The Manager manages recording and alerting rules.\n95: type Manager struct {\n96: \topts                 *ManagerOptions\n97: \tgroups               map[string]*Group\n98: \tmtx                  sync.RWMutex\n99: \tblock                chan struct{}\n100: \tdone                 chan struct{}\n101: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4@ba16cf5a7437eb3b6b71b74557b7a67e1975ad0f/-/blob/rules/manager.go%3FL1-100"
  }

  "command": "cat > /tmp/make_changes.py << 'PYEOF'\n#!/usr/bin/env python3\nimport sys\n\n# Changes to make:\n# 1. promql/engine.go line 117: Change \"QueryEngine\" to \"PromQLEvaluator\"\n# 2. promql/promqltest/test.go: Update function parameters\n# 3. web/api/v1/api.go: Update field and parameter\n# 4. rules/manager.go: Update function parameter\n\nchanges = [\n    {\n        'file': 'promql/engine.go',\n        'old': '// QueryEngine defines the interface for the *promql.Engine, so it can be replaced, wrapped or mocked.\\ntype QueryEngine interface {',\n        'new': '// PromQLEvaluator defines the interface for the *promql.Engine, so it can be replaced, wrapped or mocked.\\ntype PromQLEvaluator interface {'\n    },\n    {\n        'file': 'promql/promqltest/test.go',\n        'old': 'func RunBuiltinTests(t TBRun, engine promql.QueryEngine) {',\n        'new': 'func RunBuiltinTests(t TBRun, engine promql.PromQLEvaluator) {'\n    },\n 'func RunBuiltinTestsWithStorage(t TBRun, engine promql.PromQLEvaluator, newStorage func(testutil.T) storage.Storage) {'\n    },\n    {\n        'file': 'promql/promqltest/test.go',\n        'old': 'func RunTest(t testutil.T, input string, engine promql.QueryEngine) {',\n        'new': 'func RunTest(t testutil.T, input string, engine promql.PromQLEvaluator) {'\n    },\n    {\n        'file': 'promql/promqltest/test.go',\n        'old': 'func RunTestWithStorage(t testutil.T, input string, engine promql.QueryEngine, newStorage func(testutil.T) storage.Storage) {',\n        'new': 'func RunTestWithStorage(t testutil.T, input string, engine promql.PromQLEvaluator, newStorage func(testutil.T) storage.Storage) {'\n    },\n    {\n        'file': 'web/api/v1/api.go',\n        'old': '\\tQueryEngine       promql.QueryEngine',\n        'new': '\\tQueryEngine       promql.PromQLEvaluator'\n    },\n    {\n        'file': 'web/api/v1/api.go',\n        'old': 'func NewAPI(\\n\\tqe promql.QueryEngine,',\n        'new': 'func NewAPI(\\n\\tqe promql.PromQLEvaluator,'\n    },\n    {\n        'file': 'rules/manager.go',\n        'old': 'func EngineQueryFunc(engine promql.QueryEngine, q storage.Queryable) QueryFunc {',\n        'new': 'func EngineQueryFunc(engine promql.PromQLEvaluator, q storage.Queryable) QueryFunc {'\n    },\n]\n\nprint(f\"Total changes to make: {len(changes)}\")\nfor i, change in enumerate(changes, 1):\n    print(f\"{i}. {change['file']}\")\n\nPYEOF\npython3 /tmp/make_changes.py\n",

}

\\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/util/zeropool\\\"\\n54: )\\n55: \\n56: const (\\n57: \\tnamespace            = \\\"prometheus\\\"\\n58: \\tsubsystem            = \\\"engine\\\"\\n59: \\tqueryTag             = \\\"query\\\"\\n60: \\tenv                  = \\\"query execution\\\"\\n61: \\tdefaultLookbackDelta = 5 * time.Minute\\n62: \\n63: \\t// The largest SampleValue that can be converted to an int64 without overflow.\\n64: \\tmaxInt64 = 9223372036854774784\\n65: \\t// The smallest SampleValue that can be converted to an int64 without underflow.\\n66: \\tminInt64 = -9223372036854775808\\n67: \\n68: \\t// Max initial size for the pooled points slices.\\n69: \\t// The getHPointSlice and getFPointSlice functions are called with an estimated size which often can be\\n70: \\t// over-estimated.\\n71: \\tmaxPointsSliceSize = 5000\\n72: \\n73: \\t// The default buffer size for points used by the matrix selector.\\n74: \\tmatrixSelectorSliceSize = 16\\n75: )\\n76: \\n77: type engineMetrics struct {\\n78: \\tcurrentQueries       prometheus.Gauge\\n79: \\tmaxConcurrentQueries prometheus.Gauge\\n80: \\tqueryLogEnabled      prometheus.Gauge\\n81: \\tqueryLogFailures     prometheus.Counter\\n82: \\tqueryQueueTime       prometheus.Observer\\n83: \\tqueryPrepareTime     prometheus.Observer\\n84: \\tqueryInnerEval       prometheus.Observer\\n85: \\tqueryResultSort      prometheus.Observer\\n86: \\tquerySamples         prometheus.Counter\\n87: }\\n88: \\n89: type (\\n90: \\t// ErrQueryTimeout is returned if a query timed out during processing.\\n91: \\tErrQueryTimeout string\\n92: \\t// ErrQueryCanceled is returned if a query was canceled during processing.\\n93: \\tErrQueryCanceled string\\n94: \\t// ErrTooManySamples is returned if a query would load more than the maximum allowed samples into memory.\\n95: \\tErrTooManySamples string\\n96: \\t// ErrStorage is returned if an error was encountered in the storage layer\\n97: \\t// during query handling.\\n98: \\tErrStorage struct{ Err error }\\n99: )\\n100: \\n101: func (e ErrQueryTimeout) Error() string {\\n102: \\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\\\"query timed out in %s\\\", string(e))\\n103: }\\n104: \\n105: func (e ErrQueryCanceled) Error() string {\\n106: \\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\\\"query was canceled in %s\\\", string(e))\\n107: }\\n108: \\n109: func (e ErrTooManySamples) Error() string {\\n110: \\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\\\"query processing would load too many samples into memory in %s\\\", string(e))\\n111: }\\n112: \\n113: func (e ErrStorage) Error() string {\\n114: \\treturn e.Err.Error()\\n115: }\\n116: \\n117: // QueryEngine defines the interface for the *promql.Engine, so it can be replaced, wrapped or mocked.\\n118: type QueryEngine interface {\\n119: \\tNewInstantQuery(ctx context.Context, q storage.Queryable, opts QueryOpts, qs string, ts time.Time) (Query, error)\\n120: \\tNewRangeQuery(ctx context.Context, q storage.Queryable, opts QueryOpts, qs string, start, end time.Time, interval time.Duration) (Query, error)\\n121: }\\n122: \\n123: var _ QueryLogger = (*logging.JSONFileLogger)(nil)\\n124: \\n125: // QueryLogger is an interface that can be used to log all the queries logged\\n126: // by the engine.\\n127: type QueryLogger interface {\\n128: \\tslog.Handler\\n129: \\tio.Closer\\n130: }\\n131: \\n132: // A Query is derived from a raw query string and can be run against an engine\\n133: // it is associated with.\\n134: type Query interface {\\n135: \\t// Exec processes the query. Can only be called once.\\n136: \\tExec(ctx context.Context) *Result\\n137: \\t// Close recovers memory used by the query result.\\n138: \\tClose()\\n139: \\t// Statement returns the parsed statement of the query.\\n140: \\tStatement() parser.Statement\\n141: \\t// Stats returns statistics about the lifetime of the query.\\n142: \\tStats() *stats.Statistics\\n143: \\t// Cancel signals that a running query execution should be aborted.\\n144: \\tCancel()\\n145: \\t// String returns the original query string.\\n146: \\tString() string\\n147: }\\n148: \\n149: type PrometheusQueryOpts struct {\\n150: \\t// Enables recording per-step statistics if the engine has it enabled as well. Disabled by default.\\n151: \\tenablePerStepStats bool\\n152: \\t// Lookback delta duration for this query.\\n153: \\tlookbackDelta time.Duration\\n154: }\\n155: \\n156: var _ QueryOpts = &PrometheusQueryOpts{}\\n157: \\n158: func NewPrometheusQueryOpts(enablePerStepStats bool, lookbackDelta time.Duration) QueryOpts {\\n159: \\treturn &PrometheusQueryOpts{\\n160: \\t\\tenablePerStepStats: enablePerStepStats,\\n161: \\t\\tlookbackDelta:      lookbackDelta,\\n162: \\t}\\n163: }\\n164: \\n165: func (p *PrometheusQueryOpts) EnablePerStepStats() bool {\\n166: \\treturn p.enablePerStepStats\\n167: }\\n168: \\n169: func (p *PrometheusQueryOpts) LookbackDelta() time.Duration {\\n170: \\treturn p.lookbackDelta\\n171: }\\n172: \\n173: type QueryOpts interface {\\n174: \\t// Enables recording per-step statistics if the engine has it enabled as well. Disabled by default.\\n175: \\tEnablePerStepStats() bool\\n176: \\t// Lookback delta duration for this query.\\n177: \\tLookbackDelta() time.Duration\\n178: }\\n179: \\n180: // query implements the Query interface.\\n181: type query struct {\\n182: \\t// Underlying data provider.\\n183: \\tqueryable storage.Queryable\\n184: \\t// The original query string.\\n185: \\tq string\\n186: \\t// Statement of the parsed query.\\n187: \\tstmt parser.Statement\\n188: \\t// Timer stats for the query execution.\\n189: \\tstats *stats.QueryTimers\\n190: \\t// Sample stats for the query execution.\\n191: \\tsampleStats *stats.QuerySamples\\n192: \\t// Result matrix for reuse.\\n193: \\tmatrix Matrix\\n194: \\t// Cancellation function for the query.\\n195: \\tcancel func()\\n196: \\n197: \\t// The engine against which the query is executed.\\n198: \\tng *Engine\\n199: }\\n200: \\n201: type QueryOrigin struct{}\\n202: \\n203: // Statement implements the Query interface.\\n204: // Calling this after Exec may result in panic,\\n205: // see https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/issues/8949.\\n206: func (q *query) Statement() parser.Statement {\\n207: \\treturn q.stmt\\n208: }\\n209: \\n210: // String implements the Query interface.\\n211: func (q *query) String() string {\\n212: \\treturn q.q\\n213: }\\n214: \\n215: // Stats implements the Query interface.\\n216: func (q *query) Stats() *stats.Statistics {\\n217: \\treturn &stats.Statistics{\\n218: \\t\\tTimers:  q.stats,\\n219: \\t\\tSamples: q.sampleStats,\\n220: \\t}\\n221: }\\n222: \\n223: // Cancel implements the Query interface.\\n224: func (q *query) Cancel() {\\n225: \\tif q.cancel != nil {\\n226: \\t\\tq.cancel()\\n227: \\t}\\n228: }\\n229: \\n230: // Close implements the Query interface.\\n231: func (q *query) Close() {\\n232: \\tfor _, s := range q.matrix {\\n233: \\t\\tputFPointSlice(s.Floats)\\n234: \\t\\tputHPointSlice(s.Histograms)\\n235: \\t}\\n236: }\\n237: \\n238: // Exec implements the Query interface.\\n239: func (q *query) Exec(ctx context.Context) *Result {\\n240: \\tif span := trace.SpanFromContext(ctx); span != nil {\\n241: \\t\\tspan.SetAttributes(attribute.String(queryTag, q.stmt.String()))\\n242: \\t}\\n243: \\n244: \\t// Exec query.\\n245: \\tres, warnings, err := q.ng.exec(ctx, q)\\n246: \\treturn &Result{Err: err, Value: res, Warnings: warnings}\\n247: }\\n248: \\n249: // contextDone returns an error if the context was canceled or timed out.\\n250: func contextDone(ctx context.Context, env string) error {\\n251: \\tif err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {\\n252: \\t\\treturn contextErr(err, env)\\n253: \\t}\\n254: \\treturn nil\\n255: }\\n256: \\n257: func contextErr(err error, env string) error {\\n258: \\tswitch {\\n259: \\tcase errors.Is(err, context.Canceled):\\n260: \\t\\treturn ErrQueryCanceled(env)\\n261: \\tcase errors.Is(err, context.DeadlineExceeded):\\n262: \\t\\treturn ErrQueryTimeout(env)\\n263: \\tdefault:\\n264: \\t\\treturn err\\n265: \\t}\\n266: }\\n267: \\n268: // QueryTracker provides access to two features:\\n269: //\\n270: // 1) Tracking of active query. If PromQL engine crashes while executing any query, such query should be present\\n271: // in the tracker on restart, hence logged. After the logging on restart, the tracker gets emptied.\\n272: //\\n273: // 2) Enforcement of the maximum number of concurrent queries.\\n274: type QueryTracker interface {\\n275: \\tio.Closer\\n276: \\n277: \\t// GetMaxConcurrent returns maximum number of concurrent queries that are allowed by this tracker.\\n278: \\tGetMaxConcurrent() int\\n279: \\n280: \\t// Insert inserts query into query tracker. This call must block if maximum number of queries is already running.\\n281: \\t// If Insert doesn't return error then returned integer value should be used in subsequent Delete call.\\n282: \\t// Insert should return error if context is finished before query can proceed, and integer value returned in this case should be ignored by caller.\\n283: \\tInsert(ctx context.Context, query string) (int, error)\\n284: \\n285: \\t// Delete removes query from activity tracker. InsertIndex is value returned by Insert call.\\n286: \\tDelete(insertIndex int)\\n287: }\\n288: \\n289: // EngineOpts contains configuration options used when creating a new Engine.\\n290: type EngineOpts struct {\\n291: \\tLogger             *slog.Logger\\n292: \\tReg                prometheus.Registerer\\n293: \\tMaxSamples         int\\n294: \\tTimeout            time.Duration\\n295: \\tActiveQueryTracker QueryTracker\\n296: \\t// LookbackDelta determines the time since the last sample after which a time\\n297: \\t// series is considered stale.\\n298: \\tLookbackDelta time.Duration\\n299: \\n300: \\t// NoStepSubqueryIntervalFn is the default evaluation interval of\\n301: \\t// a subquery in milliseconds if no step in range vector was specified `[30m:<step>]`.\\n302: \\tNoStepSubqueryIntervalFn func(rangeMillis int64) int64\\n303: \\n304: \\t// EnableAtModifier if true enables @ modifier. Disabled otherwise. This\\n305: \\t// is supposed to be enabled for regular PromQL (as of Prometheus v2.33)\\n306: \\t// but the option to disable it is still provided here for those using\\n307: \\t// the Engine outside of Prometheus.\\n308: \\tEnableAtModifier bool\\n309: \\n310: \\t// EnableNegativeOffset if true enables negative (-) offset\\n311: \\t// values. Disabled otherwise. This is supposed to be enabled for\\n312: \\t// regular PromQL (as of Prometheus v2.33) but the option to disable it\\n313: \\t// is still provided here for those using the Engine outside of\\n314: \\t// Prometheus.\\n315: \\tEnableNegativeOffset bool\\n316: \\n317: \\t// EnablePerStepStats if true allows for per-step stats to be computed on request. Disabled otherwise.\\n318: \\tEnablePerStepStats bool\\n319: \\n320: \\t// EnableDelayedNameRemoval delays the removal of the __name__ label to the last step of the query evaluation.\\n321: \\t// This is useful in certain scenarios where the __name__ label must be preserved or where applying a\\n322: \\t// regex-matcher to the __name__ label may otherwise lead to duplicate labelset errors.\\n323: \\tEnableDelayedNameRemoval bool\\n324: \\t// EnableTypeAndUnitLabels will allow PromQL Engine to make decisions based on the type and unit labels.\\n325: \\tEnableTypeAndUnitLabels bool\\n326: }\\n327: \\n328: // Engine handles the lifetime of queries from beginning to end.\\n329: // It is connected to a querier.\\n330: type Engine struct {\\n331: \\tlogger                   *slog.Logger\\n332: \\tmetrics                  *engineMetrics\\n333: \\ttimeout                  time.Duration\\n334: \\tmaxSamplesPerQuery       int\\n335: \\tactiveQueryTracker       QueryTracker\\n336: \\tqueryLogger              QueryLogger\\n337: \\tqueryLoggerLock          sync.RWMutex\\n338: \\tlookbackDelta            time.Duration\\n339: \\tnoStepSubqueryIntervalFn func(rangeMillis int64) int64\\n340: \\tenableAtModifier         bool\\n341: \\tenableNegativeOffset     bool\\n342: \\tenablePerStepStats       bool\\n343: \\tenableDelayedNameRemoval bool\\n344: \\tenableTypeAndUnitLabels  bool\\n345: }\\n346: \\n347: // NewEngine returns a new engine.\\n348: func NewEngine(opts EngineOpts) *Engine {\\n349: \\tif opts.Logger == nil {\\n350: \\t\\topts.Logger = promslog.NewNopLogger()\\n351: \\t}\\n352: \\n353: \\tqueryResultSummary := prometheus.NewSummaryVec(prometheus.SummaryOpts{\\n354: \\t\\tNamespace:  namespace,\\n355: \\t\\tSubsystem:  subsystem,\\n356: \\t\\tName:       \\\"query_duration_seconds\\\",\\n357: \\t\\tHelp:       \\\"Query timings\\\",\\n358: \\t\\tObjectives: map[float64]float64{0.5: 0.05, 0.9: 0.01, 0.99: 0.001},\\n359: \\t},\\n360: \\t\\t[]string{\\\"slice\\\"},\\n361: \\t)\\n362: \\n363: \\tmetrics := &engineMetrics{\\n364: \\t\\tcurrentQueries: prometheus.NewGauge(prometheus.GaugeOpts{\\n365: \\t\\t\\tNamespace: namespace,\\n366: \\t\\t\\tSubsystem: subsystem,\\n367: \\t\\t\\tName:      \\\"queries\\\",\\n368: \\t\\t\\tHelp:      \\\"The current number of queries being executed or waiting.\\\",\\n369: \\t\\t}),\\n370: \\t\\tqueryLogEnabled: prometheus.NewGauge(prometheus.GaugeOpts{\\n371: \\t\\t\\tNamespace: namespace,\\n372: \\t\\t\\tSubsystem: subsystem,\\n373: \\t\\t\\tName:      \\\"query_log_enabled\\\",\\n374: \\t\\t\\tHelp:      \\\"State of the query log.\\\",\\n375: \\t\\t}),\\n376: \\t\\tqueryLogFailures: prometheus.NewCounter(prometheus.CounterOpts{\\n377: \\t\\t\\tNamespace: namespace,\\n378: \\t\\t\\tSubsystem: subsystem,\\n379: \\t\\t\\tName:      \\\"query_log_failures_total\\\",\\n380: \\t\\t\\tHelp:      \\\"The number of query log failures.\\\",\\n381: \\t\\t}),\\n382: \\t\\tmaxConcurrentQueries: prometheus.NewGauge(prometheus.GaugeOpts{\\n383: \\t\\t\\tNamespace: namespace,\\n384: \\t\\t\\tSubsystem: subsystem,\\n385: \\t\\t\\tName:      \\\"queries_concurrent_max\\\",\\n386: \\t\\t\\tHelp:      \\\"The max number of concurrent queries.\\\",\\n387: \\t\\t}),\\n388: \\t\\tquerySamples: prometheus.NewCounter(prometheus.CounterOpts{\\n389: \\t\\t\\tNamespace: namespace,\\n390: \\t\\t\\tSubsystem: subsystem,\\n391: \\t\\t\\tName:      \\\"query_samples_total\\\",\\n392: \\t\\t\\tHelp:      \\\"The total number of samples loaded by all queries.\\\",\\n393: \\t\\t}),\\n394: \\t\\tqueryQueueTime:   queryResultSummary.WithLabelValues(\\\"queue_time\\\"),\\n395: \\t\\tqueryPrepareTime: queryResultSummary.WithLabelValues(\\\"prepare_time\\\"),\\n396: \\t\\tqueryInnerEval:   queryResultSummary.WithLabelValues(\\\"inner_eval\\\"),\\n397: \\t\\tqueryResultSort:  queryResultSummary.WithLabelValues(\\\"result_sort\\\"),\\n398: \\t}\\n399: \\n400: \\tif t := opts.ActiveQueryTracker; t != nil {\\n401: \\t\\tmetrics.maxConcurrentQueries.Set(float64(t.GetMaxConcurrent()))\\n402: \\t} else {\\n403: \\t\\tmetrics.maxConcurrentQueries.Set(-1)\\n404: \\t}\\n405: \\n406: \\tif opts.LookbackDelta == 0 {\\n407: \\t\\topts.LookbackDelta = defaultLookbackDelta\\n408: \\t\\tif l := opts.Logger; l != nil {\\n409: \\t\\t\\tl.Debug(\\\"Lookback delta is zero, setting to default value\\\", \\\"value\\\", defaultLookbackDelta)\\n410: \\t\\t}\\n411: \\t}\\n412: \\n413: \\tif opts.Reg != nil {\\n414: \\t\\topts.Reg.MustRegister(\\n415: \\t\\t\\tmetrics.currentQueries,\\n416: \\t\\t\\tmetrics.maxConcurrentQueries,\\n417: \\t\\t\\tmetrics.queryLogEnabled,\\n418: \\t\\t\\tmetrics.queryLogFailures,\\n419: \\t\\t\\tmetrics.querySamples,\\n420: \\t\\t\\tqueryResultSummary,\\n421: \\t\\t)\\n422: \\t}\\n423: \\n424: \\treturn &Engine{\\n425: \\t\\ttimeout:                  opts.Timeout,\\n426: \\t\\tlogger:                   opts.Logger,\\n427: \\t\\tmetrics:                  metrics,\\n428: \\t\\tmaxSamplesPerQuery:       opts.MaxSamples,\\n429: \\t\\tactiveQueryTracker:       opts.ActiveQueryTracker,\\n430: \\t\\tlookbackDelta:            opts.LookbackDelta,\\n431: \\t\\tnoStepSubqueryIntervalFn: opts.NoStepSubqueryIntervalFn,\\n432: \\t\\tenableAtModifier:         opts.EnableAtModifier,\\n433: \\t\\tenableNegativeOffset:     opts.EnableNegativeOffset,\\n434: \\t\\tenablePerStepStats:       opts.EnablePerStepStats,\\n435: \\t\\tenableDelayedNameRemoval: opts.EnableDelayedNameRemoval,\\n436: \\t\\tenableTypeAndUnitLabels:  opts.EnableTypeAndUnitLabels,\\n437: \\t}\\n438: }\\n439: \\n440: // Close closes ng.\\n441: // Callers must ensure the engine is really no longer in use before calling this to avoid\\n442: // issues failures like in https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/issues/15232\\n443: func (ng *Engine) Close() error {\\n444: \\tif ng == nil {\\n445: \\t\\treturn nil\\n446: \\t}\\n447: \\n448: \\tif ng.activeQueryTracker != nil {\\n449: \\t\\treturn ng.activeQueryTracker.Close()\\n450: \\t}\\n451: \\treturn nil\\n452: }\\n453: \\n454: // SetQueryLogger sets the query logger.\\n455: func (ng *Engine) SetQueryLogger(l QueryLogger) {\\n456: \\tng.queryLoggerLock.Lock()\\n457: \\tdefer ng.queryLoggerLock.Unlock()\\n458: \\n459: \\tif ng.queryLogger != nil {\\n460: \\t\\t// An error closing the old file descriptor should\\n461: \\t\\t// not make reload fail; only log a warning.\\n462: \\t\\terr := ng.queryLogger.Close()\\n463: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n464: \\t\\t\\tng.logger.Warn(\\\"Error while closing the previous query log file\\\", \\\"err\\\", err)\\n465: \\t\\t}\\n466: \\t}\\n467: \\n468: \\tng.queryLogger = l\\n469: \\n470: \\tif l != nil {\\n471: \\t\\tng.metrics.queryLogEnabled.Set(1)\\n472: \\t} else {\\n473: \\t\\tng.metrics.queryLogEnabled.Set(0)\\n474: \\t}\\n475: }\\n476: \\n477: // NewInstantQuery returns an evaluation query for the given expression at the given time.\\n478: func (ng *Engine) NewInstantQuery(ctx context.Context, q storage.Queryable, opts QueryOpts, qs string, ts time.Time) (Query, error) {\\n479: \\tpExpr, qry := ng.newQuery(q, qs, opts, ts, ts, 0*time.Second)\\n480: \\tfinishQueue, err := ng.queueActive(ctx, qry)\\n481: \\tif err != nil {\\n482: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n483: \\t}\\n484: \\tdefer finishQueue()\\n485: \\texpr, err := parser.ParseExpr(qs)\\n486: \\tif err != nil {\\n487: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n488: \\t}\\n489: \\tif err := ng.validateOpts(expr); err != nil {\\n490: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n491: \\t}\\n492: \\t*pExpr, err = PreprocessExpr(expr, ts, ts, 0)\\n493: \\n494: \\treturn qry, err\\n495: }\\n496: \\n497: // NewRangeQuery returns an evaluation query for the given time range and with\\n498: // the resolution set by the interval.\\n499: func (ng *Engine) NewRangeQuery(ctx context.Context, q storage.Queryable, opts QueryOpts, qs string, start, end time.Time, interval time.Duration) (Query, error) {\\n500: \\tpExpr, qry := ng.newQuery(q, qs, opts, start, end, interval)\\n501: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4@ba16cf5a7437eb3b6b71b74557b7a67e1975ad0f/-/blob/promql/engine.go%3FL1-500\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: // Copyright 2013 The Prometheus Authors\n2: // Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n3: // you may not use this file except in compliance with the language governing permissions and\n12: // limitations under the License.\n13: \n14: package promql\n15: \n16: import (\n17: \t\"bytes\"\n18: \t\"container/heap\"\n19: \t\"context\"\n20: \t\"errors\"\n21: \t\"fmt\"\n22: \t\"io\"\n23: \t\"log/slog\"\n24: \t\"math\"\n25: \t\"reflect\"\n26: \t\"runtime\"\n27: \t\"slices\"\n28: \t\"sort\"\n29: \t\"strconv\"\n30: \t\"strings\"\n31: \t\"sync\"\n32: \t\"time\"\n33: \n34: \t\"github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus\"\n35: \t\"github.com/prometheus/common/model\"\n36: \t\"github.com/prometheus/common/promslog\"\n37: \t\"go.opentelemetry.io/otel\"\n38: \t\"go.opentelemetry.io/otel/attribute\"\n39: \t\"go.opentelemetry.io/otel/trace\"\n40: \n41: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/model/histogram\"\n42: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/model/labels\"\n43: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/model/timestamp\"\n44: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/model/value\"\n45: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/promql/parser\"\n46: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/promql/parser/posrange\"\n47: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/schema\"\n48: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/storage\"\n49: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/tsdb/chunkenc\"\n50: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/util/annotations\"\n51: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/util/logging\"\n52: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/util/stats\"\n53: \t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/util/zeropool\"\n54: )\n55: \n56: const (\n57: \tnamespace            = \"prometheus\"\n58: \tsubsystem            = \"engine\"\n59: \tqueryTag             = \"query\"\n60: \tenv                  = \"query execution\"\n61: \tdefaultLookbackDelta = 5 * time.Minute\n62: \n63: \t// The largest SampleValue that can be converted to an int64 without overflow.\n64: \tmaxInt64 = 9223372036854774784\n65: \t// The smallest SampleValue that can be converted to an int64 without underflow.\n66: \tminInt64 = -9223372036854775808\n67: \n68: \t// Max initial size for the pooled points slices.\n69: \t// The getHPointSlice and getFPointSlice functions are called with an estimated size which often can be\n70: \t// over-estimated.\n71: \tmaxPointsSliceSize = 5000\n72: \n73: \t// The default buffer size for points used by the matrix selector.\n74: \tmatrixSelectorSliceSize = 16\n75: )\n76: \n77: type engineMetrics struct {\n78: \tcurrentQueries       prometheus.Gauge\n79: \tmaxConcurrentQueries prometheus.Gauge\n80: \tqueryLogEnabled      prometheus.Gauge\n81: \tqueryLogFailures     prometheus.Counter\n82: \tqueryQueueTime       prometheus.Observer\n83: \tqueryPrepareTime     prometheus.Observer\n84: \tqueryInnerEval       prometheus.Observer\n85: \tqueryResultSort      prometheus.Observer\n86: \tquerySamples         prometheus.Counter\n87: }\n88: \n89: type (\n90: \t// ErrQueryTimeout is returned if a query timed out during processing.\n91: \tErrQueryTimeout string\n92: \t// ErrQueryCanceled is returned if a query was canceled during processing.\n93: \tErrQueryCanceled string\n94: \t// ErrTooManySamples is returned if a query would load more than the maximum allowed samples into memory.\n95: \tErrTooManySamples string\n96: \t// ErrStorage is returned if an error was encountered in the storage layer\n97: \t// during query handling.\n98: \tErrStorage struct{ Err error }\n99: )\n100: \n101: func (e ErrQueryTimeout) Error() string {\n102: \treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"query timed out in %s\", string(e))\n103: }\n104: \n105: func (e ErrQueryCanceled) Error() string {\n106: \treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"query was canceled in %s\", string(e))\n107: }\n108: \n109: func (e ErrTooManySamples) Error() string {\n110: \treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"query processing would load too many samples into memory in %s\", string(e))\n111: }\n112: \n113: func (e ErrStorage) Error() string {\n114: \treturn e.Err.Error()\n115: }\n116: \n117: // QueryEngine defines the interface for the *promql.Engine, so it can be replaced, wrapped or mocked.\n118: type QueryEngine interface {\n119: \tNewInstantQuery(ctx context.Context, q storage.Queryable, opts QueryOpts, qs string, ts time.Time) (Query, error)\n120: \tNewRangeQuery(ctx context.Context, q storage.Queryable, opts QueryOpts, qs string, start, end time.Time, interval time.Duration) (Query, error)\n121: }\n122: \n123: var _ QueryLogger = (*logging.JSONFileLogger)(nil)\n124: \n125: // QueryLogger is an interface that can be used to log all the queries logged\n126: // by the engine.\n127: type QueryLogger interface {\n128: \tslog.Handler\n129: \tio.Closer\n130: }\n131: \n132: // A Query is derived from a raw query string and can be run against an engine\n133: // it is associated with.\n134: type Query interface {\n135: \t// Exec processes the query. Can only be called once.\n136: \tExec(ctx context.Context) *Result\n137: \t// Close recovers memory used by the query result.\n138: \tClose()\n139: \t// Statement returns the parsed statement of the query.\n140: \tStatement() parser.Statement\n141: \t// Stats returns statistics about the lifetime of the query.\n142: \tStats() *stats.Statistics\n143: \t// Cancel signals that a running query execution should be aborted.\n144: \tCancel()\n145: \t// String returns the original query string.\n146: \tString() string\n147: }\n148: \n149: type PrometheusQueryOpts struct {\n150: \t// Enables recording per-step statistics if the engine has it enabled as well. Disabled by default.\n151: \tenablePerStepStats bool\n152: \t// Lookback delta duration for this query.\n153: \tlookbackDelta time.Duration\n154: }\n155: \n156: var _ QueryOpts = &PrometheusQueryOpts{}\n157: \n158: func NewPrometheusQueryOpts(enablePerStepStats bool, lookbackDelta time.Duration) QueryOpts {\n159: \treturn &PrometheusQueryOpts{\n160: \t\tenablePerStepStats: enablePerStepStats,\n161: \t\tlookbackDelta:      lookbackDelta,\n162: \t}\n163: }\n164: \n165: func (p *PrometheusQueryOpts) EnablePerStepStats() bool {\n166: \treturn p.enablePerStepStats\n167: }\n168: \n169: func (p *PrometheusQueryOpts) LookbackDelta() time.Duration {\n170: \treturn p.lookbackDelta\n171: }\n172: \n173: type QueryOpts interface {\n174: \t// Enables recording per-step statistics if the engine has it enabled as well. Disabled by default.\n175: \tEnablePerStepStats() bool\n176: \t// Lookback delta duration for this query.\n177: \tLookbackDelta() time.Duration\n178: }\n179: \n180: // query implements the Query interface.\n181: type query struct {\n182: \t// Underlying data provider.\n183: \tqueryable storage.Queryable\n184: \t// The original query string.\n185: \tq string\n186: \t// Statement of the parsed query.\n187: \tstmt parser.Statement\n188: \t// Timer stats for the query execution.\n189: \tstats *stats.QueryTimers\n190: \t// Sample stats for the query execution.\n191: \tsampleStats *stats.QuerySamples\n192: \t// Result matrix for reuse.\n193: \tmatrix Matrix\n194: \t// Cancellation function for the query.\n195: \tcancel func()\n196: \n197: \t// The engine against which the query is executed.\n198: \tng *Engine\n199: }\n200: \n201: type QueryOrigin struct{}\n202: \n203: // Statement implements the Query interface.\n204: // Calling this after Exec may result in panic,\n205: // see https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/issues/8949.\n206: func (q *query) Statement() parser.Statement {\n207: \treturn q.stmt\n208: }\n209: \n210: // String implements the Query interface.\n211: func (q *query) String() string {\n212: \treturn q.q\n213: }\n214: \n215: // Stats implements the Query interface.\n216: func (q *query) Stats() *stats.Statistics {\n217: \treturn &stats.Statistics{\n218: \t\tTimers:  q.stats,\n219: \t\tSamples: q.sampleStats,\n220: \t}\n221: }\n222: \n223: // Cancel implements the Query interface.\n224: func (q *query) Cancel() {\n225: \tif q.cancel != nil {\n226: \t\tq.cancel()\n227: \t}\n228: }\n229: \n230: // Close implements the Query interface.\n231: func (q *query) Close() {\n232: \tfor _, s := range q.matrix {\n233: \t\tputFPointSlice(s.Floats)\n234: \t\tputHPointSlice(s.Histograms)\n235: \t}\n236: }\n237: \n238: // Exec implements the Query interface.\n239: func (q *query) Exec(ctx context.Context) *Result {\n240: \tif span := trace.SpanFromContext(ctx); span != nil {\n241: \t\tspan.SetAttributes(attribute.String(queryTag, q.stmt.String()))\n242: \t}\n243: \n244: \t// Exec query.\n245: \tres, warnings, err := q.ng.exec(ctx, q)\n246: \treturn &Result{Err: err, Value: res, Warnings: warnings}\n247: }\n248: \n249: // contextDone returns an error if the context was canceled or timed out.\n250: func contextDone(ctx context.Context, env string) error {\n251: \tif err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {\n252: \t\treturn contextErr(err, env)\n253: \t}\n254: \treturn nil\n255: }\n256: \n257: func contextErr(err error, env string) error {\n258: \tswitch {\n259: \tcase errors.Is(err, context.Canceled):\n260: \t\treturn ErrQueryCanceled(env)\n261: \tcase errors.Is(err, context.DeadlineExceeded):\n262: \t\treturn ErrQueryTimeout(env)\n263: \tdefault:\n264: \t\treturn err\n265: \t}\n266: }\n267: \n268: // QueryTracker provides access to two features:\n269: //\n270: // 1) Tracking of active query. If PromQL engine crashes while executing any query, such query should be present\n271: // in the tracker on restart, hence logged. After the logging on restart, the tracker gets emptied.\n272: //\n273: // 2) Enforcement of the maximum number of concurrent queries.\n274: type QueryTracker interface {\n275: \tio.Closer\n276: \n277: \t// GetMaxConcurrent returns maximum number of concurrent queries that are allowed by this tracker.\n278: \tGetMaxConcurrent() int\n279: \n280: \t// Insert inserts query into query tracker. This call must block if maximum number of queries is already running.\n281: \t// If Insert doesn't return error then returned integer value should be used in subsequent Delete call.\n282: \t// Insert should return error if context is finished before query can proceed, and integer value returned in this case should be ignored by caller.\n283: \tInsert(ctx context.Context, query string) (int, error)\n284: \n285: \t// Delete removes query from activity tracker. InsertIndex is value returned by Insert call.\n286: \tDelete(insertIndex int)\n287: }\n288: \n289: // EngineOpts contains configuration options used when creating a new Engine.\n290: type EngineOpts struct {\n291: \tLogger             *slog.Logger\n292: \tReg                prometheus.Registerer\n293: \tMaxSamples         int\n294: \tTimeout            time.Duration\n295: \tActiveQueryTracker QueryTracker\n296: \t// LookbackDelta determines the time since the last sample after which a time\n297: \t// series is considered stale.\n298: \tLookbackDelta time.Duration\n299: \n300: \t// NoStepSubqueryIntervalFn is the default evaluation interval of\n301: \t// a subquery in milliseconds if no step in range vector was specified `[30m:<step>]`.\n302: \tNoStepSubqueryIntervalFn func(rangeMillis int64) int64\n303: \n304: \t// EnableAtModifier if true enables @ modifier. Disabled otherwise. This\n305: \t// is supposed to be enabled for regular PromQL (as of Prometheus v2.33)\n306: \t// but the option to disable it is still provided here for those using\n307: \t// the Engine outside of Prometheus.\n308: \tEnableAtModifier bool\n309: \n310: \t// EnableNegativeOffset if true enables negative (-) offset\n311: \t// values. Disabled otherwise. This is supposed to be enabled for\n312: \t// regular PromQL (as of Prometheus v2.33) but the option to disable it\n313: \t// is still provided here for those using the Engine outside of\n314: \t// Prometheus.\n315: \tEnableNegativeOffset bool\n316: \n317: \t// EnablePerStepStats if true allows for per-step stats to be computed on request. Disabled otherwise.\n318: \tEnablePerStepStats bool\n319: \n320: \t// EnableDelayedNameRemoval delays the removal of the __name__ label to the last step of the query evaluation.\n321: \t// This is useful in certain scenarios where the __name__ label must be preserved or where applying a\n322: \t// regex-matcher to the __name__ label may otherwise lead to duplicate labelset errors.\n323: \tEnableDelayedNameRemoval bool\n324: \t// EnableTypeAndUnitLabels will allow PromQL Engine to make decisions based on the type and unit labels.\n325: \tEnableTypeAndUnitLabels bool\n326: }\n327: \n328: // Engine handles the lifetime of queries from beginning to end.\n329: // It is connected to a querier.\n330: type Engine struct {\n331: \tlogger                   *slog.Logger\n332: \tmetrics                  *engineMetrics\n333: \ttimeout                  time.Duration\n334: \tmaxSamplesPerQuery       int\n335: \tactiveQueryTracker       QueryTracker\n336: \tqueryLogger              QueryLogger\n337: \tqueryLoggerLock          sync.RWMutex\n338: \tlookbackDelta            time.Duration\n339: \tnoStepSubqueryIntervalFn func(rangeMillis int64) int64\n340: \tenableAtModifier         bool\n341: \tenableNegativeOffset     bool\n342: \tenablePerStepStats       bool\n343: \tenableDelayedNameRemoval bool\n344: \tenableTypeAndUnitLabels  bool\n345: }\n346: \n347: // NewEngine returns a new engine.\n348: func NewEngine(opts EngineOpts) *Engine {\n349: \tif opts.Logger == nil {\n350: \t\topts.Logger = promslog.NewNopLogger()\n351: \t}\n352: \n353: \tqueryResultSummary := prometheus.NewSummaryVec(prometheus.SummaryOpts{\n354: \t\tNamespace:  namespace,\n355: \t\tSubsystem:  subsystem,\n356: \t\tName:       \"query_duration_seconds\",\n357: \t\tHelp:       \"Query timings\",\n358: \t\tObjectives: map[float64]float64{0.5: 0.05, 0.9: 0.01, 0.99: 0.001},\n359: \t},\n360: \t\t[]string{\"slice\"},\n361: \t)\n362: \n363: \tmetrics := &engineMetrics{\n364: \t\tcurrentQueries: prometheus.NewGauge(prometheus.GaugeOpts{\n365: \t\t\tNamespace: namespace,\n366: \t\t\tSubsystem: subsystem,\n367: \t\t\tName:      \"queries\",\n368: \t\t\tHelp:      \"The current number of queries being executed or waiting.\",\n369: \t\t}),\n370: \t\tqueryLogEnabled: prometheus.NewGauge(prometheus.GaugeOpts{\n371: \t\t\tNamespace: namespace,\n372: \t\t\tSubsystem: subsystem,\n373: \t\t\tName:      \"query_log_enabled\",\n374: \t\t\tHelp:      \"State of the query log.\",\n375: \t\t}),\n376: \t\tqueryLogFailures: prometheus.NewCounter(prometheus.CounterOpts{\n377: \t\t\tNamespace: namespace,\n378: \t\t\tSubsystem: subsystem,\n379: \t\t\tName:      \"query_log_failures_total\",\n380: \t\t\tHelp:      \"The number of query log failures.\",\n381: \t\t}),\n382: \t\tmaxConcurrentQueries: prometheus.NewGauge(prometheus.GaugeOpts{\n383: \t\t\tNamespace: namespace,\n384: \t\t\tSubsystem: subsystem,\n385: \t\t\tName:      \"queries_concurrent_max\",\n386: \t\t\tHelp:      \"The max number of concurrent queries.\",\n387: \t\t}),\n388: \t\tquerySamples: prometheus.NewCounter(prometheus.CounterOpts{\n389: \t\t\tNamespace: namespace,\n390: \t\t\tSubsystem: subsystem,\n391: \t\t\tName:      \"query_samples_total\",\n392: \t\t\tHelp:      \"The total number of samples loaded by all queries.\",\n393: \t\t}),\n394: \t\tqueryQueueTime:   queryResultSummary.WithLabelValues(\"queue_time\"),\n395: \t\tqueryPrepareTime: queryResultSummary.WithLabelValues(\"prepare_time\"),\n396: \t\tqueryInnerEval:   queryResultSummary.WithLabelValues(\"inner_eval\"),\n397: \t\tqueryResultSort:  queryResultSummary.WithLabelValues(\"result_sort\"),\n398: \t}\n399: \n400: \tif t := opts.ActiveQueryTracker; t != nil {\n401: \t\tmetrics.maxConcurrentQueries.Set(float64(t.GetMaxConcurrent()))\n402: \t} else {\n403: \t\tmetrics.maxConcurrentQueries.Set(-1)\n404: \t}\n405: \n406: \tif opts.LookbackDelta == 0 {\n407: \t\topts.LookbackDelta = defaultLookbackDelta\n408: \t\tif l := opts.Logger; l != nil {\n409: \t\t\tl.Debug(\"Lookback delta is zero, setting to default value\", \"value\", defaultLookbackDelta)\n410: \t\t}\n411: \t}\n412: \n413: \tif opts.Reg != nil {\n414: \t\topts.Reg.MustRegister(\n415: \t\t\tmetrics.currentQueries,\n416: \t\t\tmetrics.maxConcurrentQueries,\n417: \t\t\tmetrics.queryLogEnabled,\n418: \t\t\tmetrics.queryLogFailures,\n419: \t\t\tmetrics.querySamples,\n420: \t\t\tqueryResultSummary,\n421: \t\t)\n422: \t}\n423: \n424: \treturn &Engine{\n425: \t\ttimeout:                  opts.Timeout,\n426: \t\tlogger:                   opts.Logger,\n427: \t\tmetrics:                  metrics,\n428: \t\tmaxSamplesPerQuery:       opts.MaxSamples,\n429: \t\tactiveQueryTracker:       opts.ActiveQueryTracker,\n430: \t\tlookbackDelta:            opts.LookbackDelta,\n431: \t\tnoStepSubqueryIntervalFn: opts.NoStepSubqueryIntervalFn,\n432: \t\tenableAtModifier:         opts.EnableAtModifier,\n433: \t\tenableNegativeOffset:     opts.EnableNegativeOffset,\n434: \t\tenablePerStepStats:       opts.EnablePerStepStats,\n435: \t\tenableDelayedNameRemoval: opts.EnableDelayedNameRemoval,\n436: \t\tenableTypeAndUnitLabels:  opts.EnableTypeAndUnitLabels,\n437: \t}\n438: }\n439: \n440: // Close closes ng.\n441: // Callers must ensure the engine is really no longer in use before calling this to avoid\n442: // issues failures like in https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/issues/15232\n443: func (ng *Engine) Close() error {\n444: \tif ng == nil {\n445: \t\treturn nil\n446: \t}\n447: \n448: \tif ng.activeQueryTracker != nil {\n449: \t\treturn ng.activeQueryTracker.Close()\n450: \t}\n451: \treturn nil\n452: }\n453: \n454: // SetQueryLogger sets the query logger.\n455: func (ng *Engine) SetQueryLogger(l QueryLogger) {\n456: \tng.queryLoggerLock.Lock()\n457: \tdefer ng.queryLoggerLock.Unlock()\n458: \n459: \tif ng.queryLogger != nil {\n460: \t\t// An error closing the old file descriptor should\n461: \t\t// not make reload fail; only log a warning.\n462: \t\terr := ng.queryLogger.Close()\n463: \t\tif err != nil {\n464: \t\t\tng.logger.Warn(\"Error while closing the previous query log file\", \"err\", err)\n465: \t\t}\n466: \t}\n467: \n468: \tng.queryLogger = l\n469: \n470: \tif l != nil {\n471: \t\tng.metrics.queryLogEnabled.Set(1)\n472: \t} else {\n473: \t\tng.metrics.queryLogEnabled.Set(0)\n474: \t}\n475: }\n476: \n477: // NewInstantQuery returns an evaluation query for the given expression at the given time.\n478: func (ng *Engine) NewInstantQuery(ctx context.Context, q storage.Queryable, opts QueryOpts, qs string, ts time.Time) (Query, error) {\n479: \tpExpr, qry := ng.newQuery(q, qs, opts, ts, ts, 0*time.Second)\n480: \tfinishQueue, err := ng.queueActive(ctx, qry)\n481: \tif err != nil {\n482: \t\treturn nil, err\n483: \t}\n484: \tdefer finishQueue()\n485: \texpr, err := parser.ParseExpr(qs)\n486: \tif err != nil {\n487: \t\treturn nil, err\n488: \t}\n489: \tif err := ng.validateOpts(expr); err != nil {\n490: \t\treturn nil, err\n491: \t}\n492: \t*pExpr, err = PreprocessExpr(expr, ts, ts, 0)\n493: \n494: \treturn qry, err\n495: }\n496: \n497: // NewRangeQuery returns an evaluation query for the given time range and with\n498: // the resolution set by the interval.\n499: func (ng *Engine) NewRangeQuery(ctx context.Context, q storage.Queryable, opts QueryOpts, qs string, start, end time.Time, interval time.Duration) (Query, error) {\n500: \tpExpr, qry := ng.newQuery(q, qs, opts, start, end, interval)\n501: ",

41. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "python3 << 'PYEOF'\nimport subprocess\nimport os\n\n# Create the workspace directory structure\nos.makedirs('/workspace/promql', exist_ok=True)\nos.makedirs('/workspace/promql/promqltest', exist_ok=True)\nos.makedirs('/workspace/web/api/v1', exist_ok=True)\nos.makedirs('/workspace/rules', exist_ok=True)\n\n# Files to download and process\nfiles = [\n    ('promql/engine.go', 'promql/engine.go'),\n    ('promql/promqltest/test.go', 'promql/promqltest/test.go'),\n    ('web/api/v1/api.go', 'web/api/v1/api.go'),\n    ('rules/manager.go', 'rules/manager.go'),\n]\n\nbase_url = 'https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4/-/raw/'\n\nprint(\"Downloading files...\")\nfor src, dest in files:\n    url = base_url + src\n    local_path = '/workspace/' + dest\n    print(f\"  Downloading {src}...\")\n    result = subprocess.run(['curl', '-s', url, '-o', local_path], capture_output=True)\n    if result.returncode == 0:\n        size = os.path.getsize(local_path)\n        print(f\"    \u2713 Downloaded {size} bytes to {local_path}\")\n    else:\n        print(f\"    \u2717 Failed to download {src}\")\n\nPYEOF\n",
  "description": "Download files from Sourcegraph using curl"
}
