"{\"content\":\"1601: // evaluated MatrixSelector in its place. Note that the Name and LabelMatchers are not set.\\n1602: func (ev *evaluator) evalSubquery(ctx context.Context, subq *parser.SubqueryExpr) (*parser.MatrixSelector, int, annotations.Annotations) {\\n1603: \\tsamplesStats := ev.samplesStats\\n1604: \\t// Avoid double counting samples when running a subquery, those samples will be counted in later stage.\\n1605: \\tev.samplesStats = ev.samplesStats.NewChild()\\n1606: \\tval, ws := ev.eval(ctx, subq)\\n1607: \\t// But do incorporate the peak from the subquery.\\n1608: \\tsamplesStats.UpdatePeakFromSubquery(ev.samplesStats)\\n1609: \\tev.samplesStats = samplesStats\\n1610: \\tmat := val.(Matrix)\\n1611: \\tvs := &parser.VectorSelector{\\n1612: \\t\\tOriginalOffset: subq.OriginalOffset,\\n1613: \\t\\tOffset:         subq.Offset,\\n1614: \\t\\tSeries:         make([]storage.Series, 0, len(mat)),\\n1615: \\t\\tTimestamp:      subq.Timestamp,\\n1616: \\t}\\n1617: \\tif subq.Timestamp != nil {\\n1618: \\t\\t// The offset of subquery is not modified in case of @ modifier.\\n1619: \\t\\t// Hence we take care of that here for the result.\\n1620: \\t\\tvs.Offset = subq.OriginalOffset + time.Duration(ev.startTimestamp-*subq.Timestamp)*time.Millisecond\\n1621: \\t}\\n1622: \\tms := &parser.MatrixSelector{\\n1623: \\t\\tRange:          subq.Range,\\n1624: \\t\\tVectorSelector: vs,\\n1625: \\t}\\n1626: \\tfor _, s := range mat {\\n1627: \\t\\t// Set any \\\"NotCounterReset\\\" and \\\"CounterReset\\\" hints in native\\n1628: \\t\\t// histograms to \\\"UnknownCounterReset\\\" because we might\\n1629: \\t\\t// otherwise miss a counter reset happening in samples not\\n1630: \\t\\t// returned by the subquery, or we might over-detect counter\\n1631: \\t\\t// resets if the sample with a counter reset is returned\\n1632: \\t\\t// multiple times by a high-res subquery. This intentionally\\n1633: \\t\\t// does not attempt to be clever (like detecting if we are\\n1634: \\t\\t// really missing underlying samples or returning underlying\\n1635: \\t\\t// samples multiple times) because subqueries on counters are\\n1636: \\t\\t// inherently problematic WRT counter reset handling, so we\\n1637: \\t\\t// cannot really solve the problem for good. We only want to\\n1638: \\t\\t// avoid problems that happen due to the explicitly set counter\\n1639: \\t\\t// reset hints and go back to the behavior we already know from\\n1640: \\t\\t// float samples.\\n1641: \\t\\tfor i, hp := range s.Histograms {\\n1642: \\t\\t\\tswitch hp.H.CounterResetHint {\\n1643: \\t\\t\\tcase histogram.NotCounterReset, histogram.CounterReset:\\n1644: \\t\\t\\t\\th := *hp.H // Shallow copy is sufficient, we only change CounterResetHint.\\n1645: \\t\\t\\t\\th.CounterResetHint = histogram.UnknownCounterReset\\n1646: \\t\\t\\t\\ts.Histograms[i].H = &h\\n1647: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1648: \\t\\t}\\n1649: \\t\\tvs.Series = append(vs.Series, NewStorageSeries(s))\\n1650: \\t}\\n1651: \\treturn ms, mat.TotalSamples(), ws\\n1652: }\\n1653: \\n1654: // eval evaluates the given expression as the given AST expression node requires.\\n1655: func (ev *evaluator) eval(ctx context.Context, expr parser.Expr) (parser.Value, annotations.Annotations) {\\n1656: \\t// This is the top-level evaluation method.\\n1657: \\t// Thus, we check for timeout/cancellation here.\\n1658: \\tif err := contextDone(ctx, \\\"expression evaluation\\\"); err != nil {\\n1659: \\t\\tev.error(err)\\n1660: \\t}\\n1661: \\n1662: \\tif ev.endTimestamp < ev.startTimestamp {\\n1663: \\t\\treturn Matrix{}, nil\\n1664: \\t}\\n1665: \\n1666: \\tnumSteps := int((ev.endTimestamp-ev.startTimestamp)/ev.interval) + 1\\n1667: \\n1668: \\t// Create a new span to help investigate inner evaluation performances.\\n1669: \\tctx, span := otel.Tracer(\\\"\\\").Start(ctx, stats.InnerEvalTime.SpanOperation()+\\\" eval \\\"+reflect.TypeOf(expr).String())\\n1670: \\tdefer span.End()\\n1671: \\tif ss, ok := expr.(interface{ ShortString() string }); ok {\\n1672: \\t\\tspan.SetAttributes(attribute.String(\\\"operation\\\", ss.ShortString()))\\n1673: \\t}\\n1674: \\n1675: \\tswitch e := expr.(type) {\\n1676: \\tcase *parser.AggregateExpr:\\n1677: \\t\\t// Grouping labels must be sorted (expected both by generateGroupingKey() and aggregation()).\\n1678: \\t\\tsortedGrouping := e.Grouping\\n1679: \\t\\tslices.Sort(sortedGrouping)\\n1680: \\n1681: \\t\\tif e.Op == parser.COUNT_VALUES {\\n1682: \\t\\t\\tvalueLabel := e.Param.(*parser.StringLiteral)\\n1683: \\t\\t\\tif !model.UTF8Validation.IsValidLabelName(valueLabel.Val) {\\n1684: \\t\\t\\t\\tev.errorf(\\\"invalid label name %s\\\", valueLabel)\\n1685: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1686: \\t\\t\\tif !e.Without {\\n1687: \\t\\t\\t\\tsortedGrouping = append(sortedGrouping, valueLabel.Val)\\n1688: \\t\\t\\t\\tslices.Sort(sortedGrouping)\\n1689: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1690: \\t\\t\\treturn ev.rangeEval(ctx, nil, func(v []Vector, _ Matrix, _ [][]EvalSeriesHelper, enh *EvalNodeHelper) (Vector, annotations.Annotations) {\\n1691: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn ev.aggregationCountValues(e, sortedGrouping, valueLabel.Val, v[0], enh)\\n1692: \\t\\t\\t}, e.Expr)\\n1693: \\t\\t}\\n1694: \\n1695: \\t\\tvar warnings annotations.Annotations\\n1696: \\t\\toriginalNumSamples := ev.currentSamples\\n1697: \\t\\t// e.Param is the number k for topk/bottomk, or q for quantile.\\n1698: \\t\\tfp, ws := newFParams(ctx, ev, e.Param)\\n1699: \\t\\twarnings.Merge(ws)\\n1700: \\t\\t// Now fetch the data to be aggregated.\\n1701: \\t\\tval, ws := ev.eval(ctx, e.Expr)\\n1702: \\t\\twarnings.Merge(ws)\\n1703: \\t\\tinputMatrix := val.(Matrix)\\n1704: \\n1705: \\t\\tresult, ws := ev.rangeEvalAgg(ctx, e, sortedGrouping, inputMatrix, fp)\\n1706: \\t\\twarnings.Merge(ws)\\n1707: \\t\\tev.currentSamples = originalNumSamples + result.TotalSamples()\\n1708: \\t\\tev.samplesStats.UpdatePeak(ev.currentSamples)\\n1709: \\n1710: \\t\\treturn result, warnings\\n1711: \\n1712: \\tcase *parser.Call:\\n1713: \\t\\tcall := FunctionCalls[e.Func.Name]\\n1714: \\t\\tif e.Func.Name == \\\"timestamp\\\" {\\n1715: \\t\\t\\t// Matrix evaluation always returns the evaluation time,\\n1716: \\t\\t\\t// so this function needs special handling when given\\n1717: \\t\\t\\t// a vector selector.\\n1718: \\t\\t\\targ := unwrapStepInvariantExpr(e.Args[0])\\n1719: \\t\\t\\tvs, ok := arg.(*parser.VectorSelector)\\n1720: \\t\\t\\tif ok {\\n1721: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn ev.rangeEvalTimestampFunctionOverVectorSelector(ctx, vs, call, e)\\n1722: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1723: \\t\\t}\\n1724: \\n1725: \\t\\t// Check if the function has a matrix argument.\\n1726: \\t\\tvar (\\n1727: \\t\\t\\tmatrixArgIndex int\\n1728: \\t\\t\\tmatrixArg      bool\\n1729: \\t\\t\\twarnings       annotations.Annotations\\n1730: \\t\\t)\\n1731: \\t\\tfor i := range e.Args {\\n1732: \\t\\t\\ta := e.Args[i]\\n1733: \\t\\t\\tif _, ok := a.(*parser.MatrixSelector); ok {\\n1734: \\t\\t\\t\\tmatrixArgIndex = i\\n1735: \\t\\t\\t\\tmatrixArg = true\\n1736: \\t\\t\\t\\tbreak\\n1737: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1738: \\t\\t\\t// parser.SubqueryExpr can be used in place of parser.MatrixSelector.\\n1739: \\t\\t\\tif subq, ok := a.(*parser.SubqueryExpr); ok {\\n1740: \\t\\t\\t\\tmatrixArgIndex = i\\n1741: \\t\\t\\t\\tmatrixArg = true\\n1742: \\t\\t\\t\\t// Replacing parser.SubqueryExpr with parser.MatrixSelector.\\n1743: \\t\\t\\t\\tval, totalSamples, ws := ev.evalSubquery(ctx, subq)\\n1744: \\t\\t\\t\\te.Args[i] = val\\n1745: \\t\\t\\t\\twarnings.Merge(ws)\\n1746: \\t\\t\\t\\tdefer func() {\\n1747: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// subquery result takes space in the memory. Get rid of that at the end.\\n1748: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tval.VectorSelector.(*parser.VectorSelector).Series = nil\\n1749: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tev.currentSamples -= totalSamples\\n1750: \\t\\t\\t\\t}()\\n1751: \\t\\t\\t\\tbreak\\n1752: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1753: \\t\\t}\\n1754: \\n1755: \\t\\t// Special handling for functions that work on series not samples.\\n1756: \\t\\tswitch e.Func.Name {\\n1757: \\t\\tcase \\\"label_replace\\\":\\n1758: \\t\\t\\treturn ev.evalLabelReplace(ctx, e.Args)\\n1759: \\t\\tcase \\\"label_join\\\":\\n1760: \\t\\t\\treturn ev.evalLabelJoin(ctx, e.Args)\\n1761: \\t\\tcase \\\"info\\\":\\n1762: \\t\\t\\treturn ev.evalInfo(ctx, e.Args)\\n1763: \\t\\t}\\n1764: \\n1765: \\t\\tif !matrixArg {\\n1766: \\t\\t\\t// Does not have a matrix argument.\\n1767: \\t\\t\\treturn ev.rangeEval(ctx, nil, func(v []Vector, _ Matrix, _ [][]EvalSeriesHelper, enh *EvalNodeHelper) (Vector, annotations.Annotations) {\\n1768: \\t\\t\\t\\tvec, annos := call(v, nil, e.Args, enh)\\n1769: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn vec, warnings.Merge(annos)\\n1770: \\t\\t\\t}, e.Args...)\\n1771: \\t\\t}\\n1772: \\n1773: \\t\\t// Evaluate any non-matrix arguments.\\n1774: \\t\\tevalVals := make([]Matrix, len(e.Args))\\n1775: \\t\\tfor i, e := range e.Args {\\n1776: \\t\\t\\tif i != matrixArgIndex {\\n1777: \\t\\t\\t\\tval, ws := ev.eval(ctx, e)\\n1778: \\t\\t\\t\\tevalVals[i] = val.(Matrix)\\n1779: \\t\\t\\t\\twarnings.Merge(ws)\\n1780: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1781: \\t\\t}\\n1782: \\n1783: \\t\\targ := e.Args[matrixArgIndex]\\n1784: \\t\\tsel := arg.(*parser.MatrixSelector)\\n1785: \\t\\tselVS := sel.VectorSelector.(*parser.VectorSelector)\\n1786: \\n1787: \\t\\tws, err := checkAndExpandSeriesSet(ctx, sel)\\n1788: \\t\\twarnings.Merge(ws)\\n1789: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n1790: \\t\\t\\tev.error(errWithWarnings{fmt.Errorf(\\\"expanding series: %w\\\", err), warnings})\\n1791: \\t\\t}\\n1792: \\t\\tmat := make(Matrix, 0, len(selVS.Series)) // Output matrix.\\n1793: \\t\\toffset := durationMilliseconds(selVS.Offset)\\n1794: \\t\\tselRange := durationMilliseconds(sel.Range)\\n1795: \\t\\tstepRange := min(selRange, ev.interval)\\n1796: \\t\\t// Reuse objects across steps to save memory allocations.\\n1797: \\t\\tvar floats []FPoint\\n1798: \\t\\tvar histograms []HPoint\\n1799: \\t\\tvar prevSS *Series\\n1800: \\t\\tinMatrix := make(Matrix, 1)\\n1801: \\t\\tenh := &EvalNodeHelper{Out: make(Vector, 0, 1), enableDelayedNameRemoval: ev.enableDelayedNameRemoval}\\n1802: \\t\\t// Process all the calls for one time series at a time.\\n1803: \\t\\tit := storage.NewBuffer(selRange)\\n1804: \\t\\tvar chkIter chunkenc.Iterator\\n1805: \\n1806: \\t\\t// The last_over_time function acts like offset; thus, it\\n1807: \\t\\t// should keep the metric name.  For all the other range\\n1808: \\t\\t// vector functions, the only change needed is to drop the\\n1809: \\t\\t// metric name in the output.\\n1810: \\t\\tdropName := e.Func.Name != \\\"last_over_time\\\"\\n1811: \\t\\tvectorVals := make([]Vector, len(e.Args)-1)\\n1812: \\t\\tfor i, s := range selVS.Series {\\n1813: \\t\\t\\tif err := contextDone(ctx, \\\"expression evaluation\\\"); err != nil {\\n1814: \\t\\t\\t\\tev.error(err)\\n1815: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1816: \\t\\t\\tev.currentSamples -= len(floats) + totalHPointSize(histograms)\\n1817: \\t\\t\\tif floats != nil {\\n1818: \\t\\t\\t\\tfloats = floats[:0]\\n1819: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1820: \\t\\t\\tif histograms != nil {\\n1821: \\t\\t\\t\\thistograms = histograms[:0]\\n1822: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1823: \\t\\t\\tchkIter = s.Iterator(chkIter)\\n1824: \\t\\t\\tit.Reset(chkIter)\\n1825: \\t\\t\\tmetric := selVS.Series[i].Labels()\\n1826: \\t\\t\\tif !ev.enableDelayedNameRemoval && dropName {\\n1827: \\t\\t\\t\\tmetric = metric.DropReserved(schema.IsMetadataLabel)\\n1828: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1829: \\t\\t\\tss := Series{\\n1830: \\t\\t\\t\\tMetric:   metric,\\n1831: \\t\\t\\t\\tDropName: dropName,\\n1832: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1833: \\t\\t\\tinMatrix[0].Metric = selVS.Series[i].Labels()\\n1834: \\t\\t\\tfor ts, step := ev.startTimestamp, -1; ts <= ev.endTimestamp; ts += ev.interval {\\n1835: \\t\\t\\t\\tstep++\\n1836: \\t\\t\\t\\t// Set the non-matrix arguments.\\n1837: \\t\\t\\t\\t// They are scalar, so it is safe to use the step number\\n1838: \\t\\t\\t\\t// when looking up the argument, as there will be no gaps.\\n1839: \\t\\t\\t\\tcounter := 0\\n1840: \\t\\t\\t\\tfor j := range e.Args {\\n1841: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif j != matrixArgIndex {\\n1842: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tvectorVals[counter] = Vector{Sample{F: evalVals[j][0].Floats[step].F}}\\n1843: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcounter++\\n1844: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1845: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1846: \\t\\t\\t\\t// Evaluate the matrix selector for this series\\n1847: \\t\\t\\t\\t// for this step, but only if this is the 1st\\n1848: \\t\\t\\t\\t// iteration or no @ modifier has been used.\\n1849: \\t\\t\\t\\tif ts == ev.startTimestamp || selVS.Timestamp == nil {\\n1850: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tmaxt := ts - offset\\n1851: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tmint := maxt - selRange\\n1852: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tfloats, histograms = ev.matrixIterSlice(it, mint, maxt, floats, histograms)\\n1853: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1854: \\t\\t\\t\\tif len(floats)+len(histograms) == 0 {\\n1855: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n1856: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1857: \\t\\t\\t\\tinMatrix[0].Floats = floats\\n1858: \\t\\t\\t\\tinMatrix[0].Histograms = histograms\\n1859: \\t\\t\\t\\tenh.Ts = ts\\n1860: \\n1861: \\t\\t\\t\\t// Make the function call.\\n1862: \\t\\t\\t\\toutVec, annos := call(vectorVals, inMatrix, e.Args, enh)\\n1863: \\t\\t\\t\\twarnings.Merge(annos)\\n1864: \\t\\t\\t\\tev.samplesStats.IncrementSamplesAtStep(step, int64(len(floats)+totalHPointSize(histograms)))\\n1865: \\n1866: \\t\\t\\t\\tenh.Out = outVec[:0]\\n1867: \\t\\t\\t\\tif len(outVec) > 0 {\\n1868: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif outVec[0].H == nil {\\n1869: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif ss.Floats == nil {\\n1870: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tss.Floats = reuseOrGetFPointSlices(prevSS, numSteps)\\n1871: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1872: \\n1873: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tss.Floats = append(ss.Floats, FPoint{F: outVec[0].F, T: ts})\\n1874: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n1875: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif ss.Histograms == nil {\\n1876: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tss.Histograms = reuseOrGetHPointSlices(prevSS, numSteps)\\n1877: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1878: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tss.Histograms = append(ss.Histograms, HPoint{H: outVec[0].H, T: ts})\\n1879: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1880: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1881: \\t\\t\\t\\t// Only buffer stepRange milliseconds from the second step on.\\n1882: \\t\\t\\t\\tit.ReduceDelta(stepRange)\\n1883: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1884: \\t\\t\\thistSamples := totalHPointSize(ss.Histograms)\\n1885: \\n1886: \\t\\t\\tif len(ss.Floats)+histSamples > 0 {\\n1887: \\t\\t\\t\\tif ev.currentSamples+len(ss.Floats)+histSamples > ev.maxSamples {\\n1888: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tev.error(ErrTooManySamples(env))\\n1889: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1890: \\t\\t\\t\\tmat = append(mat, ss)\\n1891: \\t\\t\\t\\tprevSS = &mat[len(mat)-1]\\n1892: \\t\\t\\t\\tev.currentSamples += len(ss.Floats) + histSamples\\n1893: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1894: \\t\\t\\tev.samplesStats.UpdatePeak(ev.currentSamples)\\n1895: \\n1896: \\t\\t\\tif e.Func.Name == \\\"rate\\\" || e.Func.Name == \\\"increase\\\" {\\n1897: \\t\\t\\t\\tmetricName := inMatrix[0].Metric.Get(labels.MetricName)\\n1898: \\t\\t\\t\\tif metricName != \\\"\\\" && len(ss.Floats) > 0 {\\n1899: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif ev.enableTypeAndUnitLabels {\\n1900: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// When type-and-unit-labels feature is enabled, check __type__ label\\n1901: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\ttypeLabel := inMatrix[0].Metric.Get(\\\"__type__\\\")\\n1902: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif typeLabel != string(model.MetricTypeCounter) && typeLabel != string(model.MetricTypeHistogram) {\\n1903: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\twarnings.Add(annotations.NewPossibleNonCounterLabelInfo(metricName, typeLabel, e.Args[0].PositionRange()))\\n1904: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1905: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t} else if !strings.HasSuffix(metricName, \\\"_total\\\") &&\\n1906: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t!strings.HasSuffix(metricName, \\\"_sum\\\") &&\\n1907: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t!strings.HasSuffix(metricName, \\\"_count\\\") &&\\n1908: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t!strings.HasSuffix(metricName, \\\"_bucket\\\") {\\n1909: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// Fallback to name suffix checking\\n1910: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\twarnings.Add(annotations.NewPossibleNonCounterInfo(metricName, e.Args[0].PositionRange()))\\n1911: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1912: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1913: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1914: \\t\\t}\\n1915: \\t\\tev.samplesStats.UpdatePeak(ev.currentSamples)\\n1916: \\n1917: \\t\\tev.currentSamples -= len(floats) + totalHPointSize(histograms)\\n1918: \\t\\tputFPointSlice(floats)\\n1919: \\t\\tputMatrixSelectorHPointSlice(histograms)\\n1920: \\n1921: \\t\\t// The absent_over_time function returns 0 or 1 series. So far, the matrix\\n1922: \\t\\t// contains multiple series. The following code will create a new series\\n1923: \\t\\t// with values of 1 for the timestamps where no series has value.\\n1924: \\t\\tif e.Func.Name == \\\"absent_over_time\\\" {\\n1925: \\t\\t\\tsteps := int(1 + (ev.endTimestamp-ev.startTimestamp)/ev.interval)\\n1926: \\t\\t\\t// Iterate once to look for a complete series.\\n1927: \\t\\t\\tfor _, s := range mat {\\n1928: \\t\\t\\t\\tif len(s.Floats)+len(s.Histograms) == steps {\\n1929: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn Matrix{}, warnings\\n1930: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1931: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1932: \\n1933: \\t\\t\\tfound := map[int64]struct{}{}\\n1934: \\n1935: \\t\\t\\tfor i, s := range mat {\\n1936: \\t\\t\\t\\tfor _, p := range s.Floats {\\n1937: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tfound[p.T] = struct{}{}\\n1938: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1939: \\t\\t\\t\\tfor _, p := range s.Histograms {\\n1940: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tfound[p.T] = struct{}{}\\n1941: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1942: \\t\\t\\t\\tif i > 0 && len(found) == steps {\\n1943: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn Matrix{}, warnings\\n1944: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1945: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1946: \\n1947: \\t\\t\\tnewp := make([]FPoint, 0, steps-len(found))\\n1948: \\t\\t\\tfor ts := ev.startTimestamp; ts <= ev.endTimestamp; ts += ev.interval {\\n1949: \\t\\t\\t\\tif _, ok := found[ts]; !ok {\\n1950: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tnewp = append(newp, FPoint{T: ts, F: 1})\\n1951: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1952: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1953: \\n1954: \\t\\t\\treturn Matrix{\\n1955: \\t\\t\\t\\tSeries{\\n1956: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tMetric:   createLabelsForAbsentFunction(e.Args[0]),\\n1957: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tFloats:   newp,\\n1958: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tDropName: dropName,\\n1959: \\t\\t\\t\\t},\\n1960: \\t\\t\\t}, warnings\\n1961: \\t\\t}\\n1962: \\n1963: \\t\\tif !ev.enableDelayedNameRemoval && mat.ContainsSameLabelset() {\\n1964: \\t\\t\\tev.errorf(\\\"vector cannot contain metrics with the same labelset\\\")\\n1965: \\t\\t}\\n1966: \\t\\treturn mat, warnings\\n1967: \\n1968: \\tcase *parser.ParenExpr:\\n1969: \\t\\treturn ev.eval(ctx, e.Expr)\\n1970: \\n1971: \\tcase *parser.UnaryExpr:\\n1972: \\t\\tval, ws := ev.eval(ctx, e.Expr)\\n1973: \\t\\tmat := val.(Matrix)\\n1974: \\t\\tif e.Op == parser.SUB {\\n1975: \\t\\t\\tfor i := range mat {\\n1976: \\t\\t\\t\\tif !ev.enableDelayedNameRemoval {\\n1977: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tmat[i].Metric = mat[i].Metric.DropReserved(schema.IsMetadataLabel)\\n1978: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1979: \\t\\t\\t\\tmat[i].DropName = true\\n1980: \\t\\t\\t\\tfor j := range mat[i].Floats {\\n1981: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tmat[i].Floats[j].F = -mat[i].Floats[j].F\\n1982: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1983: \\t\\t\\t\\tfor j := range mat[i].Histograms {\\n1984: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tmat[i].Histograms[j].H = mat[i].Histograms[j].H.Copy().Mul(-1)\\n1985: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1986: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1987: \\t\\t\\tif !ev.enableDelayedNameRemoval && mat.ContainsSameLabelset() {\\n1988: \\t\\t\\t\\tev.errorf(\\\"vector cannot contain metrics with the same labelset\\\")\\n1989: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1990: \\t\\t}\\n1991: \\t\\treturn mat, ws\\n1992: \\n1993: \\tcase *parser.BinaryExpr:\\n1994: \\t\\tswitch lt, rt := e.LHS.Type(), e.RHS.Type(); {\\n1995: \\t\\tcase lt == parser.ValueTypeScalar && rt == parser.ValueTypeScalar:\\n1996: \\t\\t\\treturn ev.rangeEval(ctx, nil, func(v []Vector, _ Matrix, _ [][]EvalSeriesHelper, enh *EvalNodeHelper) (Vector, annotations.Annotations) {\\n1997: \\t\\t\\t\\tval := scalarBinop(e.Op, v[0][0].F, v[1][0].F)\\n1998: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn append(enh.Out, Sample{F: val}), nil\\n1999: \\t\\t\\t}, e.LHS, e.RHS)\\n2000: \\t\\tcase lt == parser.ValueTypeVector && rt == parser.ValueTypeVector:\\n2001: \\t\\t\\t// Function to compute the join signature for each series.\\n2002: \\t\\t\\tbuf := make([]byte, 0, 1024)\\n2003: \\t\\t\\tsigf := signatureFunc(e.VectorMatching.On, buf, e.VectorMatching.MatchingLabels...)\\n2004: \\t\\t\\tinitSignatures := func(series labels.Labels, h *EvalSeriesHelper) {\\n2005: \\t\\t\\t\\th.signature = sigf(series)\\n2006: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2007: \\t\\t\\tswitch e.Op {\\n2008: \\t\\t\\tcase parser.LAND:\\n2009: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn ev.rangeEval(ctx, initSignatures, func(v []Vector, _ Matrix, sh [][]EvalSeriesHelper, enh *EvalNodeHelper) (Vector, annotations.Annotations) {\\n2010: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn ev.VectorAnd(v[0], v[1], e.VectorMatching, sh[0], sh[1], enh), nil\\n2011: \\t\\t\\t\\t}, e.LHS, e.RHS)\\n2012: \\t\\t\\tcase parser.LOR:\\n2013: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn ev.rangeEval(ctx, initSignatures, func(v []Vector, _ Matrix, sh [][]EvalSeriesHelper, enh *EvalNodeHelper) (Vector, annotations.Annotations) {\\n2014: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn ev.VectorOr(v[0], v[1], e.VectorMatching, sh[0], sh[1], enh), nil\\n2015: \\t\\t\\t\\t}, e.LHS, e.RHS)\\n2016: \\t\\t\\tcase parser.LUNLESS:\\n2017: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn ev.rangeEval(ctx, initSignatures, func(v []Vector, _ Matrix, sh [][]EvalSeriesHelper, enh *EvalNodeHelper) (Vector, annotations.Annotations) {\\n2018: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn ev.VectorUnless(v[0], v[1], e.VectorMatching, sh[0], sh[1], enh), nil\\n2019: \\t\\t\\t\\t}, e.LHS, e.RHS)\\n2020: \\t\\t\\tdefault:\\n2021: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn ev.rangeEval(ctx, initSignatures, func(v []Vector, _ Matrix, sh [][]EvalSeriesHelper, enh *EvalNodeHelper) (Vector, annotations.Annotations) {\\n2022: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tvec, err := ev.VectorBinop(e.Op, v[0], v[1], e.VectorMatching, e.ReturnBool, sh[0], sh[1], enh, e.PositionRange())\\n2023: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn vec, handleVectorBinopError(err, e)\\n2024: \\t\\t\\t\\t}, e.LHS, e.RHS)\\n2025: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2026: \\n2027: \\t\\tcase lt == parser.ValueTypeVector && rt == parser.ValueTypeScalar:\\n2028: \\t\\t\\treturn ev.rangeEval(ctx, nil, func(v []Vector, _ Matrix, _ [][]EvalSeriesHelper, enh *EvalNodeHelper) (Vector, annotations.Annotations) {\\n2029: \\t\\t\\t\\tvec, err := ev.VectorscalarBinop(e.Op, v[0], Scalar{V: v[1][0].F}, false, e.ReturnBool, enh, e.PositionRange())\\n2030: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn vec, handleVectorBinopError(err, e)\\n2031: \\t\\t\\t}, e.LHS, e.RHS)\\n2032: \\n2033: \\t\\tcase lt == parser.ValueTypeScalar && rt == parser.ValueTypeVector:\\n2034: \\t\\t\\treturn ev.rangeEval(ctx, nil, func(v []Vector, _ Matrix, _ [][]EvalSeriesHelper, enh *EvalNodeHelper) (Vector, annotations.Annotations) {\\n2035: \\t\\t\\t\\tvec, err := ev.VectorscalarBinop(e.Op, v[1], Scalar{V: v[0][0].F}, true, e.ReturnBool, enh, e.PositionRange())\\n2036: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn vec, handleVectorBinopError(err, e)\\n2037: \\t\\t\\t}, e.LHS, e.RHS)\\n2038: \\t\\t}\\n2039: \\n2040: \\tcase *parser.NumberLiteral:\\n2041: \\t\\tspan.SetAttributes(attribute.Float64(\\\"value\\\", e.Val))\\n2042: \\t\\treturn ev.rangeEval(ctx, nil, func(_ []Vector, _ Matrix, _ [][]EvalSeriesHelper, enh *EvalNodeHelper) (Vector, annotations.Annotations) {\\n2043: \\t\\t\\treturn append(enh.Out, Sample{F: e.Val, Metric: labels.EmptyLabels()}), nil\\n2044: \\t\\t})\\n2045: \\n2046: \\tcase *parser.StringLiteral:\\n2047: \\t\\tspan.SetAttributes(attribute.String(\\\"value\\\", e.Val))\\n2048: \\t\\treturn String{V: e.Val, T: ev.startTimestamp}, nil\\n2049: \\n2050: \\tcase *parser.VectorSelector:\\n2051: \\t\\tws, err := checkAndExpandSeriesSet(ctx, e)\\n2052: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n2053: \\t\\t\\tev.error(errWithWarnings{fmt.Errorf(\\\"expanding series: %w\\\", err), ws})\\n2054: \\t\\t}\\n2055: \\t\\tmat := ev.evalSeries(ctx, e.Series, e.Offset, false)\\n2056: \\t\\treturn mat, ws\\n2057: \\n2058: \\tcase *parser.MatrixSelector:\\n2059: \\t\\tif ev.startTimestamp != ev.endTimestamp {\\n2060: \\t\\t\\tpanic(errors.New(\\\"cannot do range evaluation of matrix selector\\\"))\\n2061: \\t\\t}\\n2062: \\t\\treturn ev.matrixSelector(ctx, e)\\n2063: \\n2064: \\tcase *parser.SubqueryExpr:\\n2065: \\t\\toffsetMillis := durationMilliseconds(e.Offset)\\n2066: \\t\\trangeMillis := durationMilliseconds(e.Range)\\n2067: \\t\\tnewEv := &evaluator{\\n2068: \\t\\t\\tendTimestamp:             ev.endTimestamp - offsetMillis,\\n2069: \\t\\t\\tcurrentSamples:           ev.currentSamples,\\n2070: \\t\\t\\tmaxSamples:               ev.maxSamples,\\n2071: \\t\\t\\tlogger:                   ev.logger,\\n2072: \\t\\t\\tlookbackDelta:            ev.lookbackDelta,\\n2073: \\t\\t\\tsamplesStats:             ev.samplesStats.NewChild(),\\n2074: \\t\\t\\tnoStepSubqueryIntervalFn: ev.noStepSubqueryIntervalFn,\\n2075: \\t\\t\\tenableDelayedNameRemoval: ev.enableDelayedNameRemoval,\\n2076: \\t\\t\\tenableTypeAndUnitLabels:  ev.enableTypeAndUnitLabels,\\n2077: \\t\\t\\tquerier:                  ev.querier,\\n2078: \\t\\t}\\n2079: \\n2080: \\t\\tif e.Step != 0 {\\n2081: \\t\\t\\tnewEv.interval = durationMilliseconds(e.Step)\\n2082: \\t\\t} else {\\n2083: \\t\\t\\tnewEv.interval = ev.noStepSubqueryIntervalFn(rangeMillis)\\n2084: \\t\\t}\\n2085: \\n2086: \\t\\t// Start with the first timestamp after (ev.startTimestamp - offset - range)\\n2087: \\t\\t// that is aligned with the step (multiple of 'newEv.interval').\\n2088: \\t\\tnewEv.startTimestamp = newEv.interval * ((ev.startTimestamp - offsetMillis - rangeMillis) / newEv.interval)\\n2089: \\t\\tif newEv.startTimestamp <= (ev.startTimestamp - offsetMillis - rangeMillis) {\\n2090: \\t\\t\\tnewEv.startTimestamp += newEv.interval\\n2091: \\t\\t}\\n2092: \\n2093: \\t\\tif newEv.startTimestamp != ev.startTimestamp {\\n2094: \\t\\t\\t// Adjust the offset of selectors based on the new\\n2095: \\t\\t\\t// start time of the evaluator since the calculation\\n2096: \\t\\t\\t// of the offset with @ happens w.r.t. the start time.\\n2097: \\t\\t\\tsetOffsetForAtModifier(newEv.startTimestamp, e.Expr)\\n2098: \\t\\t}\\n2099: \\n2100: \\t\\tres, ws := newEv.eval(ctx, e.Expr)\\n2101: \\t\\tev.currentSamples = newEv.currentSamples\\n2102: \\t\\tev.samplesStats.UpdatePeakFromSubquery(newEv.samplesStats)\\n2103: \\t\\tev.samplesStats.IncrementSamplesAtTimestamp(ev.endTimestamp, newEv.samplesStats.TotalSamples)\\n2104: \\t\\treturn res, ws\\n2105: \\tcase *parser.StepInvariantExpr:\\n2106: \\t\\tnewEv := &evaluator{\\n2107: \\t\\t\\tstartTimestamp:           ev.startTimestamp,\\n2108: \\t\\t\\tendTimestamp:             ev.startTimestamp, // Always a single evaluation.\\n2109: \\t\\t\\tinterval:                 ev.interval,\\n2110: \\t\\t\\tcurrentSamples:           ev.currentSamples,\\n2111: \\t\\t\\tmaxSamples:               ev.maxSamples,\\n2112: \\t\\t\\tlogger:                   ev.logger,\\n2113: \\t\\t\\tlookbackDelta:            ev.lookbackDelta,\\n2114: \\t\\t\\tsamplesStats:             ev.samplesStats.NewChild(),\\n2115: \\t\\t\\tnoStepSubqueryIntervalFn: ev.noStepSubqueryIntervalFn,\\n2116: \\t\\t\\tenableDelayedNameRemoval: ev.enableDelayedNameRemoval,\\n2117: \\t\\t\\tenableTypeAndUnitLabels:  ev.enableTypeAndUnitLabels,\\n2118: \\t\\t\\tquerier:                  ev.querier,\\n2119: \\t\\t}\\n2120: \\t\\tres, ws := newEv.eval(ctx, e.Expr)\\n2121: \\t\\tev.currentSamples = newEv.currentSamples\\n2122: \\t\\tev.samplesStats.UpdatePeakFromSubquery(newEv.samplesStats)\\n2123: \\t\\tfor ts, step := ev.startTimestamp, -1; ts <= ev.endTimestamp; ts += ev.interval {\\n2124: \\t\\t\\tstep++\\n2125: \\t\\t\\tev.samplesStats.IncrementSamplesAtStep(step, newEv.samplesStats.TotalSamples)\\n2126: \\t\\t}\\n2127: \\t\\tswitch e.Expr.(type) {\\n2128: \\t\\tcase *parser.MatrixSelector, *parser.SubqueryExpr:\\n2129: \\t\\t\\t// We do not duplicate results for range selectors since result is a matrix\\n2130: \\t\\t\\t// with their unique timestamps which does not depend on the step.\\n2131: \\t\\t\\treturn res, ws\\n2132: \\t\\t}\\n2133: \\n2134: \\t\\t// For every evaluation while the value remains same, the timestamp for that\\n2135: \\t\\t// value would change for different eval times. Hence we duplicate the result\\n2136: \\t\\t// with changed timestamps.\\n2137: \\t\\tmat, ok := res.(Matrix)\\n2138: \\t\\tif !ok {\\n2139: \\t\\t\\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\\\"unexpected result in StepInvariantExpr evaluation: %T\\\", expr))\\n2140: \\t\\t}\\n2141: \\t\\tfor i := range mat {\\n2142: \\t\\t\\tif len(mat[i].Floats)+len(mat[i].Histograms) != 1 {\\n2143: \\t\\t\\t\\tpanic(errors.New(\\\"unexpected number of samples\\\"))\\n2144: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2145: \\t\\t\\tfor ts := ev.startTimestamp + ev.interval; ts <= ev.endTimestamp; ts += ev.interval {\\n2146: \\t\\t\\t\\tif len(mat[i].Floats) > 0 {\\n2147: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tmat[i].Floats = append(mat[i].Floats, FPoint{\\n2148: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tT: ts,\\n2149: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tF: mat[i].Floats[0].F,\\n2150: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t})\\n2151: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tev.currentSamples++\\n2152: \\t\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n2153: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tpoint := HPoint{\\n2154: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tT: ts,\\n2155: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tH: mat[i].Histograms[0].H,\\n2156: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n2157: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tmat[i].Histograms = append(mat[i].Histograms, point)\\n2158: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tev.currentSamples += point.size()\\n2159: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n2160: \\t\\t\\t\\tif ev.currentSamples > ev.maxSamples {\\n2161: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tev.error(ErrTooManySamples(env))\\n2162: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n2163: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2164: \\t\\t}\\n2165: \\t\\tev.samplesStats.UpdatePeak(ev.currentSamples)\\n2166: \\t\\treturn res, ws\\n2167: \\t}\\n2168: \\n2169: \\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\\\"unhandled expression of type: %T\\\", expr))\\n2170: }\\n2171: \\n2172: // reuseOrGetHPointSlices reuses the space from previous slice to create new slice if the former has lots of room.\\n2173: // The previous slices capacity is adjusted so when it is re-used from the pool it doesn't overflow into the new one.\\n2174: func reuseOrGetHPointSlices(prevSS *Series, numSteps int) (r []HPoint) {\\n2175: \\tif prevSS != nil && cap(prevSS.Histograms)-2*len(prevSS.Histograms) > 0 {\\n2176: \\t\\tr = prevSS.Histograms[len(prevSS.Histograms):]\\n2177: \\t\\tprevSS.Histograms = prevSS.Histograms[0:len(prevSS.Histograms):len(prevSS.Histograms)]\\n2178: \\t\\treturn\\n2179: \\t}\\n2180: \\n2181: \\treturn getHPointSlice(numSteps)\\n2182: }\\n2183: \\n2184: // reuseOrGetFPointSlices reuses the space from previous slice to create new slice if the former has lots of room.\\n2185: // The previous slices capacity is adjusted so when it is re-used from the pool it doesn't overflow into the new one.\\n2186: func reuseOrGetFPointSlices(prevSS *Series, numSteps int) (r []FPoint) {\\n2187: \\tif prevSS != nil && cap(prevSS.Floats)-2*len(prevSS.Floats) > 0 {\\n2188: \\t\\tr = prevSS.Floats[len(prevSS.Floats):]\\n2189: \\t\\tprevSS.Floats = prevSS.Floats[0:len(prevSS.Floats):len(prevSS.Floats)]\\n2190: \\t\\treturn\\n2191: \\t}\\n2192: \\n2193: \\treturn getFPointSlice(numSteps)\\n2194: }\\n2195: \\n2196: func (ev *evaluator) rangeEvalTimestampFunctionOverVectorSelector(ctx context.Context, vs *parser.VectorSelector, call FunctionCall, e *parser.Call) (parser.Value, annotations.Annotations) {\\n2197: \\tws, err := checkAndExpandSeriesSet(ctx, vs)\\n2198: \\tif err != nil {\\n2199: \\t\\tev.error(errWithWarnings{fmt.Errorf(\\\"expanding series: %w\\\", err), ws})\\n2200: \\t}\\n2201: \\n2202: \\tseriesIterators := make([]*storage.MemoizedSeriesIterator, len(vs.Series))\\n2203: \\tfor i, s := range vs.Series {\\n2204: \\t\\tit := s.Iterator(nil)\\n2205: \\t\\tseriesIterators[i] = storage.NewMemoizedIterator(it, durationMilliseconds(ev.lookbackDelta)-1)\\n2206: \\t}\\n2207: \\n2208: \\treturn ev.rangeEval(ctx, nil, func(_ []Vector, _ Matrix, _ [][]EvalSeriesHelper, enh *EvalNodeHelper) (Vector, annotations.Annotations) {\\n2209: \\t\\tif vs.Timestamp != nil {\\n2210: \\t\\t\\t// This is a special case for \\\"timestamp()\\\" when the @ modifier is used, to ensure that\\n2211: \\t\\t\\t// we return a point for each time step in this case.\\n2212: \\t\\t\\t// See https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/issues/8433.\\n2213: \\t\\t\\tvs.Offset = time.Duration(enh.Ts-*vs.Timestamp) * time.Millisecond\\n2214: \\t\\t}\\n2215: \\n2216: \\t\\tvec := make(Vector, 0, len(vs.Series))\\n2217: \\t\\tfor i, s := range vs.Series {\\n2218: \\t\\t\\tit := seriesIterators[i]\\n2219: \\t\\t\\tt, _, _, ok := ev.vectorSelectorSingle(it, vs.Offset, enh.Ts)\\n2220: \\t\\t\\tif !ok {\\n2221: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n2222: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2223: \\n2224: \\t\\t\\t// Note that we ignore the sample values because call only cares about the timestamp.\\n2225: \\t\\t\\tvec = append(vec, Sample{\\n2226: \\t\\t\\t\\tMetric: s.Labels(),\\n2227: \\t\\t\\t\\tT:      t,\\n2228: \\t\\t\\t})\\n2229: \\n2230: \\t\\t\\tev.currentSamples++\\n2231: \\t\\t\\tev.samplesStats.IncrementSamplesAtTimestamp(enh.Ts, 1)\\n2232: \\t\\t\\tif ev.currentSamples > ev.maxSamples {\\n2233: \\t\\t\\t\\tev.error(ErrTooManySamples(env))\\n2234: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2235: \\t\\t}\\n2236: \\t\\tev.samplesStats.UpdatePeak(ev.currentSamples)\\n2237: \\t\\tvec, annos := call([]Vector{vec}, nil, e.Args, enh)\\n2238: \\t\\treturn vec, ws.Merge(annos)\\n2239: \\t})\\n2240: }\\n2241: \\n2242: // vectorSelectorSingle evaluates an instant vector for the iterator of one time series.\\n2243: func (ev *evaluator) vectorSelectorSingle(it *storage.MemoizedSeriesIterator, offset time.Duration, ts int64) (\\n2244: \\tint64, float64, *histogram.FloatHistogram, bool,\\n2245: ) {\\n2246: \\trefTime := ts - durationMilliseconds(offset)\\n2247: \\tvar t int64\\n2248: \\tvar v float64\\n2249: \\tvar h *histogram.FloatHistogram\\n2250: \\n2251: \\tvalueType := it.Seek(refTime)\\n2252: \\tswitch valueType {\\n2253: \\tcase chunkenc.ValNone:\\n2254: \\t\\tif it.Err() != nil {\\n2255: \\t\\t\\tev.error(it.Err())\\n2256: \\t\\t}\\n2257: \\tcase chunkenc.ValFloat:\\n2258: \\t\\tt, v = it.At()\\n2259: \\tcase chunkenc.ValFloatHistogram:\\n2260: \\t\\tt, h = it.AtFloatHistogram()\\n2261: \\tdefault:\\n2262: \\t\\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\\\"unknown value type %v\\\", valueType))\\n2263: \\t}\\n2264: \\tif valueType == chunkenc.ValNone || t > refTime {\\n2265: \\t\\tvar ok bool\\n2266: \\t\\tt, v, h, ok = it.PeekPrev()\\n2267: \\t\\tif !ok || t <= refTime-durationMilliseconds(ev.lookbackDelta) {\\n2268: \\t\\t\\treturn 0, 0, nil, false\\n2269: \\t\\t}\\n2270: \\t}\\n2271: \\tif value.IsStaleNaN(v) || (h != nil && value.IsStaleNaN(h.Sum)) {\\n2272: \\t\\treturn 0, 0, nil, false\\n2273: \\t}\\n2274: \\treturn t, v, h, true\\n2275: }\\n2276: \\n2277: var (\\n2278: \\tfPointPool zeropool.Pool[[]FPoint]\\n2279: \\thPointPool zeropool.Pool[[]HPoint]\\n2280: \\n2281: \\t// matrixSelectorHPool holds reusable histogram slices used by the matrix\\n2282: \\t// selector. The key difference between this pool and the hPointPool is that\\n2283: \\t// slices returned by this pool should never hold multiple copies of the same\\n2284: \\t// histogram pointer since histogram objects are reused across query evaluation\\n2285: \\t// steps.\\n2286: \\tmatrixSelectorHPool zeropool.Pool[[]HPoint]\\n2287: )\\n2288: \\n2289: func getFPointSlice(sz int) []FPoint {\\n2290: \\tif p := fPointPool.Get(); p != nil {\\n2291: \\t\\treturn p\\n2292: \\t}\\n2293: \\n2294: \\tif sz > maxPointsSliceSize {\\n2295: \\t\\tsz = maxPointsSliceSize\\n2296: \\t}\\n2297: \\n2298: \\treturn make([]FPoint, 0, sz)\\n2299: }\\n2300: \\n2301: // putFPointSlice will return a FPoint slice of size max(maxPointsSliceSize, sz).\\n2302: // This function is called with an estimated size which often can be over-estimated.\\n2303: func putFPointSlice(p []FPoint) {\\n2304: \\tif p != nil {\\n2305: \\t\\tfPointPool.Put(p[:0])\\n2306: \\t}\\n2307: }\\n2308: \\n2309: // getHPointSlice will return a HPoint slice of size max(maxPointsSliceSize, sz).\\n2310: // This function is called with an estimated size which often can be over-estimated.\\n2311: func getHPointSlice(sz int) []HPoint {\\n2312: \\tif p := hPointPool.Get(); p != nil {\\n2313: \\t\\treturn p\\n2314: \\t}\\n2315: \\n2316: \\tif sz > maxPointsSliceSize {\\n2317: \\t\\tsz = maxPointsSliceSize\\n2318: \\t}\\n2319: \\n2320: \\treturn make([]HPoint, 0, sz)\\n2321: }\\n2322: \\n2323: func putHPointSlice(p []HPoint) {\\n2324: \\tif p != nil {\\n2325: \\t\\thPointPool.Put(p[:0])\\n2326: \\t}\\n2327: }\\n2328: \\n2329: func getMatrixSelectorHPoints() []HPoint {\\n2330: \\tif p := matrixSelectorHPool.Get(); p != nil {\\n2331: \\t\\treturn p\\n2332: \\t}\\n2333: \\n2334: \\treturn make([]HPoint, 0, matrixSelectorSliceSize)\\n2335: }\\n2336: \\n2337: func putMatrixSelectorHPointSlice(p []HPoint) {\\n2338: \\tif p != nil {\\n2339: \\t\\tmatrixSelectorHPool.Put(p[:0])\\n2340: \\t}\\n2341: }\\n2342: \\n2343: // matrixSelector evaluates a *parser.MatrixSelector expression.\\n2344: func (ev *evaluator) matrixSelector(ctx context.Context, node *parser.MatrixSelector) (Matrix, annotations.Annotations) {\\n2345: \\tvar (\\n2346: \\t\\tvs = node.VectorSelector.(*parser.VectorSelector)\\n2347: \\n2348: \\t\\toffset = durationMilliseconds(vs.Offset)\\n2349: \\t\\tmaxt   = ev.startTimestamp - offset\\n2350: \\t\\tmint   = maxt - durationMilliseconds(node.Range)\\n2351: \\t\\tmatrix = make(Matrix, 0, len(vs.Series))\\n2352: \\n2353: \\t\\tit = storage.NewBuffer(durationMilliseconds(node.Range))\\n2354: \\t)\\n2355: \\tws, err := checkAndExpandSeriesSet(ctx, node)\\n2356: \\tif err != nil {\\n2357: \\t\\tev.error(errWithWarnings{fmt.Errorf(\\\"expanding series: %w\\\", err), ws})\\n2358: \\t}\\n2359: \\n2360: \\tvar chkIter chunkenc.Iterator\\n2361: \\tseries := vs.Series\\n2362: \\tfor i, s := range series {\\n2363: \\t\\tif err := contextDone(ctx, \\\"expression evaluation\\\"); err != nil {\\n2364: \\t\\t\\tev.error(err)\\n2365: \\t\\t}\\n2366: \\t\\tchkIter = s.Iterator(chkIter)\\n2367: \\t\\tit.Reset(chkIter)\\n2368: \\t\\tss := Series{\\n2369: \\t\\t\\tMetric: series[i].Labels(),\\n2370: \\t\\t}\\n2371: \\n2372: \\t\\tss.Floats, ss.Histograms = ev.matrixIterSlice(it, mint, maxt, nil, nil)\\n2373: \\t\\ttotalSize := int64(len(ss.Floats)) + int64(totalHPointSize(ss.Histograms))\\n2374: \\t\\tev.samplesStats.IncrementSamplesAtTimestamp(ev.startTimestamp, totalSize)\\n2375: \\n2376: \\t\\tif totalSize > 0 {\\n2377: \\t\\t\\tmatrix = append(matrix, ss)\\n2378: \\t\\t} else {\\n2379: \\t\\t\\tputFPointSlice(ss.Floats)\\n2380: \\t\\t\\tputHPointSlice(ss.Histograms)\\n2381: \\t\\t}\\n2382: \\t}\\n2383: \\treturn matrix, ws\\n2384: }\\n2385: \\n2386: // matrixIterSlice populates a matrix vector covering the requested range for a\\n2387: // single time series, with points retrieved from an iterator.\\n2388: //\\n2389: // As an optimization, the matrix vector may already contain points of the same\\n2390: // time series from the evaluation of an earlier step (with lower mint and maxt\\n2391: // values). Any such points falling before mint are discarded; points that fall\\n2392: // into the [mint, maxt] range are retained; only points with later timestamps\\n2393: // are populated from the iterator.\\n2394: func (ev *evaluator) matrixIterSlice(\\n2395: \\tit *storage.BufferedSeriesIterator, mint, maxt int64,\\n2396: \\tfloats []FPoint, histograms []HPoint,\\n2397: ) ([]FPoint, []HPoint) {\\n2398: \\tmintFloats, mintHistograms := mint, mint\\n2399: \\n2400: \\t// First floats...\\n2401: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4@ba16cf5a7437eb3b6b71b74557b7a67e1975ad0f/-/blob/promql/engine.go%3FL1601-2400\"}"

"{\"content\":\"2401: \\tif len(floats) > 0 && floats[len(floats)-1].T > mint {\\n2402: \\t\\t// There is an overlap between previous and current ranges, retain common\\n2403: \\t\\t// points. In most such cases:\\n2404: \\t\\t//   (a) the overlap is significantly larger than the eval step; and/or\\n2405: \\t\\t//   (b) the number of samples is relatively small.\\n2406: \\t\\t// so a linear search will be as fast as a binary search.\\n2407: \\t\\tvar drop int\\n2408: \\t\\tfor drop = 0; floats[drop].T <= mint; drop++ {\\n2409: \\t\\t}\\n2410: \\t\\tev.currentSamples -= drop\\n2411: \\t\\tcopy(floats, floats[drop:])\\n2412: \\t\\tfloats = floats[:len(floats)-drop]\\n2413: \\t\\t// Only append points with timestamps after the last timestamp we have.\\n2414: \\t\\tmintFloats = floats[len(floats)-1].T\\n2415: \\t} else {\\n2416: \\t\\tev.currentSamples -= len(floats)\\n2417: \\t\\tif floats != nil {\\n2418: \\t\\t\\tfloats = floats[:0]\\n2419: \\t\\t}\\n2420: \\t}\\n2421: \\n2422: \\t// ...then the same for histograms. search will be as fast as a binary search.\\n2429: \\t\\tvar drop int\\n2430: \\t\\tfor drop = 0; histograms[drop].T <= mint; drop++ {\\n2431: \\t\\t}\\n2432: \\t\\t// Rotate the buffer around the drop index so that points before mint can be\\n2433: \\t\\t// reused to store new histograms.\\n2434: \\t\\ttail := make([]HPoint, drop)\\n2435: \\t\\tcopy(tail, histograms[:drop])\\n2436: \\t\\tcopy(histograms, histograms[drop:])\\n2437: \\t\\tcopy(histograms[len(histograms)-drop:], tail)\\n2438: \\t\\thistograms = histograms[:len(histograms)-drop]\\n2439: \\t\\tev.currentSamples -= mint == maxt {\\n2450: \\t\\t// Empty range: return the empty slices.\\n2451: \\t\\treturn floats, histograms\\n2452: \\t}\\n2453: \\n2454: \\tsoughtValueType := it.Seek(maxt)\\n2455: \\tif (*evaluator) VectorAnd(lhs, rhs Vector, matching *parser.VectorMatching, lhsh, rhsh []EvalSeriesHelper, enh *EvalNodeHelper) Vector {\\n2556: \\tif matching.Card != parser.CardManyToMany {\\n2557: \\t\\tpanic(\\\"set operations must only use many-to-many matching\\\")\\n2558: \\t}\\n2559: \\tif len(lhs) == 0 || len(rhs) == 0 {\\n2560: \\t\\treturn nil // Short-circuit: AND with nothing is nothing.\\n2561: \\t}\\n2562: \\n2563: \\t// The set of signatures for the right-hand side Vector.\\n2564: \\trightSigs := map[string]struct{}{}\\n2565: \\t// Add all rhs samples to a map so we can easily find matches later.\\n2566: \\tfor _, sh := range rhsh {\\n2567: \\t\\trightSigs[sh.signature] = struct{}{}\\n2568: \\t}\\n2569: \\n2570: \\tfor i, ls := range lhs {\\n2571: \\t\\t// If there's a matching entry in the right-hand side Vector, add the sample.\\n2572: \\t\\tif _, ok := rightSigs[lhsh[i].signature]; ok {\\n2573: \\t\\t\\tenh.Out = append(enh.Out, ls)\\n2574: \\t\\t}\\n2575: \\t}\\n2576: \\treturn enh.Out\\n2577: }\\n2578: \\n2579: func (*evaluator) VectorOr(lhs, rhs Vector, matching *parser.VectorMatching, lhsh, rhsh []EvalSeriesHelper, enh *EvalNodeHelper) Vector {\\n2580: \\tswitch {\\n2581: \\tcase matching.Card != parser.CardManyToMany:\\n2582: \\t\\tpanic(\\\"set operations must only use many-to-many matching\\\")\\n2583: \\tcase len(lhs) == 0: // Short-circuit.\\n2584: \\t\\tenh.Out = append(enh.Out, rhs...)\\n2585: \\t\\treturn enh.Out\\n2586: \\tcase len(rhs) == 0:\\n2587: \\t\\tenh.Out = append(enh.Out, lhs...)\\n2588: \\t\\treturn enh.Out\\n2589: \\t}\\n2590: \\n2591: \\tleftSigs := map[string]struct{}{}\\n2592: \\t// Add everything from the left-hand-side Vector.\\n2593: \\tfor i, ls := range lhs {\\n2594: \\t\\tleftSigs[lhsh[i].signature] = struct{}{}\\n2595: \\t\\tenh.Out = append(enh.Out, ls)\\n2596: \\t}\\n2597: \\t// Add all right-hand side elements which have not been added from the left-hand side.\\n2598: \\tfor j, rs := range rhs {\\n2599: \\t\\tif _, ok := leftSigs[rhsh[j].signature]; !ok {\\n2600: \\t\\t\\tenh.Out = append(enh.Out, rs)\\n2601: \\t\\t}\\n2602: \\t}\\n2603: \\treturn enh.Out\\n2604: }\\n2605: \\n2606: func (*evaluator) VectorUnless(lhs, rhs Vector, matching *parser.VectorMatching, lhsh, rhsh []EvalSeriesHelper, enh *EvalNodeHelper) Vector {\\n2607: \\tif matching.Card != parser.CardManyToMany {\\n2608: \\t\\tpanic(\\\"set operations must only use many-to-many matching\\\")\\n2609: \\t}\\n2610: \\t// Short-circuit: empty rhs means we will return everything in lhs;\\n2611: \\t// empty lhs means we will return empty - don't need to build a map.\\n2612: \\tif len(lhs) == 0 || len(rhs) == 0 {\\n2613: \\t\\tenh.Out = append(enh.Out, lhs...)\\n2614: \\t\\treturn enh.Out\\n2615: \\t}\\n2616: \\n2617: \\trightSigs := map[string]struct{}{}\\n2618: \\tfor _, sh := range rhsh {\\n2619: \\t\\trightSigs[sh.signature] = struct{}{}\\n2620: \\t}\\n2621: \\n2622: \\tfor i, ls := range lhs {\\n2623: \\t\\tif _, ok := rightSigs[lhsh[i].signature]; !ok {\\n2624: \\t\\t\\tenh.Out = append(enh.Out, ls)\\n2625: \\t\\t}\\n2626: \\t}\\n2627: \\treturn enh.Out\\n2628: }\\n2629: \\n2630: // VectorBinop evaluates a binary operation between two Vectors, excluding set operators.\\n2631: func (ev *evaluator) VectorBinop(op parser.ItemType, lhs, rhs Vector, matching *parser.VectorMatching, returnBool bool, lhsh, rhsh []EvalSeriesHelper, enh *EvalNodeHelper, pos posrange.PositionRange) (Vector, error) {\\n2632: \\tif matching.Card == parser.CardManyToMany {\\n2633: \\t\\tpanic(\\\"many-to-many only allowed for set operators\\\")\\n2634: \\t}\\n2635: \\tif len(lhs) == 0 || len(rhs) == 0 {\\n2636: \\t\\treturn nil, nil // Short-circuit: nothing is going to match.\\n2637: \\t}\\n2638: \\n2639: \\t// The control flow below handles one-to-one or many-to-one matching.\\n2640: \\t// For one-to-many, swap sidedness and account for the swap when calculating\\n2641: \\t// values.\\n2642: \\tif matching.Card == parser.CardOneToMany {\\n2643: \\t\\tlhs, rhs = rhs, lhs\\n2644: \\t\\tlhsh, rhsh = rhsh, lhsh\\n2645: \\t}\\n2646: \\n2647: \\t// All samples from the rhs hashed by the matching label/values.\\n2648: \\tif enh.rightSigs == nil {\\n2649: \\t\\tenh.rightSigs = make(map[string]Sample, len(enh.Out))\\n2650: \\t} else {\\n2651: \\t\\tfor k := range enh.rightSigs {\\n2652: \\t\\t\\tdelete(enh.rightSigs, k)\\n2653: \\t\\t}\\n2654: \\t}\\n2655: \\trightSigs := enh.rightSigs\\n2656: \\n2657: \\t// Add all rhs samples to a map so we can easily find matches later.\\n2658: \\tfor i, rs := range rhs {\\n2659: \\t\\tsig := rhsh[i].signature\\n2660: \\t\\t// The rhs is guaranteed to be the 'one' side. Having multiple samples\\n2661: \\t\\t// with the same signature means that the matching is many-to-many.\\n2662: \\t\\tif duplSample, found := rightSigs[sig]; found {\\n2663: \\t\\t\\t// oneSide represents which side of the vector represents the 'one' in the many-to-one relationship.\\n2664: \\t\\t\\toneSide := \\\"right\\\"\\n2665: \\t\\t\\tif matching.Card == parser.CardOneToMany {\\n2666: \\t\\t\\t\\toneSide = \\\"left\\\"\\n2667: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2668: \\t\\t\\tmatchedLabels := rs.Metric.MatchLabels(matching.On, matching.MatchingLabels...)\\n2669: \\t\\t\\t// Many-to-many matching not allowed.\\n2670: \\t\\t\\tev.errorf(\\\"found duplicate series for the match group %s on the %s hand-side of the operation: [%s, %s]\\\"+\\n2671: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\";many-to-many matching not allowed: matching labels must be unique on one side\\\", matchedLabels.String(), oneSide, rs.Metric.String(), duplSample.Metric.String())\\n2672: \\t\\t}\\n2673: \\t\\trightSigs[sig] = rs\\n2674: \\t}\\n2675: \\n2676: \\t// Tracks the match-signature. For one-to-one operations the value is nil. For many-to-one\\n2677: \\t// the value is a set of signatures to detect duplicated result elements.\\n2678: \\tif enh.matchedSigs == nil {\\n2679: \\t\\tenh.matchedSigs = make(map[string]map[uint64]struct{}, len(rightSigs))\\n2680: \\t} else {\\n2681: \\t\\tfor k := range enh.matchedSigs {\\n2682: \\t\\t\\tdelete(enh.matchedSigs, k)\\n2683: \\t\\t}\\n2684: \\t}\\n2685: \\tmatchedSigs := enh.matchedSigs\\n2686: \\n2687: \\t// For all lhs samples find a respective rhs sample and perform\\n2688: \\t// the binary operation.\\n2689: \\tvar lastErr error\\n2690: \\tfor i, ls := range lhs {\\n2691: \\t\\tsig := lhsh[i].signature\\n2692: \\n2693: \\t\\trs, found := rightSigs[sig] // Look for a match in the rhs Vector.\\n2694: \\t\\tif !found {\\n2695: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n2696: \\t\\t}\\n2697: \\n2698: \\t\\t// Account for potentially swapped sidedness.\\n2699: \\t\\tfl, fr := ls.F, rs.F\\n2700: \\t\\thl, hr := ls.H, rs.H\\n2701: \\t\\tif matching.Card == parser.CardOneToMany {\\n2702: \\t\\t\\tfl, fr = fr, fl\\n2703: \\t\\t\\thl, hr = hr, hl\\n2704: \\t\\t}\\n2705: \\t\\tfloatValue, histogramValue, keep, err := vectorElemBinop(op, fl, fr, hl, hr, pos)\\n2706: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n2707: \\t\\t\\tlastErr = err\\n2708: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n2709: \\t\\t}\\n2710: \\t\\tswitch {\\n2711: \\t\\tcase returnBool:\\n2712: \\t\\t\\thistogramValue = nil\\n2713: \\t\\t\\tif keep {\\n2714: \\t\\t\\t\\tfloatValue = 1.0\\n2715: \\t\\t\\t} else {\\n2716: \\t\\t\\t\\tfloatValue = 0.0\\n2717: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2718: \\t\\tcase !keep:\\n2719: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n2720: \\t\\t}\\n2721: \\t\\tmetric := resultMetric(ls.Metric, rs.Metric, op, matching, enh)\\n2722: \\t\\tif !ev.enableDelayedNameRemoval && returnBool {\\n2723: \\t\\t\\tmetric = metric.DropReserved(schema.IsMetadataLabel)\\n2724: \\t\\t}\\n2725: \\t\\tinsertedSigs, exists := matchedSigs[sig]\\n2726: \\t\\tif matching.Card == parser.CardOneToOne {\\n2727: \\t\\t\\tif exists {\\n2728: \\t\\t\\t\\tev.errorf(\\\"multiple matches for labels: many-to-one matching must be explicit (group_left/group_right)\\\")\\n2729: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2730: \\t\\t\\tmatchedSigs[sig] = nil // Set existence to true.\\n2731: \\t\\t} else {\\n2732: \\t\\t\\t// In many-to-one matching the grouping labels have to ensure a unique metric\\n2733: \\t\\t\\t// for the result Vector. Check whether those labels have already been added for\\n2734: \\t\\t\\t// the same matching labels.\\n2735: \\t\\t\\tinsertSig := metric.Hash()\\n2736: \\n2737: \\t\\t\\tif !exists {\\n2738: \\t\\t\\t\\tinsertedSigs = map[uint64]struct{}{}\\n2739: \\t\\t\\t\\tmatchedSigs[sig] = insertedSigs\\n2740: \\t\\t\\t} else if _, duplicate := insertedSigs[insertSig]; duplicate {\\n2741: \\t\\t\\t\\tev.errorf(\\\"multiple matches for labels: grouping labels must ensure unique matches\\\")\\n2742: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2743: \\t\\t\\tinsertedSigs[insertSig] = struct{}{}\\n2744: \\t\\t}\\n2745: \\n2746: \\t\\tenh.Out = append(enh.Out, Sample{\\n2747: \\t\\t\\tMetric:   metric,\\n2748: \\t\\t\\tF:        floatValue,\\n2749: \\t\\t\\tH:        histogramValue,\\n2750: \\t\\t\\tDropName: returnBool,\\n2751: \\t\\t})\\n2752: \\t}\\n2753: \\treturn enh.Out, lastErr\\n2754: }\\n2755: \\n2756: func signatureFunc(on bool, b []byte, names ...string) func(labels.Labels) string {\\n2757: \\tif on {\\n2758: \\t\\tslices.Sort(names)\\n2759: \\t\\treturn func(lset labels.Labels) string {\\n2760: \\t\\t\\treturn string(lset.BytesWithLabels(b, names...))\\n2761: \\t\\t}\\n2762: \\t}\\n2763: \\tnames = append([]string{labels.MetricName}, names...)\\n2764: \\tslices.Sort(names)\\n2765: \\treturn func(lset labels.Labels) string {\\n2766: \\t\\treturn string(lset.BytesWithoutLabels(b, names...))\\n2767: \\t}\\n2768: }\\n2769: \\n2770: // resultMetric returns the metric for the given sample(s) based on the Vector\\n2771: // binary operation and the matching options.\\n2772: func resultMetric(lhs, rhs labels.Labels, op parser.ItemType, matching *parser.VectorMatching, enh *EvalNodeHelper) labels.Labels {\\n2773: \\tif enh.resultMetric == nil {\\n2774: \\t\\tenh.resultMetric = make(map[string]labels.Labels, len(enh.Out))\\n2775: \\t}\\n2776: \\n2777: \\tenh.resetBuilder(lhs)\\n2778: \\tbuf := bytes.NewBuffer(enh.lblResultBuf[:0])\\n2779: \\tenh.lblBuf = lhs.Bytes(enh.lblBuf)\\n2780: \\tbuf.Write(enh.lblBuf)\\n2781: \\tenh.lblBuf = rhs.Bytes(enh.lblBuf)\\n2782: \\tbuf.Write(enh.lblBuf)\\n2783: \\tenh.lblResultBuf = buf.Bytes()\\n2784: \\n2785: \\tif ret, ok := enh.resultMetric[string(enh.lblResultBuf)]; ok {\\n2786: \\t\\treturn ret\\n2787: \\t}\\n2788: \\tstr := string(enh.lblResultBuf)\\n2789: \\n2790: \\tif changesMetricSchema(op) {\\n2791: \\t\\t// Setting empty Metadata causes the deletion of those if they exists.\\n2792: \\t\\tschema.Metadata{}.SetToLabels(enh.lb)\\n2793: \\t}\\n2794: \\n2795: \\tif matching.Card == parser.CardOneToOne {\\n2796: \\t\\tif matching.On {\\n2797: \\t\\t\\tenh.lb.Keep(matching.MatchingLabels...)\\n2798: \\t\\t} else {\\n2799: \\t\\t\\tenh.lb.Del(matching.MatchingLabels...)\\n2800: \\t\\t}\\n2801: \\t}\\n2802: \\tfor _, ln := range matching.Include {\\n2803: \\t\\t// Included labels from the `group_x` modifier are taken from the \\\"one\\\"-side.\\n2804: \\t\\tif v := rhs.Get(ln); v != \\\"\\\" {\\n2805: \\t\\t\\tenh.lb.Set(ln, v)\\n2806: \\t\\t} else {\\n2807: \\t\\t\\tenh.lb.Del(ln)\\n2808: \\t\\t}\\n2809: \\t}\\n2810: \\n2811: \\tret := enh.lb.Labels()\\n2812: \\tenh.resultMetric[str] = ret\\n2813: \\treturn ret\\n2814: }\\n2815: \\n2816: // VectorscalarBinop evaluates a binary operation between a Vector and a Scalar.\\n2817: func (ev *evaluator) VectorscalarBinop(op parser.ItemType, lhs Vector, rhs Scalar, swap, returnBool bool, enh *EvalNodeHelper, pos posrange.PositionRange) (Vector, error) {\\n2818: \\tvar lastErr error\\n2819: \\tfor _, lhsSample := range lhs {\\n2820: \\t\\tlf, rf := lhsSample.F, rhs.V\\n2821: \\t\\tvar rh *histogram.FloatHistogram\\n2822: \\t\\tlh := lhsSample.H\\n2823: \\t\\t// lhs always contains the Vector. If the original position was different\\n2824: \\t\\t// swap for calculating the value.\\n2825: \\t\\tif swap {\\n2826: \\t\\t\\tlf, rf = rf, lf\\n2827: \\t\\t\\tlh, rh = rh, lh\\n2828: \\t\\t}\\n2829: \\t\\tfloat, histogram, keep, err := vectorElemBinop(op, lf, rf, lh, rh, pos)\\n2830: \\t\\tif err != nil && !errors.Is(err, annotations.PromQLWarning) {\\n2831: \\t\\t\\tlastErr = err\\n2832: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n2833: \\t\\t}\\n2834: \\t\\t// Catch cases where the scalar is the LHS in a scalar-vector comparison operation.\\n2835: \\t\\t// We want to always keep the vector element value as the output value, even if it's on the RHS.\\n2836: \\t\\tif op.IsComparisonOperator() && swap {\\n2837: \\t\\t\\tfloat = rf\\n2838: \\t\\t\\thistogram = rh\\n2839: \\t\\t}\\n2840: \\t\\tif returnBool {\\n2841: \\t\\t\\tif keep {\\n2842: \\t\\t\\t\\tfloat = 1.0\\n2843: \\t\\t\\t} else {\\n2844: \\t\\t\\t\\tfloat = 0.0\\n2845: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2846: \\t\\t\\tkeep = true\\n2847: \\t\\t}\\n2848: \\t\\tif keep {\\n2849: \\t\\t\\tlhsSample.F = float\\n2850: \\t\\t\\tlhsSample.H = histogram\\n2851: \\t\\t\\tif changesMetricSchema(op) || returnBool {\\n2852: \\t\\t\\t\\tif !ev.enableDelayedNameRemoval {\\n2853: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tlhsSample.Metric = lhsSample.Metric.DropReserved(schema.IsMetadataLabel)\\n2854: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n2855: \\t\\t\\t\\tlhsSample.DropName = true\\n2856: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2857: \\t\\t\\tenh.Out = append(enh.Out, lhsSample)\\n2858: \\t\\t}\\n2859: \\t}\\n2860: \\treturn enh.Out, lastErr\\n2861: }\\n2862: \\n2863: // scalarBinop evaluates a binary operation between two Scalars.\\n2864: func scalarBinop(op parser.ItemType, lhs, rhs float64) float64 {\\n2865: \\tswitch op {\\n2866: \\tcase parser.ADD:\\n2867: \\t\\treturn lhs + rhs\\n2868: \\tcase parser.SUB:\\n2869: \\t\\treturn lhs - rhs\\n2870: \\tcase parser.MUL:\\n2871: \\t\\treturn lhs * rhs\\n2872: \\tcase parser.DIV:\\n2873: \\t\\treturn lhs / rhs\\n2874: \\tcase parser.POW:\\n2875: \\t\\treturn math.Pow(lhs, rhs)\\n2876: \\tcase parser.MOD:\\n2877: \\t\\treturn math.Mod(lhs, rhs)\\n2878: \\tcase parser.EQLC:\\n2879: \\t\\treturn btos(lhs == rhs)\\n2880: \\tcase parser.NEQ:\\n2881: \\t\\treturn btos(lhs != rhs)\\n2882: \\tcase parser.GTR:\\n2883: \\t\\treturn btos(lhs > rhs)\\n2884: \\tcase parser.LSS:\\n2885: \\t\\treturn btos(lhs < rhs)\\n2886: \\tcase parser.GTE:\\n2887: \\t\\treturn btos(lhs >= rhs)\\n2888: \\tcase parser.LTE:\\n2889: \\t\\treturn btos(lhs <= rhs)\\n2890: \\tcase parser.ATAN2:\\n2891: \\t\\treturn math.Atan2(lhs, rhs)\\n2892: \\t}\\n2893: \\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\\\"operator %q not allowed for Scalar operations\\\", op))\\n2894: }\\n2895: \\n2896: // vectorElemBinop evaluates a binary operation between two Vector elements.\\n2897: func vectorElemBinop(op parser.ItemType, lhs, rhs float64, hlhs, hrhs *histogram.FloatHistogram, pos posrange.PositionRange) (float64, *histogram.FloatHistogram, bool, error) {\\n2898: \\topName := parser.ItemTypeStr[op]\\n2899: \\tswitch {\\n2900: \\tcase hlhs == nil && hrhs == nil:\\n2901: \\t\\t{\\n2902: \\t\\t\\tswitch op {\\n2903: \\t\\t\\tcase parser.ADD:\\n2904: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn lhs + rhs, nil, true, nil\\n2905: \\t\\t\\tcase parser.SUB:\\n2906: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn lhs - rhs, nil, true, nil\\n2907: \\t\\t\\tcase parser.MUL:\\n2908: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn lhs * rhs, nil, true, nil\\n2909: \\t\\t\\tcase parser.DIV:\\n2910: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn lhs / rhs, nil, true, nil\\n2911: \\t\\t\\tcase parser.POW:\\n2912: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn math.Pow(lhs, rhs), nil, true, nil\\n2913: \\t\\t\\tcase parser.MOD:\\n2914: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn math.Mod(lhs, rhs), nil, true, nil\\n2915: \\t\\t\\tcase parser.EQLC:\\n2916: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn lhs, nil, lhs == rhs, nil\\n2917: \\t\\t\\tcase parser.NEQ:\\n2918: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn lhs, nil, lhs != rhs, nil\\n2919: \\t\\t\\tcase parser.GTR:\\n2920: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn lhs, nil, lhs > rhs, nil\\n2921: \\t\\t\\tcase parser.LSS:\\n2922: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn lhs, nil, lhs < rhs, nil\\n2923: \\t\\t\\tcase parser.GTE:\\n2924: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn lhs, nil, lhs >= rhs, nil\\n2925: \\t\\t\\tcase parser.LTE:\\n2926: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn lhs, nil, lhs <= rhs, nil\\n2927: \\t\\t\\tcase parser.ATAN2:\\n2928: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn math.Atan2(lhs, rhs), nil, true, nil\\n2929: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2930: \\t\\t}\\n2931: \\tcase hlhs == nil && hrhs != nil:\\n2932: \\t\\t{\\n2933: \\t\\t\\tswitch op {\\n2934: \\t\\t\\tcase parser.MUL:\\n2935: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn 0, hrhs.Copy().Mul(lhs).Compact(0), true, nil\\n2936: \\t\\t\\tcase parser.ADD, parser.SUB, parser.DIV, parser.POW, parser.MOD, parser.EQLC, parser.NEQ, parser.GTR, parser.LSS, parser.GTE, parser.LTE, parser.ATAN2:\\n2937: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn 0, nil, false, annotations.NewIncompatibleTypesInBinOpInfo(\\\"float\\\", opName, \\\"histogram\\\", pos)\\n2938: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2939: \\t\\t}\\n2940: \\tcase hlhs != nil && hrhs == nil:\\n2941: \\t\\t{\\n2942: \\t\\t\\tswitch op {\\n2943: \\t\\t\\tcase parser.MUL:\\n2944: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn 0, hlhs.Copy().Mul(rhs).Compact(0), true, nil\\n2945: \\t\\t\\tcase parser.DIV:\\n2946: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn 0, hlhs.Copy().Div(rhs).Compact(0), true, nil\\n2947: \\t\\t\\tcase parser.ADD, parser.SUB, parser.POW, parser.MOD, parser.EQLC, parser.NEQ, parser.GTR, parser.LSS, parser.GTE, parser.LTE, parser.ATAN2:\\n2948: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn 0, nil, false, annotations.NewIncompatibleTypesInBinOpInfo(\\\"histogram\\\", opName, \\\"float\\\", pos)\\n2949: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2950: \\t\\t}\\n2951: \\tcase hlhs != nil && hrhs != nil:\\n2952: \\t\\t{\\n2953: \\t\\t\\tswitch op {\\n2954: \\t\\t\\tcase parser.ADD:\\n2955: \\t\\t\\t\\tres, counterResetCollision, err := hlhs.Copy().Add(hrhs)\\n2956: \\t\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n2957: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn 0, nil, false, err\\n2958: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n2959: \\t\\t\\t\\tif counterResetCollision {\\n2960: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\terr = annotations.NewHistogramCounterResetCollisionWarning(pos, annotations.HistogramAdd)\\n2961: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n2962: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn 0, res.Compact(0), true, err\\n2963: \\t\\t\\tcase parser.SUB:\\n2964: \\t\\t\\t\\tres, counterResetCollision, err := hlhs.Copy().Sub(hrhs)\\n2965: \\t\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n2966: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn 0, nil, false, err\\n2967: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n2968: \\t\\t\\t\\tif counterResetCollision {\\n2969: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\terr = annotations.NewHistogramCounterResetCollisionWarning(pos, annotations.HistogramSub)\\n2970: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n2971: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn 0, res.Compact(0), true, err\\n2972: \\t\\t\\tcase parser.EQLC:\\n2973: \\t\\t\\t\\t// This operation expects that both histograms are compacted.\\n2974: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn 0, hlhs, hlhs.Equals(hrhs), nil\\n2975: \\t\\t\\tcase parser.NEQ:\\n2976: \\t\\t\\t\\t// This operation expects that both histograms are compacted.\\n2977: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn 0, hlhs, !hlhs.Equals(hrhs), nil\\n2978: \\t\\t\\tcase parser.MUL, parser.DIV, parser.POW, parser.MOD, parser.GTR, parser.LSS, parser.GTE, parser.LTE, parser.ATAN2:\\n2979: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn 0, nil, false, annotations.NewIncompatibleTypesInBinOpInfo(\\\"histogram\\\", opName, \\\"histogram\\\", pos)\\n2980: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2981: \\t\\t}\\n2982: \\t}\\n2983: \\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\\\"operator %q not allowed for operations between Vectors\\\", op))\\n2984: }\\n2985: \\n2986: type groupedAggregation struct {\\n2987: \\tfloatValue     float64\\n2988: \\thistogramValue *histogram.FloatHistogram\\n2989: \\tfloatMean      float64\\n2990: \\tfloatKahanC    float64 // \\\"Compensating value\\\" for Kahan summation.\\n2991: \\tgroupCount     float64\\n2992: \\theap           vectorByValueHeap\\n2993: \\n2994: \\t// All bools together for better packing within the struct.\\n2995: \\tseen                   bool // Was this output groups seen in the input at this timestamp.\\n2996: \\thasFloat               bool // Has at least 1 float64 sample aggregated.\\n2997: \\thasHistogram           bool // Has at least 1 histogram sample aggregated.\\n2998: \\tincompatibleHistograms bool // If true, group has seen mixed exponential and custom buckets, or incompatible custom buckets.\\n2999: \\tgroupAggrComplete      bool // Used by LIMITK to short-cut series loop when we've reached K elem on every group.\\n3000: \\tincrementalMean        bool // True after reverting to incremental calculation of the mean value.\\n3001: }\\n3002: \\n3003: // aggregation evaluates sum, avg, count, stdvar, stddev or quantile at one timestep on inputMatrix.\\n3004: // These functions produce one output series for each group specified in the expression, with just the labels from `by(...)`.\\n3005: // outputMatrix should be already populated with grouping labels; groups is one-to-one with outputMatrix.\\n3006: // seriesToResult maps inputMatrix indexes to outputMatrix indexes.\\n3007: func (ev *evaluator) aggregation(e *parser.AggregateExpr, q float64, inputMatrix, outputMatrix Matrix, seriesToResult []int, groups []groupedAggregation, enh *EvalNodeHelper) annotations.Annotations {\\n3008: \\top := e.Op\\n3009: \\tvar annos annotations.Annotations\\n3010: \\tfor i := range groups {\\n3011: \\t\\tgroups[i].seen = false\\n3012: \\t}\\n3013: \\n3014: \\tfor si := range inputMatrix {\\n3015: \\t\\tf, h, ok := ev.nextValues(enh.Ts, &inputMatrix[si])\\n3016: \\t\\tif !ok {\\n3017: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n3018: \\t\\t}\\n3019: \\n3020: \\t\\tgroup := &groups[seriesToResult[si]]\\n3021: \\t\\t// Initialize this group if it's the first time we've seen it.\\n3022: \\t\\tif !group.seen {\\n3023: \\t\\t\\t*group = groupedAggregation{\\n3024: \\t\\t\\t\\tseen:                   true,\\n3025: \\t\\t\\t\\tfloatValue:             f,\\n3026: \\t\\t\\t\\tfloatMean:              f,\\n3027: \\t\\t\\t\\tincompatibleHistograms: false,\\n3028: \\t\\t\\t\\tgroupCount:             1,\\n3029: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3030: \\t\\t\\tswitch op {\\n3031: \\t\\t\\tcase parser.AVG, parser.SUM:\\n3032: \\t\\t\\t\\tif h == nil {\\n3033: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tgroup.hasFloat = true\\n3034: \\t\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n3035: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tgroup.histogramValue = h.Copy()\\n3036: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tgroup.hasHistogram = true\\n3037: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n3038: \\t\\t\\tcase parser.STDVAR, parser.STDDEV:\\n3039: \\t\\t\\t\\tswitch {\\n3040: \\t\\t\\t\\tcase h != nil:\\n3041: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// Ignore histograms for STDVAR and STDDEV.\\n3042: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tgroup.seen = false\\n3043: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif op == parser.STDVAR {\\n3044: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\\\"stdvar\\\", e.Expr.PositionRange()))\\n3045: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n3046: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\\\"stddev\\\", e.Expr.PositionRange()))\\n3047: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n3048: \\t\\t\\t\\tcase math.IsNaN(f), math.IsInf(f, 0):\\n3049: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tgroup.floatValue = math.NaN()\\n3050: \\t\\t\\t\\tdefault:\\n3051: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tgroup.floatValue = 0\\n3052: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n3053: \\t\\t\\tcase parser.QUANTILE:\\n3054: \\t\\t\\t\\tif h != nil {\\n3055: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tgroup.seen = false\\n3056: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\\\"quantile\\\", e.Expr.PositionRange()))\\n3057: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n3058: \\t\\t\\t\\tgroup.heap = make(vectorByValueHeap, 1)\\n3059: \\t\\t\\t\\tgroup.heap[0] = Sample{F: f}\\n3060: \\t\\t\\tcase parser.GROUP:\\n3061: \\t\\t\\t\\tgroup.floatValue = 1\\n3062: \\t\\t\\tcase parser.MIN, parser.MAX:\\n3063: \\t\\t\\t\\tif h != nil {\\n3064: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tgroup.seen = false\\n3065: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif op == parser.MIN {\\n3066: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\\\"min\\\", e.Expr.PositionRange()))\\n3067: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n3068: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\\\"max\\\", e.Expr.PositionRange()))\\n3069: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n3070: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n3071: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3072: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n3073: \\t\\t}\\n3074: \\n3075: \\t\\tif group.incompatibleHistograms {\\n3076: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n3077: \\t\\t}\\n3078: \\n3079: \\t\\tswitch op {\\n3080: \\t\\tcase parser.SUM:\\n3081: \\t\\t\\tif h != nil {\\n3082: \\t\\t\\t\\tgroup.hasHistogram = true\\n3083: \\t\\t\\t\\tif group.histogramValue != nil {\\n3084: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t_, _, err := group.histogramValue.Add(h)\\n3085: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n3086: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\thandleAggregationError(err, e, inputMatrix[si].Metric.Get(model.MetricNameLabel), &annos)\\n3087: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tgroup.incompatibleHistograms = true\\n3088: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n3089: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n3090: \\t\\t\\t\\t// Otherwise the aggregation contained floats\\n3091: \\t\\t\\t\\t// previously and will be invalid anyway. No\\n3092: \\t\\t\\t\\t// point in copying the histogram in that case.\\n3093: \\t\\t\\t} else {\\n3094: \\t\\t\\t\\tgroup.hasFloat = true\\n3095: \\t\\t\\t\\tgroup.floatValue, group.floatKahanC = kahanSumInc(f, group.floatValue, group.floatKahanC)\\n3096: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3097: \\n3098: \\t\\tcase parser.AVG:\\n3099: \\t\\t\\t// For the average calculation of histograms, we use\\n3100: \\t\\t\\t// incremental mean calculation without the help of\\n3101: \\t\\t\\t// Kahan summation (but this should change, see\\n3102: \\t\\t\\t// https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/issues/14105\\n3103: \\t\\t\\t// ). For floats, we improve the accuracy with the help\\n3104: \\t\\t\\t// of Kahan summation. For a while, we assumed that\\n3105: \\t\\t\\t// incremental mean calculation combined with Kahan\\n3106: \\t\\t\\t// summation (see\\n3107: \\t\\t\\t// https://stackoverflow.com/questions/61665473/is-it-beneficial-for-precision-to-calculate-the-incremental-mean-average\\n3108: \\t\\t\\t// for inspiration) is generally the preferred solution.\\n3109: \\t\\t\\t// However, it then turned out that direct mean\\n3110: \\t\\t\\t// calculation (still in combination with Kahan\\n3111: \\t\\t\\t// summation) is often more accurate. See discussion in\\n3112: \\t\\t\\t// https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/issues/16714\\n3113: \\t\\t\\t// . The problem with the direct mean calculation is\\n3114: \\t\\t\\t// that it can overflow float64 for inputs on which the\\n3115: \\t\\t\\t// incremental mean calculation works just fine. Our\\n3116: \\t\\t\\t// current approach is therefore to use direct mean\\n3117: \\t\\t\\t// calculation as long as we do not overflow (or\\n3118: \\t\\t\\t// underflow) the running sum. Once the latter would\\n3119: \\t\\t\\t// happen, we switch to incremental mean calculation.\\n3120: \\t\\t\\t// This seems to work reasonably well, but note that a\\n3121: \\t\\t\\t// deeper understanding would be needed to find out if\\n3122: \\t\\t\\t// maybe an earlier switch to incremental mean\\n3123: \\t\\t\\t// calculation would be better in terms of accuracy.\\n3124: \\t\\t\\t// Also, we could apply a number of additional means to\\n3125: \\t\\t\\t// improve the accuracy, like processing the values in a\\n3126: \\t\\t\\t// particular order. For now, we decided that the\\n3127: \\t\\t\\t// current implementation is accurate enough for\\n3128: \\t\\t\\t// practical purposes, in particular given that changing\\n3129: \\t\\t\\t// the order of summation would be hard, given how the\\n3130: \\t\\t\\t// PromQL engine implements aggregations.\\n3131: \\t\\t\\tgroup.groupCount++\\n3132: \\t\\t\\tif h != nil {\\n3133: \\t\\t\\t\\tgroup.hasHistogram = true\\n3134: \\t\\t\\t\\tif group.histogramValue != nil {\\n3135: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tleft := h.Copy().Div(group.groupCount)\\n3136: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tright := group.histogramValue.Copy().Div(group.groupCount)\\n3137: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\ttoAdd, _, err := left.Sub(right)\\n3138: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n3139: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\thandleAggregationError(err, e, inputMatrix[si].Metric.Get(model.MetricNameLabel), &annos)\\n3140: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tgroup.incompatibleHistograms = true\\n3141: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n3142: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n3143: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t_, _, err = group.histogramValue.Add(toAdd)\\n3144: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n3145: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\thandleAggregationError(err, e, inputMatrix[si].Metric.Get(model.MetricNameLabel), &annos)\\n3146: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tgroup.incompatibleHistograms = true\\n3147: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n3148: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n3149: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n3150: \\t\\t\\t\\t// Otherwise the aggregation contained floats\\n3151: \\t\\t\\t\\t// previously and will be invalid anyway. No\\n3152: \\t\\t\\t\\t// point in copying the histogram in that case.\\n3153: \\t\\t\\t} else {\\n3154: \\t\\t\\t\\tgroup.hasFloat = true\\n3155: \\t\\t\\t\\tif !group.incrementalMean {\\n3156: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tnewV, newC := kahanSumInc(f, group.floatValue, group.floatKahanC)\\n3157: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif !math.IsInf(newV, 0) {\\n3158: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// The sum doesn't overflow, so we propagate it to the\\n3159: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// group struct and continue with the regular\\n3160: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// calculation of the mean value.\\n3161: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tgroup.floatValue, group.floatKahanC = newV, newC\\n3162: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tbreak\\n3163: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n3164: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// If we are here, we know that the sum _would_ overflow. So\\n3165: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// instead of continue to sum up, we revert to incremental\\n3166: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// calculation of the mean value from here on.\\n3167: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tgroup.incrementalMean = true\\n3168: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tgroup.floatMean = group.floatValue / (group.groupCount - 1)\\n3169: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tgroup.floatKahanC /= group.groupCount - 1\\n3170: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n3171: \\t\\t\\t\\tq := (group.groupCount - 1) / group.groupCount\\n3172: \\t\\t\\t\\tgroup.floatMean, group.floatKahanC = kahanSumInc(\\n3173: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tf/group.groupCount,\\n3174: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tq*group.floatMean,\\n3175: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tq*group.floatKahanC,\\n3176: \\t\\t\\t\\t)\\n3177: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3178: \\n3179: \\t\\tcase parser.GROUP:\\n3180: \\t\\t\\t// Do nothing. Required to avoid the panic in `default:` below.\\n3181: \\n3182: \\t\\tcase parser.MAX:\\n3183: \\t\\t\\tif h != nil {\\n3184: \\t\\t\\t\\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\\\"max\\\", e.Expr.PositionRange()))\\n3185: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n3186: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3187: \\t\\t\\tif group.floatValue < f || math.IsNaN(group.floatValue) {\\n3188: \\t\\t\\t\\tgroup.floatValue = f\\n3189: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3190: \\n3191: \\t\\tcase parser.MIN:\\n3192: \\t\\t\\tif h != nil {\\n3193: \\t\\t\\t\\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\\\"min\\\", e.Expr.PositionRange()))\\n3194: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n3195: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3196: \\t\\t\\tif group.floatValue > f || math.IsNaN(group.floatValue) {\\n3197: \\t\\t\\t\\tgroup.floatValue = f\\n3198: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3199: \\n3200: \\t\\tcase parser.COUNT:\\n3201: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4@ba16cf5a7437eb3b6b71b74557b7a67e1975ad0f/-/blob/promql/engine.go%3FL2401-3200\"}"
