\\t\\t\\t\\tgroup.groupCount++\\n3206: \\t\\t\\t\\tdelta := f - group.floatMean\\n3207: \\t\\t\\t\\tgroup.floatMean += delta / group.groupCount\\n3208: \\t\\t\\t\\tgroup.floatValue += delta * (f - group.floatMean)\\n3209: \\t\\t\\t} else {\\n3210: \\t\\t\\t\\tif op == parser.STDVAR {\\n3211: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\\\"stdvar\\\", e.Expr.PositionRange()))\\n3212: \\t\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n3213: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\\\"stddev\\\", e.Expr.PositionRange()))\\n3214: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n3215: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3216: \\n3217: \\t\\tcase parser.QUANTILE:\\n3218: \\t\\t\\tif h != nil {\\n3219: \\t\\t\\t\\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\\\"quantile\\\", e.Expr.PositionRange()))\\n3220: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n3221: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3222: \\t\\t\\tgroup.heap = append(group.heap, Sample{F: f})\\n3223: \\n3224: \\t\\tdefault:\\n3225: \\t\\t\\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\\\"expected aggregation operator but got %q\\\", op))\\n3226: \\t\\t}\\n3227: \\t}\\n3228: \\n3229: \\t// Construct the output matrix from the aggregated groups.\\n3230: \\tnumSteps := int((ev.endTimestamp-ev.startTimestamp)/ev.interval) + 1\\n3231: \\n3232: \\tfor ri, aggr := range groups {\\n3233: \\t\\tif !aggr.seen {\\n3234: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n3235: \\t\\t}\\n3236: \\t\\tswitch op {\\n3237: \\t\\tcase parser.AVG:\\n3238: \\t\\t\\tif aggr.hasFloat && aggr.hasHistogram {\\n3239: \\t\\t\\t\\t// We cannot aggregate histogram sample with a float64 sample.\\n3240: \\t\\t\\t\\tannos.Add(annotations.NewMixedFloatsHistogramsAggWarning(e.Expr.PositionRange()))\\n3241: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n3242: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3243: \\t\\t\\tswitch {\\n3244: \\t\\t\\tcase aggr.incompatibleHistograms:\\n3245: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n3246: \\t\\t\\tcase aggr.hasHistogram:\\n3247: \\t\\t\\t\\taggr.histogramValue = aggr.histogramValue.Compact(0)\\n3248: \\t\\t\\tcase aggr.incrementalMean:\\n3249: \\t\\t\\t\\taggr.floatValue = aggr.floatMean + aggr.floatKahanC\\n3250: \\t\\t\\tdefault:\\n3251: \\t\\t\\t\\taggr.floatValue = aggr.floatValue/aggr.groupCount + aggr.floatKahanC/aggr.groupCount\\n3252: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3253: \\n3254: \\t\\tcase parser.COUNT:\\n3255: \\t\\t\\taggr.floatValue = aggr.groupCount\\n3256: \\n3257: \\t\\tcase parser.STDVAR:\\n3258: \\t\\t\\taggr.floatValue /= aggr.groupCount\\n3259: \\n3260: \\t\\tcase parser.STDDEV:\\n3261: \\t\\t\\taggr.floatValue = math.Sqrt(aggr.floatValue / aggr.groupCount)\\n3262: \\n3263: \\t\\tcase parser.QUANTILE:\\n3264: \\t\\t\\taggr.floatValue = quantile(q, aggr.heap)\\n3265: \\n3266: \\t\\tcase parser.SUM:\\n3267: \\t\\t\\tif aggr.hasFloat && aggr.hasHistogram {\\n3268: \\t\\t\\t\\t// We cannot aggregate histogram sample with a float64 sample.\\n3269: \\t\\t\\t\\tannos.Add(annotations.NewMixedFloatsHistogramsAggWarning(e.Expr.PositionRange()))\\n3270: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n3271: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3272: \\t\\t\\tswitch {\\n3273: \\t\\t\\tcase aggr.incompatibleHistograms:\\n3274: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n3275: \\t\\t\\tcase aggr.hasHistogram:\\n3276: \\t\\t\\t\\taggr.histogramValue.Compact(0)\\n3277: \\t\\t\\tdefault:\\n3278: \\t\\t\\t\\taggr.floatValue += aggr.floatKahanC\\n3279: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3280: \\t\\tdefault:\\n3281: \\t\\t\\t// For other aggregations, we already have the right value.\\n3282: \\t\\t}\\n3283: \\n3284: \\t\\tss := &outputMatrix[ri]\\n3285: \\t\\taddToSeries(ss, enh.Ts, aggr.floatValue, aggr.histogramValue, numSteps)\\n3286: \\t\\tss.DropName = inputMatrix[ri].DropName\\n3287: \\t}\\n3288: \\n3289: \\treturn annos\\n3290: }\\n3291: \\n3292: // aggregationK evaluates topk, bottomk, limitk, or limit_ratio at one timestep on inputMatrix.\\n3293: // Output that has the same labels as the input, but just k of them per group.\\n3294: // seriesToResult maps inputMatrix indexes to groups indexes.\\n3295: // For an instant query, returns a Matrix in descending order for topk or ascending for bottomk, or without any order for limitk / limit_ratio.\\n3296: // For a range query, aggregates output in the seriess map.\\n3297: func (ev *evaluator) aggregationK(e *parser.AggregateExpr, fParam float64, inputMatrix Matrix, seriesToResult []int, groups []groupedAggregation, enh *EvalNodeHelper, seriess map[uint64]Series) (Matrix, annotations.Annotations) {\\n3298: \\top := e.Op\\n3299: \\tvar s Sample\\n3300: \\tvar annos annotations.Annotations\\n3301: \\t// Used to short-cut the loop for LIMITK if we already collected k elements for every group\\n3302: \\tgroupsRemaining := len(groups)\\n3303: \\tfor i := range groups {\\n3304: \\t\\tgroups[i].seen = false\\n3305: \\t}\\n3306: \\t// advanceRemainingSeries discards any values at the current timestamp `ts`\\n3307: \\t// for the remaining input series. In range queries, if these values are not\\n3308: \\t// consumed now, they will no longer be accessible in the next evaluation step.\\n3309: \\tadvanceRemainingSeries := func(ts int64, startIdx int) {\\n3310: \\t\\tfor i := startIdx; i < len(inputMatrix); i++ {\\n3311: \\t\\t\\t_, _, _ = ev.nextValues(ts, &inputMatrix[i])\\n3312: \\t\\t}\\n3313: \\t}\\n3314: \\n3315: seriesLoop:\\n3316: \\tfor si := range inputMatrix {\\n3317: \\t\\tf, h, ok := ev.nextValues(enh.Ts, &inputMatrix[si])\\n3318: \\t\\tif !ok {\\n3319: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n3320: \\t\\t}\\n3321: \\t\\ts = Sample{Metric: inputMatrix[si].Metric, F: f, H: h, DropName: inputMatrix[si].DropName}\\n3322: \\n3323: \\t\\tvar k int64\\n3324: \\t\\tvar r float64\\n3325: \\t\\tswitch op {\\n3326: \\t\\tcase parser.TOPK, parser.BOTTOMK, parser.LIMITK:\\n3327: \\t\\t\\tk = min(int64(fParam), int64(len(inputMatrix)))\\n3328: \\t\\t\\tif k < 1 {\\n3329: \\t\\t\\t\\tif enh.Ts != ev.endTimestamp {\\n3330: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tadvanceRemainingSeries(enh.Ts, si+1)\\n3331: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n3332: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil, annos\\n3333: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3334: \\t\\tcase parser.LIMIT_RATIO:\\n3335: \\t\\t\\tswitch {\\n3336: \\t\\t\\tcase fParam == 0:\\n3337: \\t\\t\\t\\tif enh.Ts != ev.endTimestamp {\\n3338: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tadvanceRemainingSeries(enh.Ts, si+1)\\n3339: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n3340: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil, annos\\n3341: \\t\\t\\tcase fParam < -1.0:\\n3342: \\t\\t\\t\\tr = -1.0\\n3343: \\t\\t\\tcase fParam > 1.0:\\n3344: \\t\\t\\t\\tr = 1.0\\n3345: \\t\\t\\tdefault:\\n3346: \\t\\t\\t\\tr = fParam\\n3347: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3348: \\t\\t}\\n3349: \\n3350: \\t\\tgroup := &groups[seriesToResult[si]]\\n3351: \\t\\t// Initialize this group if it's the first time we've seen it.\\n3352: \\t\\tif !group.seen {\\n3353: \\t\\t\\t// LIMIT_RATIO is a special case, as we may not add this very sample to the heap,\\n3354: \\t\\t\\t// while we also don't know the final size of it.\\n3355: \\t\\t\\tswitch op {\\n3356: \\t\\t\\tcase parser.LIMIT_RATIO:\\n3357: \\t\\t\\t\\t*group = groupedAggregation{\\n3358: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tseen: true,\\n3359: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\theap: make(vectorByValueHeap, 0),\\n3360: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n3361: \\t\\t\\t\\tif ratiosampler.AddRatioSample(r, &s) {\\n3362: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\theap.Push(&group.heap, &s)\\n3363: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n3364: \\t\\t\\tcase parser.LIMITK:\\n3365: \\t\\t\\t\\t*group = groupedAggregation{\\n3366: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tseen: true,\\n3367: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\theap: make(vectorByValueHeap, 1, k),\\n3368: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n3369: \\t\\t\\t\\tgroup.heap[0] = s\\n3370: \\t\\t\\tcase parser.TOPK:\\n3371: \\t\\t\\t\\t*group = groupedAggregation{\\n3372: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tseen: true,\\n3373: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\theap: make(vectorByValueHeap, 0, k),\\n3374: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n3375: \\t\\t\\t\\tif s.H != nil {\\n3376: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tgroup.seen = false\\n3377: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\\\"topk\\\", e.PosRange))\\n3378: \\t\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n3379: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\theap.Push(&group.heap, &s)\\n3380: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n3381: \\t\\t\\tcase parser.BOTTOMK:\\n3382: \\t\\t\\t\\t*group = groupedAggregation{\\n3383: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tseen: true,\\n3384: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\theap: make(vectorByValueHeap, 0, k),\\n3385: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n3386: \\t\\t\\t\\tif s.H != nil {\\n3387: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tgroup.seen = false\\n3388: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\\\"bottomk\\\", e.PosRange))\\n3389: \\t\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n3390: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\theap.Push(&group.heap, &s)\\n3391: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n3392: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3393: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n3394: \\t\\t}\\n3395: \\n3396: \\t\\tswitch op {\\n3397: \\t\\tcase parser.TOPK:\\n3398: \\t\\t\\t// We build a heap of up to k elements, with the smallest element at heap[0].\\n3399: \\t\\t\\tswitch {\\n3400: \\t\\t\\tcase s.H != nil:\\n3401: \\t\\t\\t\\t// Ignore histogram sample and add info annotation.\\n3402: \\t\\t\\t\\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\\\"topk\\\", e.PosRange))\\n3403: \\t\\t\\tcase int64(len(group.heap)) < k:\\n3404: \\t\\t\\t\\theap.Push(&group.heap, &s)\\n3405: \\t\\t\\tcase group.heap[0].F < s.F || (math.IsNaN(group.heap[0].F) && !math.IsNaN(s.F)):\\n3406: \\t\\t\\t\\t// This new element is bigger than the previous smallest element - overwrite that.\\n3407: \\t\\t\\t\\tgroup.heap[0] = s\\n3408: \\t\\t\\t\\tif k > 1 {\\n3409: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\theap.Fix(&group.heap, 0) // Maintain the heap invariant.\\n3410: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n3411: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3412: \\n3413: \\t\\tcase parser.BOTTOMK:\\n3414: \\t\\t\\t// We build a heap of up to k elements, with the biggest element at heap[0].\\n3415: \\t\\t\\tswitch {\\n3416: \\t\\t\\tcase s.H != nil:\\n3417: \\t\\t\\t\\t// Ignore histogram sample and add info annotation.\\n3418: \\t\\t\\t\\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\\\"bottomk\\\", e.PosRange))\\n3419: \\t\\t\\tcase int64(len(group.heap)) < k:\\n3420: \\t\\t\\t\\theap.Push((*vectorByReverseValueHeap)(&group.heap), &s)\\n3421: \\t\\t\\tcase group.heap[0].F > s.F || (math.IsNaN(group.heap[0].F) && !math.IsNaN(s.F)):\\n3422: \\t\\t\\t\\t// This new element is smaller than the previous biggest element - overwrite that.\\n3423: \\t\\t\\t\\tgroup.heap[0] = s\\n3424: \\t\\t\\t\\tif k > 1 {\\n3425: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\theap.Fix((*vectorByReverseValueHeap)(&group.heap), 0) // Maintain the heap invariant.\\n3426: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n3427: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3428: \\n3429: \\t\\tcase parser.LIMITK:\\n3430: \\t\\t\\tif int64(len(group.heap)) < k {\\n3431: \\t\\t\\t\\theap.Push(&group.heap, &s)\\n3432: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3433: \\t\\t\\t// LIMITK optimization: early break if we've added K elem to _every_ group,\\n3434: \\t\\t\\t// especially useful for large timeseries where the user is exploring labels via e.g.\\n3435: \\t\\t\\t// limitk(10, my_metric)\\n3436: \\t\\t\\tif !group.groupAggrComplete && int64(len(group.heap)) == k {\\n3437: \\t\\t\\t\\tgroup.groupAggrComplete = true\\n3438: \\t\\t\\t\\tgroupsRemaining--\\n3439: \\t\\t\\t\\tif groupsRemaining == 0 {\\n3440: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// Process other values in the series before breaking the loop in case of range query.\\n3441: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif enh.Ts != ev.endTimestamp {\\n3442: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tadvanceRemainingSeries(enh.Ts, si+1)\\n3443: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n3444: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tbreak seriesLoop\\n3445: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n3446: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3447: \\n3448: \\t\\tcase parser.LIMIT_RATIO:\\n3449: \\t\\t\\tif ratiosampler.AddRatioSample(r, &s) {\\n3450: \\t\\t\\t\\theap.Push(&group.heap, &s)\\n3451: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3452: \\n3453: \\t\\tdefault:\\n3454: \\t\\t\\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\\\"expected aggregation operator but got %q\\\", op))\\n3455: \\t\\t}\\n3456: \\t}\\n3457: \\n3458: \\t// Construct the result from the aggregated groups.\\n3459: \\tnumSteps := int((ev.endTimestamp-ev.startTimestamp)/ev.interval) + 1\\n3460: \\tvar mat Matrix\\n3461: \\tif ev.endTimestamp == ev.startTimestamp {\\n3462: \\t\\tmat = make(Matrix, 0, len(groups))\\n3463: \\t}\\n3464: \\n3465: \\tadd := func(lbls labels.Labels, f float64, h *histogram.FloatHistogram, dropName bool) {\\n3466: \\t\\t// If this could be an instant query, add directly to the matrix so the result is in consistent order.\\n3467: \\t\\tif ev.endTimestamp == ev.startTimestamp {\\n3468: \\t\\t\\tif h != nil {\\n3469: \\t\\t\\t\\tmat = append(mat, Series{Metric: lbls, Histograms: []HPoint{{T: enh.Ts, H: h}}, DropName: dropName})\\n3470: \\t\\t\\t} else {\\n3471: \\t\\t\\t\\tmat = append(mat, Series{Metric: lbls, Floats: []FPoint{{T: enh.Ts, F: f}}, DropName: dropName})\\n3472: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3473: \\t\\t} else {\\n3474: \\t\\t\\t// Otherwise the results are added into seriess elements.\\n3475: \\t\\t\\thash := lbls.Hash()\\n3476: \\t\\t\\tss, ok := seriess[hash]\\n3477: \\t\\t\\tif !ok {\\n3478: \\t\\t\\t\\tss = Series{Metric: lbls, DropName: dropName}\\n3479: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3480: \\t\\t\\taddToSeries(&ss, enh.Ts, f, h, numSteps)\\n3481: \\t\\t\\tseriess[hash] = ss\\n3482: \\t\\t}\\n3483: \\t}\\n3484: \\tfor _, aggr := range groups {\\n3485: \\t\\tif !aggr.seen {\\n3486: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n3487: \\t\\t}\\n3488: \\t\\tswitch op {\\n3489: \\t\\tcase parser.TOPK:\\n3490: \\t\\t\\t// The heap keeps the lowest value on top, so reverse it.\\n3491: \\t\\t\\tif len(aggr.heap) > 1 {\\n3492: \\t\\t\\t\\tsort.Sort(sort.Reverse(aggr.heap))\\n3493: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3494: \\t\\t\\tfor _, v := range aggr.heap {\\n3495: \\t\\t\\t\\tadd(v.Metric, v.F, v.H, v.DropName)\\n3496: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3497: \\n3498: \\t\\tcase parser.BOTTOMK:\\n3499: \\t\\t\\t// The heap keeps the highest value on top, so reverse it.\\n3500: \\t\\t\\tif len(aggr.heap) > 1 {\\n3501: \\t\\t\\t\\tsort.Sort(sort.Reverse((*vectorByReverseValueHeap)(&aggr.heap)))\\n3502: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3503: \\t\\t\\tfor _, v := range aggr.heap {\\n3504: \\t\\t\\t\\tadd(v.Metric, v.F, v.H, v.DropName)\\n3505: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3506: \\n3507: \\t\\tcase parser.LIMITK, parser.LIMIT_RATIO:\\n3508: \\t\\t\\tfor _, v := range aggr.heap {\\n3509: \\t\\t\\t\\tadd(v.Metric, v.F, v.H, v.DropName)\\n3510: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3511: \\t\\t}\\n3512: \\t}\\n3513: \\n3514: \\treturn mat, annos\\n3515: }\\n3516: \\n3517: // aggregationCountValues evaluates count_values on vec.\\n3518: // Outputs as many series per group as there are values in the input.\\n3519: func (*evaluator) aggregationCountValues(e *parser.AggregateExpr, grouping []string, valueLabel string, vec Vector, enh *EvalNodeHelper) (Vector, annotations.Annotations) {\\n3520: \\ttype groupCount struct {\\n3521: \\t\\tlabels labels.Labels\\n3522: \\t\\tcount  int\\n3523: \\t}\\n3524: \\tresult := map[uint64]*groupCount{}\\n3525: \\n3526: \\tvar buf []byte\\n3527: \\tfor _, s := range vec {\\n3528: \\t\\tenh.resetBuilder(s.Metric)\\n3529: \\t\\tif s.H == nil {\\n3530: \\t\\t\\tenh.lb.Set(valueLabel, strconv.FormatFloat(s.F, 'f', -1, 64))\\n3531: \\t\\t} else {\\n3532: \\t\\t\\tenh.lb.Set(valueLabel, s.H.String())\\n3533: \\t\\t}\\n3534: \\t\\tmetric := enh.lb.Labels()\\n3535: \\n3536: \\t\\t// Considering the count_values()\\n3537: \\t\\t// operator is less frequently used than other aggregations, we're fine having to\\n3538: \\t\\t// re-compute the grouping key on each step for this case.\\n3539: \\t\\tvar groupingKey uint64\\n3540: \\t\\tgroupingKey, buf = generateGroupingKey(metric, grouping, e.Without, buf)\\n3541: \\n3542: \\t\\tgroup, ok := result[groupingKey]\\n3543: \\t\\t// Add a new group if it doesn't exist.\\n3544: \\t\\tif !ok {\\n3545: \\t\\t\\tresult[groupingKey] = &groupCount{\\n3546: \\t\\t\\t\\tlabels: generateGroupingLabels(enh, metric, e.Without, grouping),\\n3547: \\t\\t\\t\\tcount:  1,\\n3548: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3549: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n3550: \\t\\t}\\n3551: \\n3552: \\t\\tgroup.count++\\n3553: \\t}\\n3554: \\n3555: \\t// Construct the result Vector from the aggregated groups.\\n3556: \\tfor _, aggr := range result {\\n3557: \\t\\tenh.Out = append(enh.Out, Sample{\\n3558: \\t\\t\\tMetric: aggr.labels,\\n3559: \\t\\t\\tF:      float64(aggr.count),\\n3560: \\t\\t})\\n3561: \\t}\\n3562: \\treturn enh.Out, nil\\n3563: }\\n3564: \\n3565: func (ev *evaluator) cleanupMetricLabels(v parser.Value) {\\n3566: \\tif v.Type() == parser.ValueTypeMatrix {\\n3567: \\t\\tmat := v.(Matrix)\\n3568: \\t\\tfor i := range mat {\\n3569: \\t\\t\\tif mat[i].DropName {\\n3570: \\t\\t\\t\\tmat[i].Metric = mat[i].Metric.DropReserved(schema.IsMetadataLabel)\\n3571: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3572: \\t\\t}\\n3573: \\t\\tif mat.ContainsSameLabelset() {\\n3574: \\t\\t\\tev.errorf(\\\"vector cannot contain metrics with the same labelset\\\")\\n3575: \\t\\t}\\n3576: \\t} else if v.Type() == parser.ValueTypeVector {\\n3577: \\t\\tvec := v.(Vector)\\n3578: \\t\\tfor i := range vec {\\n3579: \\t\\t\\tif vec[i].DropName {\\n3580: \\t\\t\\t\\tvec[i].Metric = vec[i].Metric.DropReserved(schema.IsMetadataLabel)\\n3581: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3582: \\t\\t}\\n3583: \\t\\tif vec.ContainsSameLabelset() {\\n3584: \\t\\t\\tev.errorf(\\\"vector cannot contain metrics with the same labelset\\\")\\n3585: \\t\\t}\\n3586: \\t}\\n3587: }\\n3588: \\n3589: func addToSeries(ss *Series, ts int64, f float64, h *histogram.FloatHistogram, numSteps int) {\\n3590: \\tif h == nil {\\n3591: \\t\\tif ss.Floats == nil {\\n3592: \\t\\t\\tss.Floats = getFPointSlice(numSteps)\\n3593: \\t\\t}\\n3594: \\t\\tss.Floats = append(ss.Floats, FPoint{T: ts, F: f})\\n3595: \\t\\treturn\\n3596: \\t}\\n3597: \\tif ss.Histograms == nil {\\n3598: \\t\\tss.Histograms = getHPointSlice(numSteps)\\n3599: \\t}\\n3600: \\tss.Histograms = append(ss.Histograms, HPoint{T: ts, H: h})\\n3601: }\\n3602: \\n3603: func (*evaluator) nextValues(ts int64, series *Series) (f float64, h *histogram.FloatHistogram, b bool) {\\n3604: \\tswitch {\\n3605: \\tcase len(series.Floats) > 0 && series.Floats[0].T == ts:\\n3606: \\t\\tf = series.Floats[0].F\\n3607: \\t\\tseries.Floats = series.Floats[1:] // Move input vectors forward\\n3608: \\tcase len(series.Histograms) > 0 && series.Histograms[0].T == ts:\\n3609: \\t\\th = series.Histograms[0].H\\n3610: \\t\\tseries.Histograms = series.Histograms[1:]\\n3611: \\tdefault:\\n3612: \\t\\treturn f, h, false\\n3613: \\t}\\n3614: \\treturn f, h, true\\n3615: }\\n3616: \\n3617: // handleAggregationError adds the appropriate annotation based on the aggregation error.\\n3618: func handleAggregationError(err error, e *parser.AggregateExpr, metricName string, annos *annotations.Annotations) {\\n3619: \\tpos := e.Expr.PositionRange()\\n3620: \\tif errors.Is(err, histogram.ErrHistogramsIncompatibleSchema) {\\n3621: \\t\\tannos.Add(annotations.NewMixedExponentialCustomHistogramsWarning(metricName, pos))\\n3622: \\t} else if errors.Is(err, histogram.ErrHistogramsIncompatibleBounds) {\\n3623: \\t\\tannos.Add(annotations.NewIncompatibleCustomBucketsHistogramsWarning(metricName, pos))\\n3624: \\t}\\n3625: }\\n3626: \\n3627: // handleVectorBinopError returns the appropriate annotation based on the vector binary operation error.\\n3628: func handleVectorBinopError(err error, e *parser.BinaryExpr) annotations.Annotations {\\n3629: \\tif err == nil {\\n3630: \\t\\treturn nil\\n3631: \\t}\\n3632: \\top := parser.ItemTypeStr[e.Op]\\n3633: \\tpos := e.PositionRange()\\n3634: \\tif errors.Is(err, annotations.PromQLInfo) || errors.Is(err, annotations.PromQLWarning) {\\n3635: \\t\\treturn annotations.New().Add(err)\\n3636: \\t}\\n3637: \\t// TODO(NeerajGartia21): Test the exact annotation output once the testing framework can do so.\\n3638: \\tif errors.Is(err, histogram.ErrHistogramsIncompatibleSchema) || errors.Is(err, histogram.ErrHistogramsIncompatibleBounds) {\\n3639: \\t\\treturn annotations.New().Add(annotations.NewIncompatibleBucketLayoutInBinOpWarning(op, pos))\\n3640: \\t}\\n3641: \\treturn nil\\n3642: }\\n3643: \\n3644: // generateGroupingKey builds and returns the grouping key for the given metric and\\n3645: // grouping labels.\\n3646: func generateGroupingKey(metric labels.Labels, grouping []string, without bool, buf []byte) (uint64, []byte) {\\n3647: \\tif without {\\n3648: \\t\\treturn metric.HashWithoutLabels(buf, grouping...)\\n3649: \\t}\\n3650: \\n3651: \\tif len(grouping) == 0 {\\n3652: \\t\\t// No need to generate any hash if there are no grouping labels.\\n3653: \\t\\treturn 0, buf\\n3654: \\t}\\n3655: \\n3656: \\treturn metric.HashForLabels(buf, grouping...)\\n3657: }\\n3658: \\n3659: func generateGroupingLabels(enh *EvalNodeHelper, metric labels.Labels, without bool, grouping []string) labels.Labels {\\n3660: \\tenh.resetBuilder(metric)\\n3661: \\tswitch {\\n3662: \\tcase without:\\n3663: \\t\\tenh.lb.Del(grouping...)\\n3664: \\t\\tenh.lb.Del(labels.MetricName)\\n3665: \\t\\treturn enh.lb.Labels()\\n3666: \\tcase len(grouping) > 0:\\n3667: \\t\\tenh.lb.Keep(grouping...)\\n3668: \\t\\treturn enh.lb.Labels()\\n3669: \\tdefault:\\n3670: \\t\\treturn labels.EmptyLabels()\\n3671: \\t}\\n3672: }\\n3673: \\n3674: // btos returns 1 if b is true, 0 otherwise.\\n3675: func btos(b bool) float64 {\\n3676: \\tif b {\\n3677: \\t\\treturn 1\\n3678: \\t}\\n3679: \\treturn 0\\n3680: }\\n3681: \\n3682: // changesMetricSchema returns true whether the op operation changes the semantic meaning or\\n3683: // schema of the metric.\\n3684: func changesMetricSchema(op parser.ItemType) bool {\\n3685: \\tswitch op {\\n3686: \\tcase parser.ADD, parser.SUB, parser.DIV, parser.MUL, parser.POW, parser.MOD, parser.ATAN2:\\n3687: \\t\\treturn true\\n3688: \\tdefault:\\n3689: \\t\\treturn false\\n3690: \\t}\\n3691: }\\n3692: \\n3693: // NewOriginContext returns a new context with data about the origin attached.\\n3694: func NewOriginContext(ctx context.Context, data map[string]any) context.Context {\\n3695: \\treturn context.WithValue(ctx, QueryOrigin{}, data)\\n3696: }\\n3697: \\n3698: func formatDate(t time.Time) string {\\n3699: \\treturn t.UTC().Format(\\\"2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z07:00\\\")\\n3700: }\\n3701: \\n3702: // unwrapParenExpr does the AST equivalent of removing parentheses around a expression.\\n3703: func unwrapParenExpr(e *parser.Expr) {\\n3704: \\tfor {\\n3705: \\t\\tp, ok := (*e).(*parser.ParenExpr)\\n3706: \\t\\tif !ok {\\n3707: \\t\\t\\tbreak\\n3708: \\t\\t}\\n3709: \\t\\t*e = p.Expr\\n3710: \\t}\\n3711: }\\n3712: \\n3713: func unwrapStepInvariantExpr(e parser.Expr) parser.Expr {\\n3714: \\tif p, ok := e.(*parser.StepInvariantExpr); ok {\\n3715: \\t\\treturn p.Expr\\n3716: \\t}\\n3717: \\treturn e\\n3718: }\\n3719: \\n3720: // PreprocessExpr wraps all possible step invariant parts of the given expression with\\n3721: // StepInvariantExpr. It also resolves the preprocessors, evaluates duration expressions\\n3722: // into their numeric values and removes superfluous parenthesis on parameters to functions and aggregations.\\n3723: func PreprocessExpr(expr parser.Expr, start, end time.Time, step time.Duration) (parser.Expr, error) {\\n3724: \\tdetectHistogramStatsDecoding(expr)\\n3725: \\n3726: \\tif err := parser.Walk(&durationVisitor{step: step}, expr, nil); err != nil {\\n3727: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n3728: \\t}\\n3729: \\n3730: \\t_, shouldWrap := preprocessExprHelper(expr, start, end)\\n3731: \\tif shouldWrap {\\n3732: \\t\\treturn newStepInvariantExpr(expr), nil\\n3733: \\t}\\n3734: \\treturn expr, nil\\n3735: }\\n3736: \\n3737: // preprocessExprHelper wraps child nodes of expr with a StepInvariantExpr,\\n3738: // at the highest level within the tree that is step-invariant.\\n3739: // Also resolves start() and end() on selector and subquery nodes.\\n3740: // Also remove superfluous parenthesis on parameters to functions and aggregations.\\n3741: // Return isStepInvariant is true when the whole subexpression is step invariant.\\n3742: // Return shoudlWrap is false for cases like MatrixSelector and StringLiteral that never need to be wrapped.\\n3743: func preprocessExprHelper(expr parser.Expr, start, end time.Time) (isStepInvariant, shouldWrap bool) {\\n3744: \\tswitch n := expr.(type) {\\n3745: \\tcase *parser.VectorSelector:\\n3746: \\t\\tswitch n.StartOrEnd {\\n3747: \\t\\tcase parser.START:\\n3748: \\t\\t\\tn.Timestamp = makeInt64Pointer(timestamp.FromTime(start))\\n3749: \\t\\tcase parser.END:\\n3750: \\t\\t\\tn.Timestamp = makeInt64Pointer(timestamp.FromTime(end))\\n3751: \\t\\t}\\n3752: \\t\\treturn n.Timestamp != nil, n.Timestamp != nil\\n3753: \\n3754: \\tcase *parser.AggregateExpr:\\n3755: \\t\\tunwrapParenExpr(&n.Expr)\\n3756: \\t\\tunwrapParenExpr(&n.Param)\\n3757: \\t\\treturn preprocessExprHelper(n.Expr, start, end)\\n3758: \\n3759: \\tcase *parser.BinaryExpr:\\n3760: \\t\\tisInvariantLHS, shouldWrapLHS := preprocessExprHelper(n.LHS, start, end)\\n3761: \\t\\tisInvariantRHS, shouldWrapRHS := preprocessExprHelper(n.RHS, start, end)\\n3762: \\t\\tif isInvariantLHS && isInvariantRHS {\\n3763: \\t\\t\\treturn true, true\\n3764: \\t\\t}\\n3765: \\n3766: \\t\\tif shouldWrapLHS {\\n3767: \\t\\t\\tn.LHS = newStepInvariantExpr(n.LHS)\\n3768: \\t\\t}\\n3769: \\t\\tif shouldWrapRHS {\\n3770: \\t\\t\\tn.RHS = newStepInvariantExpr(n.RHS)\\n3771: \\t\\t}\\n3772: \\n3773: \\t\\treturn false, false\\n3774: \\n3775: \\tcase *parser.Call:\\n3776: \\t\\t_, ok := AtModifierUnsafeFunctions[n.Func.Name]\\n3777: \\t\\tisStepInvariant := !ok\\n3778: \\t\\tshouldWrap := make([]bool, len(n.Args))\\n3779: \\t\\tfor i := range n.Args {\\n3780: \\t\\t\\tunwrapParenExpr(&n.Args[i])\\n3781: \\t\\t\\tvar argIsStepInvariant bool\\n3782: \\t\\t\\targIsStepInvariant, shouldWrap[i] = preprocessExprHelper(n.Args[i], start, end)\\n3783: \\t\\t\\tisStepInvariant = isStepInvariant && argIsStepInvariant\\n3784: \\t\\t}\\n3785: \\n3786: \\t\\tif isStepInvariant {\\n3787: \\t\\t\\t// The function and all arguments are step invariant.\\n3788: \\t\\t\\treturn true, true\\n3789: \\t\\t}\\n3790: \\n3791: \\t\\tfor i, isi := range shouldWrap {\\n3792: \\t\\t\\tif isi {\\n3793: \\t\\t\\t\\tn.Args[i] = newStepInvariantExpr(n.Args[i])\\n3794: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3795: \\t\\t}\\n3796: \\t\\treturn false, false\\n3797: \\n3798: \\tcase *parser.MatrixSelector:\\n3799: \\t\\t// We don't need to wrap a MatrixSelector because functions over range vectors evaluate those directly.\\n3800: \\t\\tisStepInvariant, _ := preprocessExprHelper(n.VectorSelector, start, end)\\n3801: \\t\\treturn isStepInvariant, false\\n3802: \\n3803: \\tcase *parser.SubqueryExpr:\\n3804: \\t\\t// Since we adjust offset for the @ modifier evaluation,\\n3805: \\t\\t// it gets tricky to adjust it for every subquery step.\\n3806: \\t\\t// Hence we wrap the inside of subquery irrespective of\\n3807: \\t\\t// @ on subquery (given it is also step invariant) so that\\n3808: \\t\\t// it is evaluated only once w.r.t. the start time of subquery.\\n3809: \\t\\tisInvariant, _ := preprocessExprHelper(n.Expr, start, end)\\n3810: \\t\\tif isInvariant {\\n3811: \\t\\t\\tn.Expr = newStepInvariantExpr(n.Expr)\\n3812: \\t\\t}\\n3813: \\t\\tswitch n.StartOrEnd {\\n3814: \\t\\tcase parser.START:\\n3815: \\t\\t\\tn.Timestamp = makeInt64Pointer(timestamp.FromTime(start))\\n3816: \\t\\tcase parser.END:\\n3817: \\t\\t\\tn.Timestamp = makeInt64Pointer(timestamp.FromTime(end))\\n3818: \\t\\t}\\n3819: \\t\\treturn n.Timestamp != nil, n.Timestamp != nil\\n3820: \\n3821: \\tcase *parser.ParenExpr:\\n3822: \\t\\treturn preprocessExprHelper(n.Expr, start, end)\\n3823: \\n3824: \\tcase *parser.UnaryExpr:\\n3825: \\t\\treturn preprocessExprHelper(n.Expr, start, end)\\n3826: \\n3827: \\tcase *parser.StringLiteral, *parser.NumberLiteral:\\n3828: \\t\\treturn true, false\\n3829: \\t}\\n3830: \\n3831: \\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"found unexpected node %#v\\\", expr))\\n3832: }\\n3833: \\n3834: func newStepInvariantExpr(expr parser.Expr) parser.Expr {\\n3835: \\treturn &parser.StepInvariantExpr{Expr: expr}\\n3836: }\\n3837: \\n3838: // setOffsetForAtModifier modifies the offset of vector and matrix selector\\n3839: // and subquery in the tree to accommodate the timestamp of @ modifier.\\n3840: // The offset is adjusted w.r.t. the given evaluation time.\\n3841: func setOffsetForAtModifier(evalTime int64, expr parser.Expr) {\\n3842: \\tgetOffset := func(ts *int64, originalOffset time.Duration, path []parser.Node) time.Duration {\\n3843: \\t\\tif ts == nil {\\n3844: \\t\\t\\treturn originalOffset\\n3845: \\t\\t}\\n3846: \\n3847: \\t\\tsubqOffset, _, subqTs := subqueryTimes(path)\\n3848: \\t\\tif subqTs != nil {\\n3849: \\t\\t\\tsubqOffset += time.Duration(evalTime-*subqTs) * time.Millisecond\\n3850: \\t\\t}\\n3851: \\n3852: \\t\\toffsetForTs := time.Duration(evalTime-*ts) * time.Millisecond\\n3853: \\t\\toffsetDiff := offsetForTs - subqOffset\\n3854: \\t\\treturn originalOffset + offsetDiff\\n3855: \\t}\\n3856: \\n3857: \\tparser.Inspect(expr, func(node parser.Node, path []parser.Node) error {\\n3858: \\t\\tswitch n := node.(type) {\\n3859: \\t\\tcase *parser.VectorSelector:\\n3860: \\t\\t\\tn.Offset = getOffset(n.Timestamp, n.OriginalOffset, path)\\n3861: \\n3862: \\t\\tcase *parser.MatrixSelector:\\n3863: \\t\\t\\tvs := n.VectorSelector.(*parser.VectorSelector)\\n3864: \\t\\t\\tvs.Offset = getOffset(vs.Timestamp, vs.OriginalOffset, path)\\n3865: \\n3866: \\t\\tcase *parser.SubqueryExpr:\\n3867: \\t\\t\\tn.Offset = getOffset(n.Timestamp, n.OriginalOffset, path)\\n3868: \\t\\t}\\n3869: \\t\\treturn nil\\n3870: \\t})\\n3871: }\\n3872: \\n3873: // detectHistogramStatsDecoding modifies the expression by setting the\\n3874: // SkipHistogramBuckets field in those vector selectors for which it is safe to\\n3875: // return only histogram statistics (sum and count), excluding histogram spans\\n3876: // and buckets. The function can be treated as an optimization and is not\\n3877: // required for correctness.\\n3878: func detectHistogramStatsDecoding(expr parser.Expr) {\\n3879: \\tparser.Inspect(expr, func(node parser.Node, path []parser.Node) error {\\n3880: \\t\\tn, ok := (node).(*parser.VectorSelector)\\n3881: \\t\\tif !ok {\\n3882: \\t\\t\\treturn nil\\n3883: \\t\\t}\\n3884: \\n3885: \\t\\tfor i := len(path) - 1; i > 0; i-- { // Walk backwards up the path.\\n3886: \\t\\t\\tcall, ok := path[i].(*parser.Call)\\n3887: \\t\\t\\tif !ok {\\n3888: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n3889: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3890: \\t\\t\\tswitch call.Func.Name {\\n3891: \\t\\t\\tcase \\\"histogram_count\\\", \\\"histogram_sum\\\", \\\"histogram_avg\\\":\\n3892: \\t\\t\\t\\tn.SkipHistogramBuckets = true\\n3893: \\t\\t\\tcase \\\"histogram_quantile\\\", \\\"histogram_fraction\\\":\\n3894: \\t\\t\\t\\tn.SkipHistogramBuckets = false\\n3895: \\t\\t\\tdefault:\\n3896: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n3897: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3898: \\t\\t\\tbreak\\n3899: \\t\\t}\\n3900: \\t\\treturn errors.New(\\\"stop\\\")\\n3901: \\t})\\n3902: }\\n3903: \\n3904: func makeInt64Pointer(val int64) *int64 {\\n3905: \\tvalp := new(int64)\\n3906: \\t*valp = val\\n3907: \\treturn valp\\n3908: }\\n3909: \\n3910: // RatioSampler allows unit-testing (previously: Randomizer).\\n3911: type RatioSampler interface {\\n3912: \\t// Return this sample \\\"offset\\\" between [0.0, 1.0]\\n3913: \\tsampleOffset(ts int64, sample *Sample) float64\\n3914: \\tAddRatioSample(r float64, sample *Sample) bool\\n3915: }\\n3916: \\n3917: // HashRatioSampler uses Hash(labels.String()) / maxUint64 as a \\\"deterministic\\\"\\n3918: // value in [0.0, 1.0].\\n3919: type HashRatioSampler struct{}\\n3920: \\n3921: var ratiosampler RatioSampler = NewHashRatioSampler()\\n3922: \\n3923: func NewHashRatioSampler() *HashRatioSampler {\\n3924: \\treturn &HashRatioSampler{}\\n3925: }\\n3926: \\n3927: func (*HashRatioSampler) sampleOffset(_ int64, sample *Sample) float64 {\\n3928: \\tconst (\\n3929: \\t\\tfloat64MaxUint64 = float64(math.MaxUint64)\\n3930: \\t)\\n3931: \\treturn float64(sample.Metric.Hash()) / float64MaxUint64\\n3932: }\\n3933: \\n3934: func (s *HashRatioSampler) AddRatioSample(ratioLimit float64, sample *Sample) bool {\\n3935: \\t// If ratioLimit >= 0: add sample if sampleOffset is lesser than ratioLimit\\n3936: \\t//\\n3937: \\t// 0.0        ratioLimit                1.0\\n3938: \\t//  [---------|--------------------------]\\n3939: \\t//  [#########...........................]\\n3940: \\t//\\n3941: \\t// e.g.:\\n3942: \\t//   sampleOffset==0.3 && ratioLimit==0.4\\n3943: \\t//     0.3 < 0.4 ? --> add sample\\n3944: \\t//\\n3945: \\t// Else if ratioLimit < 0: add sample if rand() return the \\\"complement\\\" of ratioLimit>=0 case\\n3946: \\t// (loosely similar behavior to negative array index in other programming languages)\\n3947: \\t//\\n3948: \\t// 0.0       1+ratioLimit               1.0\\n3949: \\t//  [---------|--------------------------]\\n3950: \\t//  [.........###########################]\\n3951: \\t//\\n3952: \\t// e.g.:\\n3953: \\t//   sampleOffset==0.3 && ratioLimit==-0.6\\n3954: \\t//     0.3 >= 0.4 ? --> don't add sample\\n3955: \\tsampleOffset := s.sampleOffset(sample.T, sample)\\n3956: \\treturn (ratioLimit >= 0 && sampleOffset < ratioLimit) ||\\n3957: \\t\\t(ratioLimit < 0 && sampleOffset >= (1.0+ratioLimit))\\n3958: }\\n3959: \\n3960: type histogramStatsSeries struct {\\n3961: \\tstorage.Series\\n3962: }\\n3963: \\n3964: func newHistogramStatsSeries(series storage.Series) *histogramStatsSeries {\\n3965: \\treturn &histogramStatsSeries{Series: series}\\n3966: }\\n3967: \\n3968: func (s histogramStatsSeries) Iterator(it chunkenc.Iterator) chunkenc.Iterator {\\n3969: \\t// Try to reuse the iterator if we can.\\n3970: \\tif statsIterator, ok := it.(*HistogramStatsIterator); ok {\\n3971: \\t\\tstatsIterator.Reset(s.Series.Iterator(statsIterator.Iterator))\\n3972: \\t\\treturn statsIterator\\n3973: \\t}\\n3974: \\n3975: \\treturn NewHistogramStatsIterator(s.Series.Iterator(it))\\n3976: }\\n3977: \\n3978: // gatherVector gathers a Vector for ts from the series in input.\\n3979: // output is used as a buffer.\\n3980: // If bufHelpers and seriesHelpers are provided, seriesHelpers[i] is appended to bufHelpers for every input index i.\\n3981: // The gathered Vector and bufHelper are returned.\\n3982: func (ev *evaluator) gatherVector(ts int64, input Matrix, output Vector, bufHelpers, seriesHelpers []EvalSeriesHelper) (Vector, []EvalSeriesHelper) {\\n3983: \\toutput = output[:0]\\n3984: \\tfor i, series := range input {\\n3985: \\t\\tswitch {\\n3986: \\t\\tcase len(series.Floats) > 0 && series.Floats[0].T == ts:\\n3987: \\t\\t\\ts := series.Floats[0]\\n3988: \\t\\t\\toutput = append(output, Sample{Metric: series.Metric, F: s.F, T: ts, DropName: series.DropName})\\n3989: \\t\\t\\t// Move input vectors forward so we don't have to re-scan the same\\n3990: \\t\\t\\t// past points at the next step.\\n3991: \\t\\t\\tinput[i].Floats = series.Floats[1:]\\n3992: \\t\\tcase len(series.Histograms) > 0 && series.Histograms[0].T == ts:\\n3993: \\t\\t\\ts := series.Histograms[0]\\n3994: \\t\\t\\toutput = append(output, Sample{Metric: series.Metric, H: s.H, T: ts, DropName: series.DropName})\\n3995: \\t\\t\\tinput[i].Histograms = series.Histograms[1:]\\n3996: \\t\\tdefault:\\n3997: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n3998: \\t\\t}\\n3999: \\t\\tif len(seriesHelpers) > 0 {\\n4000: \\t\\t\\tbufHelpers = append(bufHelpers, seriesHelpers[i])\\n4001: \\t\\t}\\n4002: \\n4003: \\t\\t// Don't add histogram size here because we only\\n4004: \\t\\t// copy the pointer above, not the whole\\n4005: \\t\\t// histogram.\\n4006: \\t\\tev.currentSamples++\\n4007: \\t\\tif ev.currentSamples > ev.maxSamples {\\n4008: \\t\\t\\tev.error(ErrTooManySamples(env))\\n4009: \\t\\t}\\n4010: \\t}\\n4011: \\tev.samplesStats.UpdatePeak(ev.currentSamples)\\n4012: \\n4013: \\treturn output, bufHelpers\\n4014: }\\n4015: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4@ba16cf5a7437eb3b6b71b74557b7a67e1975ad0f/-/blob/promql/engine.go%3FL3201-4014\"}"

\\\"canceled\\\"}\\n99: \\terrorExec          = errorType{ErrorExec, \\\"execution\\\"}\\n100: \\terrorBadData       = errorType{ErrorBadData, \\\"bad_data\\\"}\\n101: \\terrorInternal      = []string{\\\"127.0.0.1\\\", \\\"localhost\\\", \\\"::1\\\"}\\n112: \\n113: type apiError struct {\\n114: \\ttyp errorType\\n115: \\terr error\\n116: }\\n117: \\n118: func (e *apiError) Error() string {\\n124: \\tScrapePools() []string\\n125: }\\n126: \\n127: // TargetRetriever provides the list of active/dropped targets to scrape or not.\\n128: type TargetRetriever interface {\\n129: \\tTargetsActive() map[string][]*scrape.Target\\n130: \\tTargetsDropped() map[string][]*scrape.Target\\n131: \\tTargetsDroppedCounts() map[string]int\\n132: }\\n133: \\n134: // AlertmanagerRetriever }\\n145: \\n146: // StatsRenderer converts engine statistics into a format suitable for the API.\\n147: type StatsRenderer func(context.Context, *stats.Statistics, string) stats.QueryStats\\n148: \\n149: // DefaultStatsRenderer is the default stats renderer for the API.\\n150: func DefaultStatsRenderer(_ context.Context, s *stats.Statistics, param string) stats.QueryStats {\\n151: \\tif param != \\\"\\\" {\\n152: \\t\\treturn stats.NewQueryStats(s)\\n153: \\t}\\n154: \\treturn nil\\n155: }\\n156: \\n157: // PrometheusVersion contains build information about Prometheus.\\n158: type PrometheusVersion struct {\\n159: \\tVersion   string `json:\\\"version\\\"`\\n160: \\tRevision  string `json:\\\"revision\\\"`\\n161: \\tBranch    string `json:\\\"branch\\\"`\\n162: \\tBuildUser string `json:\\\"buildUser\\\"`\\n163: \\tBuildDate string `json:\\\"buildDate\\\"`\\n164: \\tGoVersion string `json:\\\"goVersion\\\"`\\n165: }\\n166: \\n167: // RuntimeInfo contains runtime information about Prometheus.\\n168: type RuntimeInfo struct {\\n169: \\tStartTime           time.Time `json:\\\"startTime\\\"`\\n170: \\tCWD                 string    `json:\\\"CWD\\\"`\\n171: \\tHostname            string    `json:\\\"hostname\\\"`\\n172: \\tServerTime          time.Time `json:\\\"serverTime\\\"`\\n173: \\tReloadConfigSuccess bool      `json:\\\"reloadConfigSuccess\\\"`\\n174: \\tLastConfigTime      time.Time `json:\\\"lastConfigTime\\\"`\\n175: \\tCorruptionCount     int64     `json:\\\"corruptionCount\\\"`\\n176: \\tGoroutineCount      int       `json:\\\"goroutineCount\\\"`\\n177: \\tGOMAXPROCS          int       `json:\\\"GOMAXPROCS\\\"`\\n178: \\tGOMEMLIMIT          int64     `json:\\\"GOMEMLIMIT\\\"`\\n179: \\tGOGC Response contains a response to a HTTP API request.\\n185: type Response struct {\\n186: \\tStatus    status   `json:\\\"status\\\"`\\n187: \\tData      any      `json:\\\"data,omitempty\\\"`\\n188: \\tErrorType string   `json:\\\"errorType,omitempty\\\"`\\n189: \\tError     string   `json:\\\"error,omitempty\\\"`\\n190: \\tWarnings  []string `json:\\\"warnings,omitempty\\\"`\\n191: \\tInfos     []string `json:\\\"infos,omitempty\\\"`\\n192: }\\n193: \\n194: type apiFuncResult struct {\\n195: \\tdata      any\\n196: \\terr       *apiError\\n197: \\twarnings  annotations.Annotations\\n198: \\tfinalizer func()\\n199: }\\n200: \\n201: type apiFunc func(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult\\n202: \\n203: // TSDBAdminStats defines the tsdb interfaces used by the v1 API for admin operations as well as statistics.\\n204: type TSDBAdminStats interface {\\n205: \\tCleanTombstones() error\\n206: \\tDelete(ctx context.Context, mint, maxt int64, ms ...*labels.Matcher) error\\n207: \\tSnapshot(dir string, withHead bool) error\\n208: \\tStats(statsByLabelName string, limit int) (*tsdb.Stats, error)\\n209: \\tWALReplayStatus() (tsdb.WALReplayStatus, error)\\n210: \\tBlockMetas() ([]tsdb.BlockMeta, error)\\n211: }\\n212: \\n213: type QueryOpts interface {\\n214: \\tEnablePerStepStats() bool\\n215: \\tLookbackDelta() time.Duration\\n216: }\\n217: \\n218: // API can register a set of endpoints in a router and handle\\n219: // them using the provided storage and query engine.\\n220: type API struct {\\n221: \\tQueryable         storage.SampleAndChunkQueryable\\n222: \\tQueryEngine       promql.QueryEngine\\n223: \\tExemplarQueryable storage.ExemplarQueryable\\n224: \\n225: \\tscrapePoolsRetriever  func(context.Context) ScrapePoolsRetriever\\n226: \\ttargetRetriever       func(context.Context) TargetRetriever\\n227: \\talertmanagerRetriever func(context.Context) AlertmanagerRetriever\\n228: \\trulesRetriever        func(context.Context) RulesRetriever\\n229: \\tnow                   func() time.Time\\n230: \\tconfig                func() config.Config\\n231: \\tflagsMap              map[string]string\\n232: \\tready                 func(http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc\\n233: \\tglobalURLOptions      GlobalURLOptions\\n234: \\n235: \\tdb                  TSDBAdminStats\\n236: \\tdbDir               string\\n237: \\tenableAdmin         bool\\n238: \\tlogger              *slog.Logger\\n239: \\tCORSOrigin          *regexp.Regexp\\n240: \\tbuildInfo           *PrometheusVersion\\n241: \\truntimeInfo         func() (RuntimeInfo, error)\\n242: \\tgatherer            prometheus.Gatherer\\n243: \\tisAgent             bool\\n244: \\tstatsRenderer       StatsRenderer\\n245: \\tnotificationsGetter func() OverrideErrorCode\\n249: \\n250: \\tremoteWriteHandler http.Handler\\n251: \\tremoteReadHandler  http.Handler\\n252: \\totlpWriteHandler   http.Handler\\n253: \\n254: \\tcodecs []Codec\\n255: }\\n256: \\n257: // NewAPI returns an initialized API type.\\n258: func NewAPI(\\n259: \\tqe promql.QueryEngine,\\n260: \\tq storage.SampleAndChunkQueryable,\\n261: \\tap storage.Appendable,\\n262: \\teq storage.ExemplarQueryable,\\n263: \\tspsr func(context.Context) ScrapePoolsRetriever,\\n264: \\ttr func(context.Context) TargetRetriever,\\n265: \\tar func(context.Context) AlertmanagerRetriever,\\n266: \\tconfigFunc func() config.Config,\\n267: \\tflagsMap map[string]string,\\n268: \\tglobalURLOptions GlobalURLOptions,\\n269: \\treadyFunc func(http.HandlerFunc) \\tremoteReadSampleLimit int,\\n276: \\tremoteReadConcurrencyLimit int,\\n277: \\tremoteReadMaxBytesInFrame int,\\n278: \\tisAgent bool,\\n279: \\tcorsOrigin *regexp.Regexp,\\n280: \\truntimeInfo func() (RuntimeInfo, error),\\n281: \\tbuildInfo *PrometheusVersion,\\n282: \\tnotificationsGetter func() []notifications.Notification,\\n283: \\tnotificationsSub func() (<-chan notifications.Notification, func(), bool),\\n284: \\tgatherer prometheus.Gatherer,\\n285: \\tregisterer prometheus.Registerer,\\n286: \\tstatsRenderer StatsRenderer,\\n287: \\trwEnabled bool,\\n288: time.Duration,\\n292: \\tenableTypeAndUnitLabels bool,\\n293: \\toverrideErrorCode OverrideErrorCode,\\n294: ) *API {\\n295: \\ta := &API{\\n296: \\t\\tQueryEngine:       qe,\\n297: \\t\\tQueryable: \\t\\tlogger:              logger,\\n314: \\t\\tCORSOrigin:          corsOrigin,\\n315: \\t\\truntimeInfo:         runtimeInfo,\\n316: \\t\\tbuildInfo:           buildInfo,\\n317: \\t\\tgatherer:            gatherer,\\n318: \\t\\tisAgent:             isAgent,\\n319: \\t\\tstatsRenderer:       DefaultStatsRenderer,\\n320: \\t\\tnotificationsGetter: notificationsGetter,\\n321: \\t\\tnotificationsSub:    notificationsSub,\\n322: \\t\\toverrideErrorCode:   overrideErrorCode,\\n323: \\n324: \\t\\tremoteReadHandler: remote.NewReadHandler(logger, registerer, q, configFunc, remoteReadSampleLimit, remoteReadConcurrencyLimit, remoteReadMaxBytesInFrame),\\n325: \\t}\\n326: \\n327: \\ta.InstallCodec(JSONCodec{})\\n328: \\n329: \\tif statsRenderer != nil {\\n330: \\t\\ta.statsRenderer = statsRenderer\\n331: \\t}\\n332: \\n333: \\tif ap == nil && (rwEnabled || otlpEnabled) {\\n334: \\t\\tpanic(\\\"remote write or otlp write enabled, but no appender passed in.\\\")\\n335: \\t}\\n336: \\n337: \\tif rwEnabled {\\n338: \\t\\ta.remoteWriteHandler = remote.NewWriteHandler(logger, registerer, ap, acceptRemoteWriteProtoMsgs, ctZeroIngestionEnabled)\\n339: \\t}\\n340: \\tif otlpEnabled {\\n341: \\t\\ta.otlpWriteHandler = remote.NewOTLPWriteHandler(logger, registerer, ap, configFunc, remote.OTLPOptions{\\n342: \\t\\t\\tConvertDelta:            otlpDeltaToCumulative,\\n343: \\t\\t\\tNativeDelta:             otlpNativeDeltaIngestion,\\n344: \\t\\t\\tLookbackDelta:           lookbackDelta,\\n345: \\t\\t\\tEnableTypeAndUnitLabels: enableTypeAndUnitLabels,\\n346: \\t\\t})\\n347: \\t}\\n348: \\n349: \\treturn a\\n350: }\\n351: \\n352: // InstallCodec adds codec to this API's available codecs.\\n353: // Codecs installed first take precedence over codecs installed later when evaluating wildcards in Accept headers.\\n354: // The first installed codec is used as a fallback when the Accept header cannot be satisfied or if there is no Accept header.\\n355: func (api *API) InstallCodec(codec Codec) {\\n356: \\tapi.codecs = append(api.codecs, codec)\\n357: }\\n358: \\n359: // ClearCodecs removes all available codecs from this API, including the default codec installed by NewAPI.\\n360: func (api *API) ClearCodecs() {\\n361: \\tapi.codecs = nil\\n362: }\\n363: \\n364: func setUnavailStatusOnTSDBNotReady(r apiFuncResult) apiFuncResult {\\n365: \\tif r.err != nil && errors.Is(r.err.err, tsdb.ErrNotReady) {\\n366: \\t\\tr.err.typ = errorUnavailable\\n367: \\t}\\n368: \\treturn r\\n369: }\\n370: \\n371: // Register the API's endpoints in the given router.\\n372: func (api *API) Register(r *route.Router) {\\n373: \\twrap := func(f apiFunc) http.HandlerFunc {\\n374: \\t\\thf := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\\n375: \\t\\t\\thttputil.SetCORS(w, api.CORSOrigin, r)\\n376: \\t\\t\\tresult := setUnavailStatusOnTSDBNotReady(f(r))\\n377: \\t\\t\\tif result.finalizer != nil {\\n378: \\t\\t\\t\\tdefer result.finalizer()\\n379: \\t\\t\\t}\\n380: \\t\\t\\tif result.err != nil {\\n381: \\t\\t\\t\\tapi.respondError(w, result.err, result.data)\\n382: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n383: \\t\\t\\t}\\n384: \\n385: \\t\\t\\tif result.data != nil {\\n386: \\t\\t\\t\\tapi.respond(w, r, result.data, result.warnings, r.FormValue(\\\"query\\\"))\\n387: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n388: \\t\\t\\t}\\n389: \\t\\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)\\n390: \\t\\t})\\n391: \\t\\treturn api.ready(httputil.CompressionHandler{\\n392: \\t\\t\\tHandler: hf,\\n393: \\t\\t}.ServeHTTP)\\n394: \\t}\\n395: \\n396: \\twrapAgent := func(f apiFunc) http.HandlerFunc {\\n397: \\t\\treturn wrap(func(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\\n398: \\t\\t\\tif api.isAgent {\\n399: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorExec, errors.New(\\\"unavailable with Prometheus Agent\\\")}, nil, nil}\\n400: \\t\\t\\t}\\n401: \\t\\t\\treturn f(r)\\n402: \\t\\t})\\n403: \\t}\\n404: \\n405: \\tr.Options(\\\"/*path\\\", wrap(api.options))\\n406: \\n407: \\tr.Get(\\\"/query\\\", wrapAgent(api.query))\\n408: \\tr.Post(\\\"/query\\\", wrapAgent(api.query))\\n409: `json:\\\"stats,omitempty\\\"`\\n465: }\\n466: \\n467: func invalidParamError(err error, parameter string) apiFuncResult {\\n468: \\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{\\n469: \\t\\terrorBadData, fmt.Errorf(\\\"invalid parameter %q: %w\\\", parameter, err),\\n470: \\t}, nil, nil}\\n471: }\\n472: \\n473: func (*API) options(*http.Request) apiFuncResult {\\n474: \\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, nil, nil, nil}\\n475: }\\n476: \\n477: func (api *API) query(r *http.Request) (result apiFuncResult) {\\n478: \\tlimit, err := parseLimitParam(r.FormValue(\\\"limit\\\"))\\n479: \\tif err != nil {\\n480: \\t\\treturn invalidParamError(err, \\\"limit\\\")\\n481: \\t}\\n482: \\tts, err := parseTimeParam(r, \\\"time\\\", api.now())\\n483: \\tif err != nil {\\n484: \\t\\treturn invalidParamError(err, \\\"time\\\")\\n485: \\t}\\n486: \\tctx := r.Context()\\n487: \\tif to := r.FormValue(\\\"timeout\\\"); to != \\\"\\\" {\\n488: \\t\\tvar cancel context.CancelFunc\\n489: \\t\\ttimeout, err := parseDuration(to)\\n490: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n491: \\t\\t\\treturn invalidParamError(err, \\\"timeout\\\")\\n492: \\t\\t}\\n493: \\n494: \\t\\tctx, cancel = context.WithDeadline(ctx, api.now().Add(timeout))\\n495: \\t\\tdefer cancel()\\n496: \\t}\\n497: \\n498: \\topts, err := extractQueryOpts(r)\\n499: \\tif err != nil {\\n500: \\t\\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\\n501: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4@ba16cf5a7437eb3b6b71b74557b7a67e1975ad0f/-/blob/web/api/v1/api.go%3FL1-500\"}"

"{\"content\":\"1: // Copyright 2013 The Prometheus Authors\\n2: // Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n3: // you may not use this file except in compliance with the specific language governing permissions and\\n12: // limitations under the License.\\n13: \\n14: package rules\\n15: \\n16: import (\\n17: \\t\\\"context\\\"\\n18: \\t\\\"errors\\\"\\n19: \\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n20: \\t\\\"log/slog\\\"\\n21: \\tmaps0 \\\"maps\\\"\\n22: \\t\\\"net/url\\\"\\n23: \\t\\\"path/filepath\\\"\\n24: \\t\\\"slices\\\"\\n25: \\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n26: \\t\\\"sync\\\"\\n27: \\t\\\"time\\\"\\n28: \\n29: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus\\\"\\n30: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/common/model\\\"\\n31: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/common/promslog\\\"\\n32: \\t\\\"golang.org/x/sync/semaphore\\\"\\n33: \\n34: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/model/labels\\\"\\n35: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/model/rulefmt\\\"\\n36: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/notifier\\\"\\n37: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/promql\\\"\\n38: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/promql/parser\\\"\\n39: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/storage\\\"\\n40: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/util/strutil\\\"\\n41: )\\n42: \\n43: // QueryFunc processes PromQL queries.\\n44: type QueryFunc func(ctx context.Context, q string, t time.Time) (promql.Vector, error)\\n45: \\n46: // EngineQueryFunc returns a new query function that executes instant queries against\\n47: // the given engine.\\n48: // It converts scalar into vector results.\\n49: func EngineQueryFunc(engine promql.QueryEngine, q storage.Queryable) QueryFunc {\\n50: \\treturn func(ctx context.Context, qs string, t time.Time) (promql.Vector, error) {\\n51: \\t\\tq, err := engine.NewInstantQuery(ctx, q, nil, qs, t)\\n52: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n53: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n54: \\t\\t}\\n55: \\t\\tres := q.Exec(ctx)\\n56: \\t\\tif res.Err != nil {\\n57: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, res.Err\\n58: \\t\\t}\\n59: \\t\\tswitch v := res.Value.(type) {\\n60: \\t\\tcase promql.Vector:\\n61: \\t\\t\\treturn v, nil\\n62: \\t\\tcase promql.Scalar:\\n63: \\t\\t\\treturn promql.Vector{promql.Sample{\\n64: \\t\\t\\t\\tT:      v.T,\\n65: \\t\\t\\t\\tF:      v.V,\\n66: \\t\\t\\t\\tMetric: labels.Labels{},\\n67: \\t\\t\\t}}, nil\\n68: \\t\\tdefault:\\n69: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, errors.New(\\\"rule result is not a vector or scalar\\\")\\n70: \\t\\t}\\n71: \\t}\\n72: }\\n73: \\n74: // DefaultEvalIterationFunc is the default implementation of\\n75: // GroupEvalIterationFunc that is periodically invoked to evaluate the rules\\n76: // in a group at a given point in time and updates Group state and metrics\\n77: // accordingly. Custom GroupEvalIterationFunc implementations are recommended\\n78: // to invoke this function as well, to ensure correct Group state and metrics\\n79: // are maintained.\\n80: func DefaultEvalIterationFunc(ctx context.Context, g *Group, evalTimestamp time.Time) {\\n81: \\tg.metrics.IterationsScheduled.WithLabelValues(GroupKey(g.file, g.name)).Inc()\\n82: \\n83: \\tstart := time.Now()\\n84: \\tg.Eval(ctx, evalTimestamp)\\n85: \\ttimeSinceStart := time.Since(start)\\n86: \\n87: \\tg.metrics.IterationDuration.Observe(timeSinceStart.Seconds())\\n88: \\tg.updateRuleEvaluationTimeSum()\\n89: \\tg.setEvaluationTime(timeSinceStart)\\n90: \\tg.setLastEvaluation(start)\\n91: \\tg.setLastEvalTimestamp(evalTimestamp)\\n92: }\\n93: \\n94: // The Manager manages recording and alerting rules.\\n95: type Manager struct {\\n96: \\topts                 *ManagerOptions\\n97: \\tgroups               map[string]*Group\\n98: \\tmtx                  sync.RWMutex\\n99: \\tblock                chan struct{}\\n100: \\tdone                 chan struct{}\\n101: \\trestored             bool\\n102: \\trestoreNewRuleGroups bool\\n103: \\n104: \\tlogger *slog.Logger\\n105: }\\n106: \\n107: // NotifyFunc sends notifications about a set of alerts generated by the given expression.\\n108: type NotifyFunc func(ctx context.Context, expr string, alerts ...*Alert)\\n109: \\n110: // ManagerOptions bundles options for the Manager.\\n111: type ManagerOptions struct {\\n112: \\tNameValidationScheme      model.ValidationScheme\\n113: \\tExternalURL               *url.URL\\n114: \\tQueryFunc                 QueryFunc\\n115: \\tNotifyFunc                NotifyFunc\\n116: \\tContext                   context.Context\\n117: \\tAppendable                storage.Appendable\\n118: \\tQueryable                 storage.Queryable\\n119: \\tLogger                    *slog.Logger\\n120: \\tRegisterer                prometheus.Registerer\\n121: \\tOutageTolerance           time.Duration\\n122: \\tForGracePeriod            time.Duration\\n123: \\tResendDelay               time.Duration\\n124: \\tGroupLoader               GroupLoader\\n125: \\tDefaultRuleQueryOffset    func() time.Duration\\n126: \\tMaxConcurrentEvals        int64\\n127: \\tConcurrentEvalsEnabled    bool\\n128: \\tRuleConcurrencyController RuleConcurrencyController\\n129: \\tRuleDependencyController  RuleDependencyController\\n130: \\t// At present, manager only restores `for` state when manager is newly created which happens\\n131: \\t// during restarts. This flag provides an option to restore the `for` state when new rule groups are\\n132: \\t// added to an existing manager\\n133: \\tRestoreNewRuleGroups bool\\n134: \\n135: \\tMetrics *Metrics\\n136: }\\n137: \\n138: // NewManager returns an implementation of Manager, ready to be started\\n139: // by calling the Run method.\\n140: func NewManager(o *ManagerOptions) *Manager {\\n141: \\tswitch o.NameValidationScheme {\\n142: \\tcase model.UTF8Validation, model.LegacyValidation:\\n143: \\tcase model.UnsetValidation:\\n144: \\t\\to.NameValidationScheme = model.UTF8Validation\\n145: \\tdefault:\\n146: \\t\\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\\\"unrecognized name validation scheme: %s\\\", o.NameValidationScheme))\\n147: \\t}\\n148: \\tif o.Context == nil {\\n149: \\t\\to.Context = context.Background()\\n150: \\t}\\n151: \\n152: \\tif o.Metrics == nil {\\n153: \\t\\to.Metrics = NewGroupMetrics(o.Registerer)\\n154: \\t}\\n155: \\n156: \\tif o.GroupLoader == nil {\\n157: \\t\\to.GroupLoader = FileLoader{}\\n158: \\t}\\n159: \\n160: \\tif o.RuleConcurrencyController == nil {\\n161: \\t\\tif o.ConcurrentEvalsEnabled {\\n162: \\t\\t\\to.RuleConcurrencyController = newRuleConcurrencyController(o.MaxConcurrentEvals)\\n163: \\t\\t} else {\\n164: \\t\\t\\to.RuleConcurrencyController = sequentialRuleEvalController{}\\n165: \\t\\t}\\n166: \\t}\\n167: \\n168: \\tif o.RuleDependencyController == nil {\\n169: \\t\\to.RuleDependencyController = ruleDependencyController{}\\n170: \\t}\\n171: \\n172: \\tif o.Logger == nil {\\n173: \\t\\to.Logger = promslog.NewNopLogger()\\n174: \\t}\\n175: \\n176: \\tm := &Manager{\\n177: \\t\\tgroups:               map[string]*Group{},\\n178: \\t\\topts:                 o,\\n179: \\t\\tblock:                make(chan struct{}),\\n180: \\t\\tdone:                 make(chan struct{}),\\n181: \\t\\tlogger:               o.Logger,\\n182: \\t\\trestoreNewRuleGroups: o.RestoreNewRuleGroups,\\n183: \\t}\\n184: \\n185: \\treturn m\\n186: }\\n187: \\n188: // Run starts processing of the rule manager. It is blocking.\\n189: func (m *Manager) Run() {\\n190: \\tm.logger.Info(\\\"Starting rule manager...\\\")\\n191: \\tm.start()\\n192: \\t<-m.done\\n193: }\\n194: \\n195: func (m *Manager) start() {\\n196: \\tclose(m.block)\\n197: }\\n198: \\n199: // Stop the rule manager's rule evaluation cycles.\\n200: func (m *Manager) Stop() {\\n201: \\tm.mtx.Lock()\\n202: \\tdefer m.mtx.Unlock()\\n203: \\n204: \\tm.logger.Info(\\\"Stopping rule manager...\\\")\\n205: \\n206: \\t// Stop all groups asynchronously, then wait for them to finish.\\n207: \\t// This is faster than stopping and waiting for each group in sequence.\\n208: \\tfor _, eg := range m.groups {\\n209: \\t\\teg.stopAsync()\\n210: \\t}\\n211: \\n212: \\tfor _, eg := range m.groups {\\n213: \\t\\teg.waitStopped()\\n214: \\t}\\n215: \\n216: \\t// Shut down the groups waiting multiple evaluation intervals to write\\n217: \\t// staleness markers.\\n218: \\tclose(m.done)\\n219: \\n220: \\tm.logger.Info(\\\"Rule manager stopped\\\")\\n221: }\\n222: \\n223: // Update the rule manager's state as the config requires. If\\n224: // loading the new rules failed the old rule set is restored.\\n225: // This method will no-op in case the manager is already stopped.\\n226: func (m *Manager) Update(interval time.Duration, files []string, externalLabels labels.Labels, externalURL string, groupEvalIterationFunc GroupEvalIterationFunc) error {\\n227: \\tm.mtx.Lock()\\n228: \\tdefer m.mtx.Unlock()\\n229: \\n230: \\t// We cannot update a stopped manager\\n231: \\tselect {\\n232: \\tcase <-m.done:\\n233: \\t\\treturn nil\\n234: \\tdefault:\\n235: \\t}\\n236: \\n237: \\tgroups, errs := m.LoadGroups(interval, externalLabels, externalURL, groupEvalIterationFunc, false, files...)\\n238: \\n239: \\tif errs != nil {\\n240: \\t\\tfor _, e := range errs {\\n241: \\t\\t\\tm.logger.Error(\\\"loading groups failed\\\", \\\"err\\\", e)\\n242: \\t\\t}\\n243: \\t\\treturn errors.New(\\\"error loading rules, previous rule set restored\\\")\\n244: \\t}\\n245: \\tm.restored = true\\n246: \\n247: \\tvar wg sync.WaitGroup\\n248: \\tfor _, newg := range groups {\\n249: \\t\\t// If there is an old group with the same identifier,\\n250: \\t\\t// check if new group equals with the old group, if yes then skip it.\\n251: \\t\\t// If not equals, stop it and wait for it to finish the current iteration.\\n252: \\t\\t// Then copy it into the new group.\\n253: \\t\\tgn := GroupKey(newg.file, newg.name)\\n254: \\t\\toldg, ok := m.groups[gn]\\n255: \\t\\tdelete(m.groups, gn)\\n256: \\n257: \\t\\tif ok && oldg.Equals(newg) {\\n258: \\t\\t\\tgroups[gn] = oldg\\n259: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n260: \\t\\t}\\n261: \\n262: \\t\\twg.Add(1)\\n263: \\t\\tgo func(newg *Group) {\\n264: \\t\\t\\tif ok {\\n265: \\t\\t\\t\\toldg.stop()\\n266: \\t\\t\\t\\tnewg.CopyState(oldg)\\n267: \\t\\t\\t}\\n268: \\t\\t\\twg.Done()\\n269: \\t\\t\\t// Wait with starting evaluation until the rule manager\\n270: \\t\\t\\t// is told to run. This is necessary to avoid running\\n271: \\t\\t\\t// queries against a bootstrapping storage.\\n272: \\t\\t\\t<-m.block\\n273: \\t\\t\\tnewg.run(m.opts.Context)\\n274: \\t\\t}(newg)\\n275: \\t}\\n276: \\n277: \\t// Stop remaining old groups.\\n278: \\twg.Add(len(m.groups))\\n279: \\tfor n, oldg := range m.groups {\\n280: \\t\\tgo func(n string, g *Group) {\\n281: \\t\\t\\tg.markStale = true\\n282: \\t\\t\\tg.stop()\\n283: \\t\\t\\tif m := g.metrics; m != nil {\\n284: \\t\\t\\t\\tm.IterationsMissed.DeleteLabelValues(n)\\n285: \\t\\t\\t\\tm.IterationsScheduled.DeleteLabelValues(n)\\n286: \\t\\t\\t\\tm.EvalTotal.DeleteLabelValues(n)\\n287: \\t\\t\\t\\tm.EvalFailures.DeleteLabelValues(n)\\n288: \\t\\t\\t\\tm.GroupInterval.DeleteLabelValues(n)\\n289: \\t\\t\\t\\tm.GroupLastEvalTime.DeleteLabelValues(n)\\n290: \\t\\t\\t\\tm.GroupLastDuration.DeleteLabelValues(n)\\n291: \\t\\t\\t\\tm.GroupRules.DeleteLabelValues(n)\\n292: \\t\\t\\t\\tm.GroupSamples.DeleteLabelValues((n))\\n293: \\t\\t\\t}\\n294: \\t\\t\\twg.Done()\\n295: \\t\\t}(n, oldg)\\n296: \\t}\\n297: \\n298: \\twg.Wait()\\n299: \\tm.groups = groups\\n300: \\n301: \\treturn nil\\n302: }\\n303: \\n304: // GroupLoader is responsible for loading rule groups from arbitrary sources and parsing them.\\n305: type GroupLoader interface {\\n306: \\tLoad(identifier string, ignoreUnknownFields bool, nameValidationScheme model.ValidationScheme) (*rulefmt.RuleGroups, []error)\\n307: \\tParse(query string) (parser.Expr, error)\\n308: }\\n309: \\n310: // FileLoader is the default GroupLoader implementation. It defers to rulefmt.ParseFile\\n311: // and parser.ParseExpr.\\n312: type FileLoader struct{}\\n313: \\n314: func (FileLoader) Load(identifier string, ignoreUnknownFields bool, nameValidationScheme model.ValidationScheme) (*rulefmt.RuleGroups, []error) {\\n315: \\treturn rulefmt.ParseFile(identifier, ignoreUnknownFields, nameValidationScheme)\\n316: }\\n317: \\n318: func (FileLoader) Parse(query string) (parser.Expr, error) { return parser.ParseExpr(query) }\\n319: \\n320: // LoadGroups reads groups from a list of files.\\n321: func (m *Manager) LoadGroups(\\n322: \\tinterval time.Duration, externalLabels labels.Labels, externalURL string, groupEvalIterationFunc GroupEvalIterationFunc, ignoreUnknownFields bool, filenames ...string,\\n323: ) (map[string]*Group, []error) {\\n324: \\tgroups := make(map[string]*Group)\\n325: \\n326: \\tshouldRestore := !m.restored || m.restoreNewRuleGroups\\n327: \\n328: \\tfor _, fn := range filenames {\\n329: \\t\\trgs, errs := m.opts.GroupLoader.Load(fn, ignoreUnknownFields, m.opts.NameValidationScheme)\\n330: \\t\\tif errs != nil {\\n331: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, errs\\n332: \\t\\t}\\n333: \\n334: \\t\\tfor _, rg := range rgs.Groups {\\n335: \\t\\t\\titv := interval\\n336: \\t\\t\\tif rg.Interval != 0 {\\n337: \\t\\t\\t\\titv = time.Duration(rg.Interval)\\n338: \\t\\t\\t}\\n339: \\n340: \\t\\t\\trules := make([]Rule, 0, len(rg.Rules))\\n341: \\t\\t\\tfor _, r := range rg.Rules {\\n342: \\t\\t\\t\\texpr, err := m.opts.GroupLoader.Parse(r.Expr)\\n343: \\t\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n344: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil, []error{fmt.Errorf(\\\"%s: %w\\\", fn, err)}\\n345: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n346: \\n347: \\t\\t\\t\\tmLabels := FromMaps(rg.Labels, r.Labels)\\n348: \\n349: \\t\\t\\t\\tif r.Alert != \\\"\\\" {\\n350: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\trules = append(rules, NewAlertingRule(\\n351: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tr.Alert,\\n352: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\texpr,\\n353: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\ttime.Duration(r.For),\\n354: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\ttime.Duration(r.KeepFiringFor),\\n355: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tmLabels,\\n356: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tlabels.FromMap(r.Annotations),\\n357: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\texternalLabels,\\n358: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\texternalURL,\\n359: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t!shouldRestore,\\n360: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tm.logger.With(\\\"alert\\\", r.Alert),\\n361: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t))\\n362: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n363: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n364: \\t\\t\\t\\trules = append(rules, NewRecordingRule(\\n365: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tr.Record,\\n366: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\texpr,\\n367: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tmLabels,\\n368: \\t\\t\\t\\t))\\n369: \\t\\t\\t}\\n370: \\n371: \\t\\t\\t// Check dependencies between rules and store it on the Rule itself.\\n372: \\t\\t\\tm.opts.RuleDependencyController.AnalyseRules(rules)\\n373: \\n374: \\t\\t\\tgroups[GroupKey(fn, rg.Name)] = NewGroup(GroupOptions{\\n375: \\t\\t\\t\\tName:              rg.Name,\\n376: \\t\\t\\t\\tFile:              fn,\\n377: \\t\\t\\t\\tInterval:          itv,\\n378: \\t\\t\\t\\tLimit:             rg.Limit,\\n379: \\t\\t\\t\\tRules:             rules,\\n380: \\t\\t\\t\\tShouldRestore:     shouldRestore,\\n381: \\t\\t\\t\\tOpts:              m.opts,\\n382: \\t\\t\\t\\tQueryOffset:       (*time.Duration)(rg.QueryOffset),\\n383: \\t\\t\\t\\tdone:              m.done,\\n384: \\t\\t\\t\\tEvalIterationFunc: groupEvalIterationFunc,\\n385: \\t\\t\\t})\\n386: \\t\\t}\\n387: \\t}\\n388: \\n389: \\treturn groups, nil\\n390: }\\n391: \\n392: // RuleGroups returns the list of manager's rule groups.\\n393: func (m *Manager) RuleGroups() []*Group {\\n394: \\tm.mtx.RLock()\\n395: \\tdefer m.mtx.RUnlock()\\n396: \\n397: \\trgs := make([]*Group, 0, len(m.groups))\\n398: \\tfor _, g := range m.groups {\\n399: \\t\\trgs = append(rgs, g)\\n400: \\t}\\n401: \\n402: \\tslices.SortFunc(rgs, func(a, b *Group) int {\\n403: \\t\\tfileCompare := strings.Compare(a.file, b.file)\\n404: \\n405: \\t\\t// If its 0, then the file names are the same.\\n406: \\t\\t// Lets look at the group names in that case.\\n407: \\t\\tif fileCompare != 0 {\\n408: \\t\\t\\treturn fileCompare\\n409: \\t\\t}\\n410: \\t\\treturn strings.Compare(a.name, b.name)\\n411: \\t})\\n412: \\n413: \\treturn rgs\\n414: }\\n415: \\n416: // Rules returns the list of the manager's rules.\\n417: func (m *Manager) Rules(matcherSets ...[]*labels.Matcher) []Rule {\\n418: \\tm.mtx.RLock()\\n419: \\tdefer m.mtx.RUnlock()\\n420: \\n421: \\tvar rules []Rule\\n422: \\tfor _, g := range m.groups {\\n423: \\t\\trules = append(rules, g.Rules(matcherSets...)...)\\n424: \\t}\\n425: \\n426: \\treturn rules\\n427: }\\n428: \\n429: // AlertingRules returns the list of the manager's alerting rules.\\n430: func (m *Manager) AlertingRules() []*AlertingRule {\\n431: \\talerts := []*AlertingRule{}\\n432: \\tfor _, rule := range m.Rules() {\\n433: \\t\\tif alertingRule, ok := rule.(*AlertingRule); ok {\\n434: \\t\\t\\talerts = append(alerts, alertingRule)\\n435: \\t\\t}\\n436: \\t}\\n437: \\n438: \\treturn alerts\\n439: }\\n440: \\n441: type Sender interface {\\n442: \\tSend(alerts ...*notifier.Alert)\\n443: }\\n444: \\n445: // SendAlerts implements the rules.NotifyFunc for a Notifier.\\n446: func SendAlerts(s Sender, externalURL string) NotifyFunc {\\n447: \\treturn func(_ context.Context, expr string, alerts ...*Alert) {\\n448: \\t\\tvar res []*notifier.Alert\\n449: \\n450: \\t\\tfor _, alert := range alerts {\\n451: \\t\\t\\ta := &notifier.Alert{\\n452: \\t\\t\\t\\tStartsAt:     alert.FiredAt,\\n453: \\t\\t\\t\\tLabels:       alert.Labels,\\n454: \\t\\t\\t\\tAnnotations:  alert.Annotations,\\n455: \\t\\t\\t\\tGeneratorURL: externalURL + strutil.TableLinkForExpression(expr),\\n456: \\t\\t\\t}\\n457: \\t\\t\\tif !alert.ResolvedAt.IsZero() {\\n458: \\t\\t\\t\\ta.EndsAt = alert.ResolvedAt\\n459: \\t\\t\\t} else {\\n460: \\t\\t\\t\\ta.EndsAt = alert.ValidUntil\\n461: \\t\\t\\t}\\n462: \\t\\t\\tres = append(res, a)\\n463: \\t\\t}\\n464: \\n465: \\t\\tif len(alerts) > 0 {\\n466: \\t\\t\\ts.Send(res...)\\n467: \\t\\t}\\n468: \\t}\\n469: }\\n470: \\n471: // RuleDependencyController controls whether a set of rules have dependencies between each other.\\n472: type RuleDependencyController interface {\\n473: \\t// AnalyseRules analyses dependencies between the input rules. For each rule that it's guaranteed\\n474: \\t// not having any dependants and/or dependency, this function should call Rule.SetDependentRules(...)\\n475: \\t// and/or Rule.SetDependencyRules(...).\\n476: \\tAnalyseRules(rules []Rule)\\n477: }\\n478: \\n479: type ruleDependencyController struct{}\\n480: \\n481: // AnalyseRules implements RuleDependencyController.\\n482: func (ruleDependencyController) AnalyseRules(rules []Rule) {\\n483: \\tdepMap := buildDependencyMap(rules)\\n484: \\n485: \\tif depMap == nil {\\n486: \\t\\treturn\\n487: \\t}\\n488: \\n489: \\tfor _, r := range rules {\\n490: \\t\\tr.SetDependentRules(depMap.dependents(r))\\n491: \\t\\tr.SetDependencyRules(depMap.dependencies(r))\\n492: \\t}\\n493: }\\n494: \\n495: // ConcurrentRules represents a slice of indexes of rules that can be evaluated concurrently.\\n496: type ConcurrentRules []int\\n497: \\n498: // RuleConcurrencyController controls concurrency for rules that are safe to be evaluated concurrently.\\n499: // Its purpose is to bound the amount of concurrency in rule evaluations to avoid overwhelming the Prometheus\\n500: // server with additional query load. Concurrency is controlled globally, not on a per-group basis.\\n501: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4@ba16cf5a7437eb3b6b71b74557b7a67e1975ad0f/-/blob/rules/manager.go%3FL1-500\"}"

\\t\\\"errors\\\"\\n20: \\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n21: \\t\\\"io/fs\\\"\\n22: \\t\\\"math\\\"\\n23: \\t\\\"slices\\\"\\n24: \\t\\\"sort\\\"\\n25: \\t\\\"strconv\\\"\\n26: \\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n27: \\t\\\"testing\\\"\\n28: \\t\\\"time\\\"\\n29: \\n30: \\t\\\"github.com/grafana/regexp\\\"\\n31: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/common/model\\\"\\n32: \\t\\\"github.com/stretchr/testify/require\\\"\\n33: \\n34: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/model/exemplar\\\"\\n35: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/model/histogram\\\"\\n36: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/model/labels\\\"\\n37: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/model/timestamp\\\"\\n38: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/promql\\\"\\n39: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/promql/parser\\\"\\n40: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/promql/parser/posrange\\\"\\n41: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/storage\\\"\\n42: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/util/almost\\\"\\n43: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/util/annotations\\\"\\n44: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/util/convertnhcb\\\"\\n45: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/util/teststorage\\\"\\n46: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/util/testutil\\\"\\n47: )\\n48: \\n49: var (\\n50: \\tpatSpace       = regexp.MustCompile(\\\"[\\\\t ]+\\\")\\n51: \\tpatLoad        = regexp.MustCompile(`^load(?:_(with_nhcb))?\\\\s+(.+?)$`)\\n52: \\tpatEvalInstant = regexp.MustCompile(`^eval(?:_(fail|warn|ordered|info))?\\\\s+instant\\\\s+(?:at\\\\s+(.+?))?\\\\s+(.+)$`)\\n53: \\tpatEvalRange   = regexp.MustCompile(`^eval(?:_(fail|warn|info))?\\\\s+range\\\\s+from\\\\s+(.+)\\\\s+to\\\\s+(.+)\\\\s+step\\\\s+(.+?)\\\\s+(.+)$`)\\n54: \\tpatExpect      = regexp.MustCompile(`^expect\\\\s+(ordered|fail|warn|no_warn|info|no_info)(?:\\\\s+(regex|msg):(.+))?$`)\\n55: \\tpatMatchAny    = regexp.MustCompile(`^.*$`)\\n56: )\\n57: \\n58: const (\\n59: \\tdefaultEpsilon            = 0.000001 // Relative error allowed for sample values.\\n60: \\tDefaultMaxSamplesPerQuery = 10000\\n61: )\\n62: \\n63: type TBRun interface {\\n64: \\ttesting.TB\\n65: \\tRun(string, func(*testing.T)) bool\\n66: }\\n67: \\n68: var testStartTime = time.Unix(0, 0).UTC()\\n69: \\n70: // LoadedStorage returns storage with generated data using the provided load statements.\\n71: // Non-load statements will cause test errors.\\n72: func LoadedStorage(t testutil.T, input string) *teststorage.TestStorage {\\n73: \\ttest, err := newTest(t, input, false, newTestStorage)\\n74: \\trequire.NoError(t, err)\\n75: \\n76: \\tfor _, cmd := range test.cmds {\\n77: \\t\\tswitch cmd.(type) {\\n78: \\t\\tcase *loadCmd:\\n79: \\t\\t\\trequire.NoError(t, test.exec(cmd, nil))\\n80: \\t\\tdefault:\\n81: \\t\\t\\tt.Errorf(\\\"only 'load' commands accepted, got '%s'\\\", cmd)\\n82: \\t\\t}\\n83: \\t}\\n84: \\treturn test.storage.(*teststorage.TestStorage)\\n85: }\\n86: \\n87: // NewTestEngine creates a promql.Engine with enablePerStepStats, lookbackDelta and maxSamples, and returns it.\\n88: func NewTestEngine(tb testing.TB, enablePerStepStats bool, lookbackDelta time.Duration, maxSamples int) *promql.Engine {\\n89: \\treturn NewTestEngineWithOpts(tb, promql.EngineOpts{\\n90: \\t\\tLogger:                   nil,\\n91: \\t\\tReg:                      nil,\\n92: \\t\\tMaxSamples:               maxSamples,\\n93: \\t\\tTimeout:                  100 * time.Second,\\n94: \\t\\tNoStepSubqueryIntervalFn: func(int64) int64 { return durationMilliseconds(1 * time.Minute) },\\n95: \\t\\tEnableAtModifier:         true,\\n96: \\t\\tEnableNegativeOffset:     true,\\n97: \\t\\tEnablePerStepStats:       enablePerStepStats,\\n98: \\t\\tLookbackDelta:            lookbackDelta,\\n99: \\t\\tEnableDelayedNameRemoval: true,\\n100: \\t})\\n101: }\\n102: \\n103: // NewTestEngineWithOpts creates a promql.Engine with opts and returns it.\\n104: func NewTestEngineWithOpts(tb testing.TB, opts promql.EngineOpts) *promql.Engine {\\n105: \\ttb.Helper()\\n106: \\tng := promql.NewEngine(opts)\\n107: \\ttb.Cleanup(func() {\\n108: \\t\\trequire.NoError(tb, ng.Close())\\n109: \\t})\\n110: \\treturn ng\\n111: }\\n112: \\n113: // RunBuiltinTests runs an acceptance test suite against the provided engine.\\n114: func RunBuiltinTests(t TBRun, engine promql.QueryEngine) {\\n115: \\tRunBuiltinTestsWithStorage(t, engine, newTestStorage)\\n116: }\\n117: \\n118: // RunBuiltinTestsWithStorage runs an acceptance test suite against the provided engine and storage.\\n119: func RunBuiltinTestsWithStorage(t TBRun, engine promql.QueryEngine, newStorage func(testutil.T) storage.Storage) {\\n120: \\tt.Cleanup(func() {\\n121: \\t\\tparser.EnableExperimentalFunctions = false\\n122: \\t\\tparser.ExperimentalDurationExpr = false\\n123: \\t})\\n124: \\tparser.EnableExperimentalFunctions = true\\n125: \\tparser.ExperimentalDurationExpr = true\\n126: \\n127: \\tfiles, err := fs.Glob(testsFs, \\\"*/*.test\\\")\\n128: \\trequire.NoError(t, err)\\n129: \\n130: \\tfor _, fn := range files {\\n131: \\t\\tt.Run(fn, func(t *testing.T) {\\n132: \\t\\t\\tcontent, err := fs.ReadFile(testsFs, fn)\\n133: \\t\\t\\trequire.NoError(t, err)\\n134: \\t\\t\\tRunTestWithStorage(t, string(content), engine, newStorage)\\n135: \\t\\t})\\n136: \\t}\\n137: }\\n138: \\n139: // RunTest parses and runs the test against the provided engine.\\n140: func RunTest(t testutil.T, input string, engine promql.QueryEngine) {\\n141: \\tRunTestWithStorage(t, input, engine, newTestStorage)\\n142: }\\n143: \\n144: // RunTestWithStorage parses and runs the test against the provided engine and storage.\\n145: func RunTestWithStorage(t testutil.T, input string, engine promql.QueryEngine, newStorage func(testutil.T) storage.Storage) {\\n146: \\trequire.NoError(t, runTest(t, input, engine, newStorage, false))\\n147: }\\n148: \\n149: // testTest allows tests to be run in \\\"test-the-test\\\" mode (true for\\n150: // testingMode). This is a special mode for testing test code execution itself.\\n151: func testTest(t testutil.T, input string, engine promql.QueryEngine) error {\\n152: \\treturn runTest(t, input, engine, newTestStorage, true)\\n153: }\\n154: \\n155: func runTest(t testutil.T, input string, engine promql.QueryEngine, newStorage func(testutil.T) storage.Storage, testingMode bool) error {\\n156: \\ttest, err := newTest(t, input, testingMode, newStorage)\\n157: \\n158: \\t// Why do this before checking err? newTest() can create the test storage and then return an error,\\n159: \\t// and we want to make sure to clean that up to avoid leaking goroutines.\\n160: \\tdefer func() {\\n161: \\t\\tif test == nil {\\n162: \\t\\t\\treturn\\n163: \\t\\t}\\n164: \\t\\tif test.storage != nil {\\n165: \\t\\t\\ttest.storage.Close()\\n166: \\t\\t}\\n167: \\t\\tif test.cancelCtx != nil {\\n168: \\t\\t\\ttest.cancelCtx()\\n169: \\t\\t}\\n170: \\t}()\\n171: \\n172: \\tif err != nil {\\n173: \\t\\treturn err\\n174: \\t}\\n175: \\n176: \\tfor _, cmd := range test.cmds {\\n177: \\t\\tif err := test.exec(cmd, engine); err != nil {\\n178: \\t\\t\\t// TODO(fabxc): aggregate command errors, yield diffs for result\\n179: \\t\\t\\t// comparison errors.\\n180: \\t\\t\\treturn err\\n181: \\t\\t}\\n182: \\t}\\n183: \\n184: \\treturn nil\\n185: }\\n186: \\n187: // test is a sequence of read and write commands that are run\\n188: // against a test storage.\\n189: type test struct {\\n190: \\ttestutil.T\\n191: \\t// testingMode distinguishes between normal execution and test-execution mode.\\n192: \\ttestingMode bool\\n193: \\n194: \\tcmds []testCommand\\n195: \\n196: \\topen    func(testutil.T) storage.Storage\\n197: \\tstorage storage.Storage\\n198: \\n199: \\tcontext   context.Context\\n200: \\tcancelCtx context.CancelFunc\\n201: }\\n202: \\n203: // newTest returns an initialized empty Test.\\n204: func newTest(t testutil.T, input string, testingMode bool, newStorage func(testutil.T) storage.Storage) (*test, error) {\\n205: \\ttest := &test{\\n206: \\t\\tT:           t,\\n207: \\t\\tcmds:        []testCommand{},\\n208: \\t\\ttestingMode: testingMode,\\n209: \\t\\topen:        newStorage,\\n210: \\t}\\n211: \\terr := test.parse(input)\\n212: \\ttest.clear()\\n213: \\n214: \\treturn test, err\\n215: }\\n216: \\n217: func newTestStorage(t testutil.T) storage.Storage { return teststorage.New(t) }\\n218: \\n219: //go:embed testdata\\n220: var testsFs embed.FS\\n221: \\n222: func raise(line int, format string, v ...any) error {\\n223: \\treturn &parser.ParseErr{\\n224: \\t\\tLineOffset: line,\\n225: \\t\\tErr:        fmt.Errorf(format, v...),\\n226: \\t}\\n227: }\\n228: \\n229: func parseLoad(lines []string, i int) (int, *loadCmd, error) {\\n230: \\tif !patLoad.MatchString(lines[i]) {\\n231: \\t\\treturn i, nil, raise(i, \\\"invalid load command. (load[_with_nhcb] <step:duration>)\\\")\\n232: \\t}\\n233: \\tparts := patLoad.FindStringSubmatch(lines[i])\\n234: \\tvar (\\n235: \\t\\twithNHCB = parts[1] == \\\"with_nhcb\\\"\\n236: \\t\\tstep     = parts[2]\\n237: \\t)\\n238: \\tgap, err := model.ParseDuration(step)\\n239: \\tif err != nil {\\n240: \\t\\treturn i, nil, raise(i, \\\"invalid step definition %q: %s\\\", step, err)\\n241: \\t}\\n242: \\tcmd := newLoadCmd(time.Duration(gap), withNHCB)\\n243: \\tfor i+1 < len(lines) {\\n244: \\t\\ti++\\n245: \\t\\tdefLine := lines[i]\\n246: \\t\\tif len(defLine) == 0 {\\n247: \\t\\t\\ti--\\n248: \\t\\t\\tbreak\\n249: \\t\\t}\\n250: \\t\\tmetric, vals, err := parseSeries(defLine, i)\\n251: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n252: \\t\\t\\treturn i, nil, err\\n253: \\t\\t}\\n254: \\t\\tcmd.set(metric, vals...)\\n255: \\t}\\n256: \\treturn i, cmd, nil\\n257: }\\n258: \\n259: func parseSeries(defLine string, line int) (labels.Labels, []parser.SequenceValue, error) {\\n260: \\tmetric, vals, err := parser.ParseSeriesDesc(defLine)\\n261: \\tif err != nil {\\n262: \\t\\tparser.EnrichParseError(err, func(parseErr *parser.ParseErr) {\\n263: \\t\\t\\tparseErr.LineOffset = line\\n264: \\t\\t})\\n265: \\t\\treturn labels.Labels{}, nil, err\\n266: \\t}\\n267: \\treturn metric, vals, nil\\n268: }\\n269: \\n270: func parseExpect(defLine string) (expectCmdType, expectCmd, error) {\\n271: \\texpectParts := patExpect.FindStringSubmatch(strings.TrimSpace(defLine))\\n272: \\texpCmd := expectCmd{}\\n273: \\tif expectParts == nil {\\n274: \\t\\treturn 0, expCmd, errors.New(\\\"invalid expect statement, must match `expect <type> <match_type>: <string>` format\\\")\\n275: \\t}\\n276: \\tvar (\\n277: \\t\\tmode            = expectParts[1]\\n278: \\t\\thasOptionalPart = expectParts[2] != \\\"\\\"\\n279: \\t)\\n280: \\texpectType, ok := expectTypeStr[mode]\\n281: \\tif !ok {\\n282: \\t\\treturn 0, expCmd, fmt.Errorf(\\\"invalid expected error/annotation type %s\\\", mode)\\n283: \\t}\\n284: \\tif hasOptionalPart {\\n285: \\t\\tswitch expectParts[2] {\\n286: \\t\\tcase \\\"msg\\\":\\n287: \\t\\t\\texpCmd.message = strings.TrimSpace(expectParts[3])\\n288: \\t\\tcase \\\"regex\\\":\\n289: \\t\\t\\tpattern := strings.TrimSpace(expectParts[3])\\n290: \\t\\t\\tregex, err := regexp.Compile(pattern)\\n291: \\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n292: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn 0, expCmd, fmt.Errorf(\\\"invalid regex %s for %s\\\", pattern, mode)\\n293: \\t\\t\\t}\\n294: \\t\\t\\texpCmd.regex = regex\\n295: \\t\\tdefault:\\n296: \\t\\t\\treturn 0, expCmd, fmt.Errorf(\\\"invalid token %s after %s\\\", expectParts[2], mode)\\n297: \\t\\t}\\n298: \\t} else {\\n299: \\t\\texpCmd.regex = patMatchAny\\n300: \\t}\\n301: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4@ba16cf5a7437eb3b6b71b74557b7a67e1975ad0f/-/blob/promql/promqltest/test.go%3FL1-300\"}"
54. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "grep -n \"promql.QueryEngine\\|QueryEngine\" /dev/stdin << 'EOF'\n114: func RunBuiltinTests(t TBRun, engine promql.QueryEngine) {\n119: func RunBuiltinTestsWithStorage(t TBRun, engine promql.QueryEngine, newStorage func(testutil.T) storage.Storage) {\n140: func RunTest(t testutil.T, input string, engine promql.QueryEngine) {\n145: func RunTestWithStorage(t testutil.T, input string, engine promql.QueryEngine, newStorage func(testutil.T) storage.Storage) {\n151: func testTest(t testutil.T, input string, engine promql.QueryEngine) error {\n155: func runTest(t testutil.T, input string, engine promql.QueryEngine, newStorage func(testutil.T) storage.Storage, testingMode bool) error {\nEOF\n",
  "description": "Check test.go lines that use QueryEngine"
}
Output
