[][]EvalSeriesHelper, enh *EvalNodeHelper) (Vector, annotations.Annotations) {\n2209: \t\tif vs.Timestamp != nil {\n2210: \t\t\t// This is a special case for \"timestamp()\" when the @ modifier is used, to ensure that\n2211: \t\t\t// we return a point for each time step in this case.\n2212: \t\t\t// See https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/issues/8433.\n2213: \t\t\tvs.Offset = time.Duration(enh.Ts-*vs.Timestamp) * time.Millisecond\n2214: \t\t}\n2215: \n2216: \t\tvec := make(Vector, 0, len(vs.Series))\n2217: \t\tfor i, s := range vs.Series {\n2218: \t\t\tit := seriesIterators[i]\n2219: \t\t\tt, _, _, ok := ev.vectorSelectorSingle(it, vs.Offset, enh.Ts)\n2220: \t\t\tif !ok {\n2221: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n2222: \t\t\t}\n2223: \n2224: \t\t\t// Note that we ignore the sample values because call only cares about the timestamp.\n2225: \t\t\tvec = append(vec, Sample{\n2226: \t\t\t\tMetric: s.Labels(),\n2227: \t\t\t\tT:      t,\n2228: \t\t\t})\n2229: \n2230: \t\t\tev.currentSamples++\n2231: \t\t\tev.samplesStats.IncrementSamplesAtTimestamp(enh.Ts, 1)\n2232: \t\t\tif ev.currentSamples > ev.maxSamples {\n2233: \t\t\t\tev.error(ErrTooManySamples(env))\n2234: \t\t\t}\n2235: \t\t}\n2236: \t\tev.samplesStats.UpdatePeak(ev.currentSamples)\n2237: \t\tvec, annos := call([]Vector{vec}, nil, e.Args, enh)\n2238: \t\treturn vec, ws.Merge(annos)\n2239: \t})\n2240: }\n2241: \n2242: // vectorSelectorSingle evaluates an instant vector for the iterator of one time series.\n2243: func (ev *evaluator) vectorSelectorSingle(it *storage.MemoizedSeriesIterator, offset time.Duration, ts int64) (\n2244: \tint64, float64, *histogram.FloatHistogram, bool,\n2245: ) {\n2246: \trefTime := ts - durationMilliseconds(offset)\n2247: \tvar t int64\n2248: \tvar v float64\n2249: \tvar h *histogram.FloatHistogram\n2250: \n2251: \tvalueType := it.Seek(refTime)\n2252: \tswitch valueType {\n2253: \tcase chunkenc.ValNone:\n2254: \t\tif it.Err() != nil {\n2255: \t\t\tev.error(it.Err())\n2256: \t\t}\n2257: \tcase chunkenc.ValFloat:\n2258: \t\tt, v = it.At()\n2259: \tcase chunkenc.ValFloatHistogram:\n2260: \t\tt, h = it.AtFloatHistogram()\n2261: \tdefault:\n2262: \t\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\"unknown value type %v\", valueType))\n2263: \t}\n2264: \tif valueType == chunkenc.ValNone || t > refTime {\n2265: \t\tvar ok bool\n2266: \t\tt, v, h, ok = it.PeekPrev()\n2267: \t\tif !ok || t <= refTime-durationMilliseconds(ev.lookbackDelta) {\n2268: \t\t\treturn 0, 0, nil, false\n2269: \t\t}\n2270: \t}\n2271: \tif value.IsStaleNaN(v) || (h != nil && value.IsStaleNaN(h.Sum)) {\n2272: \t\treturn 0, 0, nil, false\n2273: \t}\n2274: \treturn t, v, h, true\n2275: }\n2276: \n2277: var (\n2278: \tfPointPool zeropool.Pool[[]FPoint]\n2279: \thPointPool zeropool.Pool[[]HPoint]\n2280: \n2281: \t// matrixSelectorHPool holds reusable histogram slices used by the matrix\n2282: \t// selector. The key difference between this pool and the hPointPool is that\n2283: \t// slices returned by this pool should never hold multiple copies of the same\n2284: \t// histogram pointer since histogram objects are reused across query evaluation\n2285: \t// steps.\n2286: \tmatrixSelectorHPool zeropool.Pool[[]HPoint]\n2287: )\n2288: \n2289: func getFPointSlice(sz int) []FPoint {\n2290: \tif p := fPointPool.Get(); p != nil {\n2291: \t\treturn p\n2292: \t}\n2293: \n2294: \tif sz > maxPointsSliceSize {\n2295: \t\tsz = maxPointsSliceSize\n2296: \t}\n2297: \n2298: \treturn make([]FPoint, 0, sz)\n2299: }\n2300: \n2301: // putFPointSlice will return a FPoint slice of size max(maxPointsSliceSize, sz).\n2302: // This function is called with an estimated size which often can be over-estimated.\n2303: func putFPointSlice(p []FPoint) {\n2304: \tif p != nil {\n2305: \t\tfPointPool.Put(p[:0])\n2306: \t}\n2307: }\n2308: \n2309: // getHPointSlice will return a HPoint slice of size max(maxPointsSliceSize, sz).\n2310: // This function is called with an estimated size which often can be over-estimated.\n2311: func getHPointSlice(sz int) []HPoint {\n2312: \tif p := hPointPool.Get(); p != nil {\n2313: \t\treturn p\n2314: \t}\n2315: \n2316: \tif sz > maxPointsSliceSize {\n2317: \t\tsz = maxPointsSliceSize\n2318: \t}\n2319: \n2320: \treturn make([]HPoint, 0, sz)\n2321: }\n2322: \n2323: func putHPointSlice(p []HPoint) {\n2324: \tif p != nil {\n2325: \t\thPointPool.Put(p[:0])\n2326: \t}\n2327: }\n2328: \n2329: func getMatrixSelectorHPoints() []HPoint {\n2330: \tif p := matrixSelectorHPool.Get(); p != nil {\n2331: \t\treturn p\n2332: \t}\n2333: \n2334: \treturn make([]HPoint, 0, matrixSelectorSliceSize)\n2335: }\n2336: \n2337: func putMatrixSelectorHPointSlice(p []HPoint) {\n2338: \tif p != nil {\n2339: \t\tmatrixSelectorHPool.Put(p[:0])\n2340: \t}\n2341: }\n2342: \n2343: // matrixSelector evaluates a *parser.MatrixSelector expression.\n2344: func (ev *evaluator) matrixSelector(ctx context.Context, node *parser.MatrixSelector) (Matrix, annotations.Annotations) {\n2345: \tvar (\n2346: \t\tvs = node.VectorSelector.(*parser.VectorSelector)\n2347: \n2348: \t\toffset = durationMilliseconds(vs.Offset)\n2349: \t\tmaxt   = ev.startTimestamp - offset\n2350: \t\tmint   = maxt - durationMilliseconds(node.Range)\n2351: \t\tmatrix = make(Matrix, 0, len(vs.Series))\n2352: \n2353: \t\tit = storage.NewBuffer(durationMilliseconds(node.Range))\n2354: \t)\n2355: \tws, err := checkAndExpandSeriesSet(ctx, node)\n2356: \tif err != nil {\n2357: \t\tev.error(errWithWarnings{fmt.Errorf(\"expanding series: %w\", err), ws})\n2358: \t}\n2359: \n2360: \tvar chkIter chunkenc.Iterator\n2361: \tseries := vs.Series\n2362: \tfor i, s := range series {\n2363: \t\tif err := contextDone(ctx, \"expression evaluation\"); err != nil {\n2364: \t\t\tev.error(err)\n2365: \t\t}\n2366: \t\tchkIter = s.Iterator(chkIter)\n2367: \t\tit.Reset(chkIter)\n2368: \t\tss := Series{\n2369: \t\t\tMetric: series[i].Labels(),\n2370: \t\t}\n2371: \n2372: \t\tss.Floats, ss.Histograms = ev.matrixIterSlice(it, mint, maxt, nil, nil)\n2373: \t\ttotalSize := int64(len(ss.Floats)) + int64(totalHPointSize(ss.Histograms))\n2374: \t\tev.samplesStats.IncrementSamplesAtTimestamp(ev.startTimestamp, totalSize)\n2375: \n2376: \t\tif totalSize > 0 {\n2377: \t\t\tmatrix = append(matrix, ss)\n2378: \t\t} else {\n2379: \t\t\tputFPointSlice(ss.Floats)\n2380: \t\t\tputHPointSlice(ss.Histograms)\n2381: \t\t}\n2382: \t}\n2383: \treturn matrix, ws\n2384: }\n2385: \n2386: // matrixIterSlice populates a matrix vector covering the requested range for a\n2387: // single time series, with points retrieved from an iterator.\n2388: //\n2389: // As an optimization, the matrix vector may already contain points of the same\n2390: // time series from the evaluation of an earlier step (with lower mint and maxt\n2391: // values). Any such points falling before mint are discarded; points that fall\n2392: // into the [mint, maxt] range are retained; only points with later timestamps\n2393: // are populated from the iterator.\n2394: func (ev *evaluator) matrixIterSlice(\n2395: \tit *storage.BufferedSeriesIterator, mint, maxt int64,\n2396: \tfloats []FPoint, histograms []HPoint,\n2397: ) ([]FPoint, []HPoint) {\n2398: \tmintFloats, mintHistograms := mint, mint\n2399: \n2400: \t// First floats...\n2401: \tif len(floats) > 0 && floats[len(floats)-1].T > mint {\n2402: \t\t// There is an overlap between previous and current ranges, retain common\n2403: \t\t// points. In most such cases:\n2404: \t\t//   (a) the overlap is significantly larger than the eval step; and/or\n2405: \t\t//   (b) the number of samples is relatively small.\n2406: \t\t// so a linear search will be as fast as a binary search.\n2407: \t\tvar drop int\n2408: \t\tfor drop = 0; floats[drop].T <= mint; drop++ {\n2409: \t\t}\n2410: \t\tev.currentSamples -= drop\n2411: \t\tcopy(floats, floats[drop:])\n2412: \t\tfloats = floats[:len(floats)-drop]\n2413: \t\t// Only append points with timestamps after the last timestamp we have.\n2414: \t\tmintFloats = floats[len(floats)-1].T\n2415: \t} else {\n2416: \t\tev.currentSamples -= len(floats)\n2417: \t\tif floats != nil {\n2418: \t\t\tfloats = floats[:0]\n2419: \t\t}\n2420: \t}\n2421: \n2422: \t// ...then the same for histograms. TODO(beorn7): Use generics?\n2423: \tif len(histograms) > 0 && histograms[len(histograms)-1].T > mint {\n2424: \t\t// There is an overlap between previous and current ranges, retain common\n2425: \t\t// points. In most such cases:\n2426: \t\t//   (a) the overlap is significantly larger than the eval step; and/or\n2427: \t\t//   (b) the number of samples is relatively small.\n2428: \t\t// so a linear search will be as fast as a binary search.\n2429: \t\tvar drop int\n2430: \t\tfor drop = 0; histograms[drop].T <= mint; drop++ {\n2431: \t\t}\n2432: \t\t// Rotate the buffer around the drop index so that points before mint can be\n2433: \t\t// reused to store new histograms.\n2434: \t\ttail := make([]HPoint, drop)\n2435: \t\tcopy(tail, histograms[:drop])\n2436: \t\tcopy(histograms, histograms[drop:])\n2437: \t\tcopy(histograms[len(histograms)-drop:], tail)\n2438: \t\thistograms = histograms[:len(histograms)-drop]\n2439: \t\tev.currentSamples -= totalHPointSize(histograms)\n2440: \t\t// Only append points with timestamps after the last timestamp we have.\n2441: \t\tmintHistograms = histograms[len(histograms)-1].T\n2442: \t} else {\n2443: \t\tev.currentSamples -= totalHPointSize(histograms)\n2444: \t\tif histograms != nil {\n2445: \t\t\thistograms = histograms[:0]\n2446: \t\t}\n2447: \t}\n2448: \n2449: \tif mint == maxt {\n2450: \t\t// Empty range: return the empty slices.\n2451: \t\treturn floats, histograms\n2452: \t}\n2453: \n2454: \tsoughtValueType := it.Seek(maxt)\n2455: \tif soughtValueType == chunkenc.ValNone {\n2456: \t\tif it.Err() != nil {\n2457: \t\t\tev.error(it.Err())\n2458: \t\t}\n2459: \t}\n2460: \n2461: \tbuf := it.Buffer()\n2462: loop:\n2463: \tfor {\n2464: \t\tswitch buf.Next() {\n2465: \t\tcase chunkenc.ValNone:\n2466: \t\t\tbreak loop\n2467: \t\tcase chunkenc.ValFloatHistogram, chunkenc.ValHistogram:\n2468: \t\t\tt := buf.AtT()\n2469: \t\t\t// Values in the buffer are guaranteed to be smaller than maxt.\n2470: \t\t\tif t > mintHistograms {\n2471: \t\t\t\tif histograms == nil {\n2472: \t\t\t\t\thistograms = getMatrixSelectorHPoints()\n2473: \t\t\t\t}\n2474: \t\t\t\tn := len(histograms)\n2475: \t\t\t\tif n < cap(histograms) {\n2476: \t\t\t\t\thistograms = histograms[:n+1]\n2477: \t\t\t\t} else {\n2478: \t\t\t\t\thistograms = append(histograms, HPoint{H: &histogram.FloatHistogram{}})\n2479: \t\t\t\t}\n2480: \t\t\t\thistograms[n].T, histograms[n].H = buf.AtFloatHistogram(histograms[n].H)\n2481: \t\t\t\tif value.IsStaleNaN(histograms[n].H.Sum) {\n2482: \t\t\t\t\thistograms = histograms[:n]\n2483: \t\t\t\t\tcontinue loop\n2484: \t\t\t\t}\n2485: \t\t\t\tev.currentSamples += histograms[n].size()\n2486: \t\t\t\tif ev.currentSamples > ev.maxSamples {\n2487: \t\t\t\t\tev.error(ErrTooManySamples(env))\n2488: \t\t\t\t}\n2489: \t\t\t}\n2490: \t\tcase chunkenc.ValFloat:\n2491: \t\t\tt, f := buf.At()\n2492: \t\t\tif value.IsStaleNaN(f) {\n2493: \t\t\t\tcontinue loop\n2494: \t\t\t}\n2495: \t\t\t// Values in the buffer are guaranteed to be smaller than maxt.\n2496: \t\t\tif t > mintFloats {\n2497: \t\t\t\tev.currentSamples++\n2498: \t\t\t\tif ev.currentSamples > ev.maxSamples {\n2499: \t\t\t\t\tev.error(ErrTooManySamples(env))\n2500: \t\t\t\t}\n2501: \t\t\t\tif floats == nil {\n2502: \t\t\t\t\tfloats = getFPointSlice(16)\n2503: \t\t\t\t}\n2504: \t\t\t\tfloats = append(floats, FPoint{T: t, F: f})\n2505: \t\t\t}\n2506: \t\t}\n2507: \t}\n2508: \t// The sought sample might also be in the range.\n2509: \tswitch soughtValueType {\n2510: \tcase chunkenc.ValFloatHistogram, chunkenc.ValHistogram:\n2511: \t\tif it.AtT() != maxt {\n2512: \t\t\tbreak\n2513: \t\t}\n2514: \t\tif histograms == nil {\n2515: \t\t\thistograms = getMatrixSelectorHPoints()\n2516: \t\t}\n2517: \t\tn := len(histograms)\n2518: \t\tif n < cap(histograms) {\n2519: \t\t\thistograms = histograms[:n+1]\n2520: \t\t} else {\n2521: \t\t\thistograms = append(histograms, HPoint{H: &histogram.FloatHistogram{}})\n2522: \t\t}\n2523: \t\tif histograms[n].H == nil {\n2524: \t\t\t// Make sure to pass non-nil H to AtFloatHistogram so that it does a deep-copy.\n2525: \t\t\t// Not an issue in the loop above since that uses an intermediate buffer.\n2526: \t\t\thistograms[n].H = &histogram.FloatHistogram{}\n2527: \t\t}\n2528: \t\thistograms[n].T, histograms[n].H = it.AtFloatHistogram(histograms[n].H)\n2529: \t\tif value.IsStaleNaN(histograms[n].H.Sum) {\n2530: \t\t\thistograms = histograms[:n]\n2531: \t\t\tbreak\n2532: \t\t}\n2533: \t\tev.currentSamples += histograms[n].size()\n2534: \t\tif ev.currentSamples > ev.maxSamples {\n2535: \t\t\tev.error(ErrTooManySamples(env))\n2536: \t\t}\n2537: \n2538: \tcase chunkenc.ValFloat:\n2539: \t\tt, f := it.At()\n2540: \t\tif t == maxt && !value.IsStaleNaN(f) {\n2541: \t\t\tev.currentSamples++\n2542: \t\t\tif ev.currentSamples > ev.maxSamples {\n2543: \t\t\t\tev.error(ErrTooManySamples(env))\n2544: \t\t\t}\n2545: \t\t\tif floats == nil {\n2546: \t\t\t\tfloats = getFPointSlice(16)\n2547: \t\t\t}\n2548: \t\t\tfloats = append(floats, FPoint{T: t, F: f})\n2549: \t\t}\n2550: \t}\n2551: \tev.samplesStats.UpdatePeak(ev.currentSamples)\n2552: \treturn floats, histograms\n2553: }\n2554: \n2555: func (*evaluator) VectorAnd(lhs, rhs Vector, matching *parser.VectorMatching, lhsh, rhsh []EvalSeriesHelper, enh *EvalNodeHelper) Vector {\n2556: \tif matching.Card != parser.CardManyToMany {\n2557: \t\tpanic(\"set operations must only use many-to-many matching\")\n2558: \t}\n2559: \tif len(lhs) == 0 || len(rhs) == 0 {\n2560: \t\treturn nil // Short-circuit: AND with nothing is nothing.\n2561: \t}\n2562: \n2563: \t// The set of signatures for the right-hand side Vector.\n2564: \trightSigs := map[string]struct{}{}\n2565: \t// Add all rhs samples to a map so we can easily find matches later.\n2566: \tfor _, sh := range rhsh {\n2567: \t\trightSigs[sh.signature] = struct{}{}\n2568: \t}\n2569: \n2570: \tfor i, ls := range lhs {\n2571: \t\t// If there's a matching entry in the right-hand side Vector, add the sample.\n2572: \t\tif _, ok := rightSigs[lhsh[i].signature]; ok {\n2573: \t\t\tenh.Out = append(enh.Out, ls)\n2574: \t\t}\n2575: \t}\n2576: \treturn enh.Out\n2577: }\n2578: \n2579: func (*evaluator) VectorOr(lhs, rhs Vector, matching *parser.VectorMatching, lhsh, rhsh []EvalSeriesHelper, enh *EvalNodeHelper) Vector {\n2580: \tswitch {\n2581: \tcase matching.Card != parser.CardManyToMany:\n2582: \t\tpanic(\"set operations must only use many-to-many matching\")\n2583: \tcase len(lhs) == 0: // Short-circuit.\n2584: \t\tenh.Out = append(enh.Out, rhs...)\n2585: \t\treturn enh.Out\n2586: \tcase len(rhs) == 0:\n2587: \t\tenh.Out = append(enh.Out, lhs...)\n2588: \t\treturn enh.Out\n2589: \t}\n2590: \n2591: \tleftSigs := map[string]struct{}{}\n2592: \t// Add everything from the left-hand-side Vector.\n2593: \tfor i, ls := range lhs {\n2594: \t\tleftSigs[lhsh[i].signature] = struct{}{}\n2595: \t\tenh.Out = append(enh.Out, ls)\n2596: \t}\n2597: \t// Add all right-hand side elements which have not been added from the left-hand side.\n2598: \tfor j, rs := range rhs {\n2599: \t\tif _, ok := leftSigs[rhsh[j].signature]; !ok {\n2600: \t\t\tenh.Out = append(enh.Out, rs)\n2601: \t\t}\n2602: \t}\n2603: \treturn enh.Out\n2604: }\n2605: \n2606: func (*evaluator) VectorUnless(lhs, rhs Vector, matching *parser.VectorMatching, lhsh, rhsh []EvalSeriesHelper, enh *EvalNodeHelper) Vector {\n2607: \tif matching.Card != parser.CardManyToMany {\n2608: \t\tpanic(\"set operations must only use many-to-many matching\")\n2609: \t}\n2610: \t// Short-circuit: empty rhs means we will return everything in lhs;\n2611: \t// empty lhs means we will return empty - don't need to build a map.\n2612: \tif len(lhs) == 0 || len(rhs) == 0 {\n2613: \t\tenh.Out = append(enh.Out, lhs...)\n2614: \t\treturn enh.Out\n2615: \t}\n2616: \n2617: \trightSigs := map[string]struct{}{}\n2618: \tfor _, sh := range rhsh {\n2619: \t\trightSigs[sh.signature] = struct{}{}\n2620: \t}\n2621: \n2622: \tfor i, ls := range lhs {\n2623: \t\tif _, ok := rightSigs[lhsh[i].signature]; !ok {\n2624: \t\t\tenh.Out = append(enh.Out, ls)\n2625: \t\t}\n2626: \t}\n2627: \treturn enh.Out\n2628: }\n2629: \n2630: // VectorBinop evaluates a binary operation between two Vectors, excluding set operators.\n2631: func (ev *evaluator) VectorBinop(op parser.ItemType, lhs, rhs Vector, matching *parser.VectorMatching, returnBool bool, lhsh, rhsh []EvalSeriesHelper, enh *EvalNodeHelper, pos posrange.PositionRange) (Vector, error) {\n2632: \tif matching.Card == parser.CardManyToMany {\n2633: \t\tpanic(\"many-to-many only allowed for set operators\")\n2634: \t}\n2635: \tif len(lhs) == 0 || len(rhs) == 0 {\n2636: \t\treturn nil, nil // Short-circuit: nothing is going to match.\n2637: \t}\n2638: \n2639: \t// The control flow below handles one-to-one or many-to-one matching.\n2640: \t// For one-to-many, swap sidedness and account for the swap when calculating\n2641: \t// values.\n2642: \tif matching.Card == parser.CardOneToMany {\n2643: \t\tlhs, rhs = rhs, lhs\n2644: \t\tlhsh, rhsh = rhsh, lhsh\n2645: \t}\n2646: \n2647: \t// All samples from the rhs hashed by the matching label/values.\n2648: \tif enh.rightSigs == nil {\n2649: \t\tenh.rightSigs = make(map[string]Sample, len(enh.Out))\n2650: \t} else {\n2651: \t\tfor k := range enh.rightSigs {\n2652: \t\t\tdelete(enh.rightSigs, k)\n2653: \t\t}\n2654: \t}\n2655: \trightSigs := enh.rightSigs\n2656: \n2657: \t// Add all rhs samples to a map so we can easily find matches later.\n2658: \tfor i, rs := range rhs {\n2659: \t\tsig := rhsh[i].signature\n2660: \t\t// The rhs is guaranteed to be the 'one' side. Having multiple samples\n2661: \t\t// with the same signature means that the matching is many-to-many.\n2662: \t\tif duplSample, found := rightSigs[sig]; found {\n2663: \t\t\t// oneSide represents which side of the vector represents the 'one' in the many-to-one relationship.\n2664: \t\t\toneSide := \"right\"\n2665: \t\t\tif matching.Card == parser.CardOneToMany {\n2666: \t\t\t\toneSide = \"left\"\n2667: \t\t\t}\n2668: \t\t\tmatchedLabels := rs.Metric.MatchLabels(matching.On, matching.MatchingLabels...)\n2669: \t\t\t// Many-to-many matching not allowed.\n2670: \t\t\tev.errorf(\"found duplicate series for the match group %s on the %s hand-side of the operation: [%s, %s]\"+\n2671: \t\t\t\t\";many-to-many matching not allowed: matching labels must be unique on one side\", matchedLabels.String(), oneSide, rs.Metric.String(), duplSample.Metric.String())\n2672: \t\t}\n2673: \t\trightSigs[sig] = rs\n2674: \t}\n2675: \n2676: \t// Tracks the match-signature. For one-to-one operations the value is nil. For many-to-one\n2677: \t// the value is a set of signatures to detect duplicated result elements.\n2678: \tif enh.matchedSigs == nil {\n2679: \t\tenh.matchedSigs = make(map[string]map[uint64]struct{}, len(rightSigs))\n2680: \t} else {\n2681: \t\tfor k := range enh.matchedSigs {\n2682: \t\t\tdelete(enh.matchedSigs, k)\n2683: \t\t}\n2684: \t}\n2685: \tmatchedSigs := enh.matchedSigs\n2686: \n2687: \t// For all lhs samples find a respective rhs sample and perform\n2688: \t// the binary operation.\n2689: \tvar lastErr error\n2690: \tfor i, ls := range lhs {\n2691: \t\tsig := lhsh[i].signature\n2692: \n2693: \t\trs, found := rightSigs[sig] // Look for a match in the rhs Vector.\n2694: \t\tif !found {\n2695: \t\t\tcontinue\n2696: \t\t}\n2697: \n2698: \t\t// Account for potentially swapped sidedness.\n2699: \t\tfl, fr := ls.F, rs.F\n2700: \t\thl, hr := ls.H, rs.H\n2701: \t\tif matching.Card == parser.CardOneToMany {\n2702: \t\t\tfl, fr = fr, fl\n2703: \t\t\thl, hr = hr, hl\n2704: \t\t}\n2705: \t\tfloatValue, histogramValue, keep, err := vectorElemBinop(op, fl, fr, hl, hr, pos)\n2706: \t\tif err != nil {\n2707: \t\t\tlastErr = err\n2708: \t\t\tcontinue\n2709: \t\t}\n2710: \t\tswitch {\n2711: \t\tcase returnBool:\n2712: \t\t\thistogramValue = nil\n2713: \t\t\tif keep {\n2714: \t\t\t\tfloatValue = 1.0\n2715: \t\t\t} else {\n2716: \t\t\t\tfloatValue = 0.0\n2717: \t\t\t}\n2718: \t\tcase !keep:\n2719: \t\t\tcontinue\n2720: \t\t}\n2721: \t\tmetric := resultMetric(ls.Metric, rs.Metric, op, matching, enh)\n2722: \t\tif !ev.enableDelayedNameRemoval && returnBool {\n2723: \t\t\tmetric = metric.DropReserved(schema.IsMetadataLabel)\n2724: \t\t}\n2725: \t\tinsertedSigs, exists := matchedSigs[sig]\n2726: \t\tif matching.Card == parser.CardOneToOne {\n2727: \t\t\tif exists {\n2728: \t\t\t\tev.errorf(\"multiple matches for labels: many-to-one matching must be explicit (group_left/group_right)\")\n2729: \t\t\t}\n2730: \t\t\tmatchedSigs[sig] = nil // Set existence to true.\n2731: \t\t} else {\n2732: \t\t\t// In many-to-one matching the grouping labels have to ensure a unique metric\n2733: \t\t\t// for the result Vector. Check whether those labels have already been added for\n2734: \t\t\t// the same matching labels.\n2735: \t\t\tinsertSig := metric.Hash()\n2736: \n2737: \t\t\tif !exists {\n2738: \t\t\t\tinsertedSigs = map[uint64]struct{}{}\n2739: \t\t\t\tmatchedSigs[sig] = insertedSigs\n2740: \t\t\t} else if _, duplicate := insertedSigs[insertSig]; duplicate {\n2741: \t\t\t\tev.errorf(\"multiple matches for labels: grouping labels must ensure unique matches\")\n2742: \t\t\t}\n2743: \t\t\tinsertedSigs[insertSig] = struct{}{}\n2744: \t\t}\n2745: \n2746: \t\tenh.Out = append(enh.Out, Sample{\n2747: \t\t\tMetric:   metric,\n2748: \t\t\tF:        floatValue,\n2749: \t\t\tH:        histogramValue,\n2750: \t\t\tDropName: returnBool,\n2751: \t\t})\n2752: \t}\n2753: \treturn enh.Out, lastErr\n2754: }\n2755: \n2756: func signatureFunc(on bool, b []byte, names ...string) func(labels.Labels) string {\n2757: \tif on {\n2758: \t\tslices.Sort(names)\n2759: \t\treturn func(lset labels.Labels) string {\n2760: \t\t\treturn string(lset.BytesWithLabels(b, names...))\n2761: \t\t}\n2762: \t}\n2763: \tnames = append([]string{labels.MetricName}, names...)\n2764: \tslices.Sort(names)\n2765: \treturn func(lset labels.Labels) string {\n2766: \t\treturn string(lset.BytesWithoutLabels(b, names...))\n2767: \t}\n2768: }\n2769: \n2770: // resultMetric returns the metric for the given sample(s) based on the Vector\n2771: // binary operation and the matching options.\n2772: func resultMetric(lhs, rhs labels.Labels, op parser.ItemType, matching *parser.VectorMatching, enh *EvalNodeHelper) labels.Labels {\n2773: \tif enh.resultMetric == nil {\n2774: \t\tenh.resultMetric = make(map[string]labels.Labels, len(enh.Out))\n2775: \t}\n2776: \n2777: \tenh.resetBuilder(lhs)\n2778: \tbuf := bytes.NewBuffer(enh.lblResultBuf[:0])\n2779: \tenh.lblBuf = lhs.Bytes(enh.lblBuf)\n2780: \tbuf.Write(enh.lblBuf)\n2781: \tenh.lblBuf = rhs.Bytes(enh.lblBuf)\n2782: \tbuf.Write(enh.lblBuf)\n2783: \tenh.lblResultBuf = buf.Bytes()\n2784: \n2785: \tif ret, ok := enh.resultMetric[string(enh.lblResultBuf)]; ok {\n2786: \t\treturn ret\n2787: \t}\n2788: \tstr := string(enh.lblResultBuf)\n2789: \n2790: \tif changesMetricSchema(op) {\n2791: \t\t// Setting empty Metadata causes the deletion of those if they exists.\n2792: \t\tschema.Metadata{}.SetToLabels(enh.lb)\n2793: \t}\n2794: \n2795: \tif matching.Card == parser.CardOneToOne {\n2796: \t\tif matching.On {\n2797: \t\t\tenh.lb.Keep(matching.MatchingLabels...)\n2798: \t\t} else {\n2799: \t\t\tenh.lb.Del(matching.MatchingLabels...)\n2800: \t\t}\n2801: \t}\n2802: \tfor _, ln := range matching.Include {\n2803: \t\t// Included labels from the `group_x` modifier are taken from the \"one\"-side.\n2804: \t\tif v := rhs.Get(ln); v != \"\" {\n2805: \t\t\tenh.lb.Set(ln, v)\n2806: \t\t} else {\n2807: \t\t\tenh.lb.Del(ln)\n2808: \t\t}\n2809: \t}\n2810: \n2811: \tret := enh.lb.Labels()\n2812: \tenh.resultMetric[str] = ret\n2813: \treturn ret\n2814: }\n2815: \n2816: // VectorscalarBinop evaluates a binary operation between a Vector and a Scalar.\n2817: func (ev *evaluator) VectorscalarBinop(op parser.ItemType, lhs Vector, rhs Scalar, swap, returnBool bool, enh *EvalNodeHelper, pos posrange.PositionRange) (Vector, error) {\n2818: \tvar lastErr error\n2819: \tfor _, lhsSample := range lhs {\n2820: \t\tlf, rf := lhsSample.F, rhs.V\n2821: \t\tvar rh *histogram.FloatHistogram\n2822: \t\tlh := lhsSample.H\n2823: \t\t// lhs always contains the Vector. If the original position was different\n2824: \t\t// swap for calculating the value.\n2825: \t\tif swap {\n2826: \t\t\tlf, rf = rf, lf\n2827: \t\t\tlh, rh = rh, lh\n2828: \t\t}\n2829: \t\tfloat, histogram, keep, err := vectorElemBinop(op, lf, rf, lh, rh, pos)\n2830: \t\tif err != nil && !errors.Is(err, annotations.PromQLWarning) {\n2831: \t\t\tlastErr = err\n2832: \t\t\tcontinue\n2833: \t\t}\n2834: \t\t// Catch cases where the scalar is the LHS in a scalar-vector comparison operation.\n2835: \t\t// We want to always keep the vector element value as the output value, even if it's on the RHS.\n2836: \t\tif op.IsComparisonOperator() && swap {\n2837: \t\t\tfloat = rf\n2838: \t\t\thistogram = rh\n2839: \t\t}\n2840: \t\tif returnBool {\n2841: \t\t\tif keep {\n2842: \t\t\t\tfloat = 1.0\n2843: \t\t\t} else {\n2844: \t\t\t\tfloat = 0.0\n2845: \t\t\t}\n2846: \t\t\tkeep = true\n2847: \t\t}\n2848: \t\tif keep {\n2849: \t\t\tlhsSample.F = float\n2850: \t\t\tlhsSample.H = histogram\n2851: \t\t\tif changesMetricSchema(op) || returnBool {\n2852: \t\t\t\tif !ev.enableDelayedNameRemoval {\n2853: \t\t\t\t\tlhsSample.Metric = lhsSample.Metric.DropReserved(schema.IsMetadataLabel)\n2854: \t\t\t\t}\n2855: \t\t\t\tlhsSample.DropName = true\n2856: \t\t\t}\n2857: \t\t\tenh.Out = append(enh.Out, lhsSample)\n2858: \t\t}\n2859: \t}\n2860: \treturn enh.Out, lastErr\n2861: }\n2862: \n2863: // scalarBinop evaluates a binary operation between two Scalars.\n2864: func scalarBinop(op parser.ItemType, lhs, rhs float64) float64 {\n2865: \tswitch op {\n2866: \tcase parser.ADD:\n2867: \t\treturn lhs + rhs\n2868: \tcase parser.SUB:\n2869: \t\treturn lhs - rhs\n2870: \tcase parser.MUL:\n2871: \t\treturn lhs * rhs\n2872: \tcase parser.DIV:\n2873: \t\treturn lhs / rhs\n2874: \tcase parser.POW:\n2875: \t\treturn math.Pow(lhs, rhs)\n2876: \tcase parser.MOD:\n2877: \t\treturn math.Mod(lhs, rhs)\n2878: \tcase parser.EQLC:\n2879: \t\treturn btos(lhs == rhs)\n2880: \tcase parser.NEQ:\n2881: \t\treturn btos(lhs != rhs)\n2882: \tcase parser.GTR:\n2883: \t\treturn btos(lhs > rhs)\n2884: \tcase parser.LSS:\n2885: \t\treturn btos(lhs < rhs)\n2886: \tcase parser.GTE:\n2887: \t\treturn btos(lhs >= rhs)\n2888: \tcase parser.LTE:\n2889: \t\treturn btos(lhs <= rhs)\n2890: \tcase parser.ATAN2:\n2891: \t\treturn math.Atan2(lhs, rhs)\n2892: \t}\n2893: \tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\"operator %q not allowed for Scalar operations\", op))\n2894: }\n2895: \n2896: // vectorElemBinop evaluates a binary operation between two Vector elements.\n2897: func vectorElemBinop(op parser.ItemType, lhs, rhs float64, hlhs, hrhs *histogram.FloatHistogram, pos posrange.PositionRange) (float64, *histogram.FloatHistogram, bool, error) {\n2898: \topName := parser.ItemTypeStr[op]\n2899: \tswitch {\n2900: \tcase hlhs == nil && hrhs == nil:\n2901: \t\t{\n2902: \t\t\tswitch op {\n2903: \t\t\tcase parser.ADD:\n2904: \t\t\t\treturn lhs + rhs, nil, true, nil\n2905: \t\t\tcase parser.SUB:\n2906: \t\t\t\treturn lhs - rhs, nil, true, nil\n2907: \t\t\tcase parser.MUL:\n2908: \t\t\t\treturn lhs * rhs, nil, true, nil\n2909: \t\t\tcase parser.DIV:\n2910: \t\t\t\treturn lhs / rhs, nil, true, nil\n2911: \t\t\tcase parser.POW:\n2912: \t\t\t\treturn math.Pow(lhs, rhs), nil, true, nil\n2913: \t\t\tcase parser.MOD:\n2914: \t\t\t\treturn math.Mod(lhs, rhs), nil, true, nil\n2915: \t\t\tcase parser.EQLC:\n2916: \t\t\t\treturn lhs, nil, lhs == rhs, nil\n2917: \t\t\tcase parser.NEQ:\n2918: \t\t\t\treturn lhs, nil, lhs != rhs, nil\n2919: \t\t\tcase parser.GTR:\n2920: \t\t\t\treturn lhs, nil, lhs > rhs, nil\n2921: \t\t\tcase parser.LSS:\n2922: \t\t\t\treturn lhs, nil, lhs < rhs, nil\n2923: \t\t\tcase parser.GTE:\n2924: \t\t\t\treturn lhs, nil, lhs >= rhs, nil\n2925: \t\t\tcase parser.LTE:\n2926: \t\t\t\treturn lhs, nil, lhs <= rhs, nil\n2927: \t\t\tcase parser.ATAN2:\n2928: \t\t\t\treturn math.Atan2(lhs, rhs), nil, true, nil\n2929: \t\t\t}\n2930: \t\t}\n2931: \tcase hlhs == nil && hrhs != nil:\n2932: \t\t{\n2933: \t\t\tswitch op {\n2934: \t\t\tcase parser.MUL:\n2935: \t\t\t\treturn 0, hrhs.Copy().Mul(lhs).Compact(0), true, nil\n2936: \t\t\tcase parser.ADD, parser.SUB, parser.DIV, parser.POW, parser.MOD, parser.EQLC, parser.NEQ, parser.GTR, parser.LSS, parser.GTE, parser.LTE, parser.ATAN2:\n2937: \t\t\t\treturn 0, nil, false, annotations.NewIncompatibleTypesInBinOpInfo(\"float\", opName, \"histogram\", pos)\n2938: \t\t\t}\n2939: \t\t}\n2940: \tcase hlhs != nil && hrhs == nil:\n2941: \t\t{\n2942: \t\t\tswitch op {\n2943: \t\t\tcase parser.MUL:\n2944: \t\t\t\treturn 0, hlhs.Copy().Mul(rhs).Compact(0), true, nil\n2945: \t\t\tcase parser.DIV:\n2946: \t\t\t\treturn 0, hlhs.Copy().Div(rhs).Compact(0), true, nil\n2947: \t\t\tcase parser.ADD, parser.SUB, parser.POW, parser.MOD, parser.EQLC, parser.NEQ, parser.GTR, parser.LSS, parser.GTE, parser.LTE, parser.ATAN2:\n2948: \t\t\t\treturn 0, nil, false, annotations.NewIncompatibleTypesInBinOpInfo(\"histogram\", opName, \"float\", pos)\n2949: \t\t\t}\n2950: \t\t}\n2951: \tcase hlhs != nil && hrhs != nil:\n2952: \t\t{\n2953: \t\t\tswitch op {\n2954: \t\t\tcase parser.ADD:\n2955: \t\t\t\tres, counterResetCollision, err := hlhs.Copy().Add(hrhs)\n2956: \t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n2957: \t\t\t\t\treturn 0, nil, false, err\n2958: \t\t\t\t}\n2959: \t\t\t\tif counterResetCollision {\n2960: \t\t\t\t\terr = annotations.NewHistogramCounterResetCollisionWarning(pos, annotations.HistogramAdd)\n2961: \t\t\t\t}\n2962: \t\t\t\treturn 0, res.Compact(0), true, err\n2963: \t\t\tcase parser.SUB:\n2964: \t\t\t\tres, counterResetCollision, err := hlhs.Copy().Sub(hrhs)\n2965: \t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n2966: \t\t\t\t\treturn 0, nil, false, err\n2967: \t\t\t\t}\n2968: \t\t\t\tif counterResetCollision {\n2969: \t\t\t\t\terr = annotations.NewHistogramCounterResetCollisionWarning(pos, annotations.HistogramSub)\n2970: \t\t\t\t}\n2971: \t\t\t\treturn 0, res.Compact(0), true, err\n2972: \t\t\tcase parser.EQLC:\n2973: \t\t\t\t// This operation expects that both histograms are compacted.\n2974: \t\t\t\treturn 0, hlhs, hlhs.Equals(hrhs), nil\n2975: \t\t\tcase parser.NEQ:\n2976: \t\t\t\t// This operation expects that both histograms are compacted.\n2977: \t\t\t\treturn 0, hlhs, !hlhs.Equals(hrhs), nil\n2978: \t\t\tcase parser.MUL, parser.DIV, parser.POW, parser.MOD, parser.GTR, parser.LSS, parser.GTE, parser.LTE, parser.ATAN2:\n2979: \t\t\t\treturn 0, nil, false, annotations.NewIncompatibleTypesInBinOpInfo(\"histogram\", opName, \"histogram\", pos)\n2980: \t\t\t}\n2981: \t\t}\n2982: \t}\n2983: \tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\"operator %q not allowed for operations between Vectors\", op))\n2984: }\n2985: \n2986: type groupedAggregation struct {\n2987: \tfloatValue     float64\n2988: \thistogramValue *histogram.FloatHistogram\n2989: \tfloatMean      float64\n2990: \tfloatKahanC    float64 // \"Compensating value\" for Kahan summation.\n2991: \tgroupCount     float64\n2992: \theap           vectorByValueHeap\n2993: \n2994: \t// All bools together for better packing within the struct.\n2995: \tseen                   bool // Was this output groups seen in the input at this timestamp.\n2996: \thasFloat               bool // Has at least 1 float64 sample aggregated.\n2997: \thasHistogram           bool // Has at least 1 histogram sample aggregated.\n2998: \tincompatibleHistograms bool // If true, group has seen mixed exponential and custom buckets, or incompatible custom buckets.\n2999: \tgroupAggrComplete      bool // Used by LIMITK to short-cut series loop when we've reached K elem on every group.\n3000: \tincrementalMean        bool // True after reverting to incremental calculation of the mean value.\n3001: }\n3002: \n3003: // aggregation evaluates sum, avg, count, stdvar, stddev or quantile at one timestep on inputMatrix.\n3004: // These functions produce one output series for each group specified in the expression, with just the labels from `by(...)`.\n3005: // outputMatrix should be already populated with grouping labels; groups is one-to-one with outputMatrix.\n3006: // seriesToResult maps inputMatrix indexes to outputMatrix indexes.\n3007: func (ev *evaluator) aggregation(e *parser.AggregateExpr, q float64, inputMatrix, outputMatrix Matrix, seriesToResult []int, groups []groupedAggregation, enh *EvalNodeHelper) annotations.Annotations {\n3008: \top := e.Op\n3009: \tvar annos annotations.Annotations\n3010: \tfor i := range groups {\n3011: \t\tgroups[i].seen = false\n3012: \t}\n3013: \n3014: \tfor si := range inputMatrix {\n3015: \t\tf, h, ok := ev.nextValues(enh.Ts, &inputMatrix[si])\n3016: \t\tif !ok {\n3017: \t\t\tcontinue\n3018: \t\t}\n3019: \n3020: \t\tgroup := &groups[seriesToResult[si]]\n3021: \t\t// Initialize this group if it's the first time we've seen it.\n3022: \t\tif !group.seen {\n3023: \t\t\t*group = groupedAggregation{\n3024: \t\t\t\tseen:                   true,\n3025: \t\t\t\tfloatValue:             f,\n3026: \t\t\t\tfloatMean:              f,\n3027: \t\t\t\tincompatibleHistograms: false,\n3028: \t\t\t\tgroupCount:             1,\n3029: \t\t\t}\n3030: \t\t\tswitch op {\n3031: \t\t\tcase parser.AVG, parser.SUM:\n3032: \t\t\t\tif h == nil {\n3033: \t\t\t\t\tgroup.hasFloat = true\n3034: \t\t\t\t} else {\n3035: \t\t\t\t\tgroup.histogramValue = h.Copy()\n3036: \t\t\t\t\tgroup.hasHistogram = true\n3037: \t\t\t\t}\n3038: \t\t\tcase parser.STDVAR, parser.STDDEV:\n3039: \t\t\t\tswitch {\n3040: \t\t\t\tcase h != nil:\n3041: \t\t\t\t\t// Ignore histograms for STDVAR and STDDEV.\n3042: \t\t\t\t\tgroup.seen = false\n3043: \t\t\t\t\tif op == parser.STDVAR {\n3044: \t\t\t\t\t\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\"stdvar\", e.Expr.PositionRange()))\n3045: \t\t\t\t\t} else {\n3046: \t\t\t\t\t\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\"stddev\", e.Expr.PositionRange()))\n3047: \t\t\t\t\t}\n3048: \t\t\t\tcase math.IsNaN(f), math.IsInf(f, 0):\n3049: \t\t\t\t\tgroup.floatValue = math.NaN()\n3050: \t\t\t\tdefault:\n3051: \t\t\t\t\tgroup.floatValue = 0\n3052: \t\t\t\t}\n3053: \t\t\tcase parser.QUANTILE:\n3054: \t\t\t\tif h != nil {\n3055: \t\t\t\t\tgroup.seen = false\n3056: \t\t\t\t\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\"quantile\", e.Expr.PositionRange()))\n3057: \t\t\t\t}\n3058: \t\t\t\tgroup.heap = make(vectorByValueHeap, 1)\n3059: \t\t\t\tgroup.heap[0] = Sample{F: f}\n3060: \t\t\tcase parser.GROUP:\n3061: \t\t\t\tgroup.floatValue = 1\n3062: \t\t\tcase parser.MIN, parser.MAX:\n3063: \t\t\t\tif h != nil {\n3064: \t\t\t\t\tgroup.seen = false\n3065: \t\t\t\t\tif op == parser.MIN {\n3066: \t\t\t\t\t\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\"min\", e.Expr.PositionRange()))\n3067: \t\t\t\t\t} else {\n3068: \t\t\t\t\t\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\"max\", e.Expr.PositionRange()))\n3069: \t\t\t\t\t}\n3070: \t\t\t\t}\n3071: \t\t\t}\n3072: \t\t\tcontinue\n3073: \t\t}\n3074: \n3075: \t\tif group.incompatibleHistograms {\n3076: \t\t\tcontinue\n3077: \t\t}\n3078: \n3079: \t\tswitch op {\n3080: \t\tcase parser.SUM:\n3081: \t\t\tif h != nil {\n3082: \t\t\t\tgroup.hasHistogram = true\n3083: \t\t\t\tif group.histogramValue != nil {\n3084: \t\t\t\t\t_, _, err := group.histogramValue.Add(h)\n3085: \t\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n3086: \t\t\t\t\t\thandleAggregationError(err, e, inputMatrix[si].Metric.Get(model.MetricNameLabel), &annos)\n3087: \t\t\t\t\t\tgroup.incompatibleHistograms = true\n3088: \t\t\t\t\t}\n3089: \t\t\t\t}\n3090: \t\t\t\t// Otherwise the aggregation contained floats\n3091: \t\t\t\t// previously and will be invalid anyway. No\n3092: \t\t\t\t// point in copying the histogram in that case.\n3093: \t\t\t} else {\n3094: \t\t\t\tgroup.hasFloat = true\n3095: \t\t\t\tgroup.floatValue, group.floatKahanC = kahanSumInc(f, group.floatValue, group.floatKahanC)\n3096: \t\t\t}\n3097: \n3098: \t\tcase parser.AVG:\n3099: \t\t\t// For the average calculation of histograms, we use\n3100: \t\t\t// incremental mean calculation without the help of\n3101: \t\t\t// Kahan summation (but this should change, see\n3102: \t\t\t// https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/issues/14105\n3103: \t\t\t// ). For floats, we improve the accuracy with the help\n3104: \t\t\t// of Kahan summation. For a while, we assumed that\n3105: \t\t\t// incremental mean calculation combined with Kahan\n3106: \t\t\t// summation (see\n3107: \t\t\t// https://stackoverflow.com/questions/61665473/is-it-beneficial-for-precision-to-calculate-the-incremental-mean-average\n3108: \t\t\t// for inspiration) is generally the preferred solution.\n3109: \t\t\t// However, it then turned out that direct mean\n3110: \t\t\t// calculation (still in combination with Kahan\n3111: \t\t\t// summation) is often more accurate. See discussion in\n3112: \t\t\t// https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/issues/16714\n3113: \t\t\t// . The problem with the direct mean calculation is\n3114: \t\t\t// that it can overflow float64 for inputs on which the\n3115: \t\t\t// incremental mean calculation works just fine. Our\n3116: \t\t\t// current approach is therefore to use direct mean\n3117: \t\t\t// calculation as long as we do not overflow (or\n3118: \t\t\t// underflow) the running sum. Once the latter would\n3119: \t\t\t// happen, we switch to incremental mean calculation.\n3120: \t\t\t// This seems to work reasonably well, but note that a\n3121: \t\t\t// deeper understanding would be needed to find out if\n3122: \t\t\t// maybe an earlier switch to incremental mean\n3123: \t\t\t// calculation would be better in terms of accuracy.\n3124: \t\t\t// Also, we could apply a number of additional means to\n3125: \t\t\t// improve the accuracy, like processing the values in a\n3126: \t\t\t// particular order. For now, we decided that the\n3127: \t\t\t// current implementation is accurate enough for\n3128: \t\t\t// practical purposes, in particular given that changing\n3129: \t\t\t// the order of summation would be hard, given how the\n3130: \t\t\t// PromQL engine implements aggregations.\n3131: \t\t\tgroup.groupCount++\n3132: \t\t\tif h != nil {\n3133: \t\t\t\tgroup.hasHistogram = true\n3134: \t\t\t\tif group.histogramValue != nil {\n3135: \t\t\t\t\tleft := h.Copy().Div(group.groupCount)\n3136: \t\t\t\t\tright := group.histogramValue.Copy().Div(group.groupCount)\n3137: \t\t\t\t\ttoAdd, _, err := left.Sub(right)\n3138: \t\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n3139: \t\t\t\t\t\thandleAggregationError(err, e, inputMatrix[si].Metric.Get(model.MetricNameLabel), &annos)\n3140: \t\t\t\t\t\tgroup.incompatibleHistograms = true\n3141: \t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n3142: \t\t\t\t\t}\n3143: \t\t\t\t\t_, _, err = group.histogramValue.Add(toAdd)\n3144: \t\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n3145: \t\t\t\t\t\thandleAggregationError(err, e, inputMatrix[si].Metric.Get(model.MetricNameLabel), &annos)\n3146: \t\t\t\t\t\tgroup.incompatibleHistograms = true\n3147: \t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n3148: \t\t\t\t\t}\n3149: \t\t\t\t}\n3150: \t\t\t\t// Otherwise the aggregation contained floats\n3151: \t\t\t\t// previously and will be invalid anyway. No\n3152: \t\t\t\t// point in copying the histogram in that case.\n3153: \t\t\t} else {\n3154: \t\t\t\tgroup.hasFloat = true\n3155: \t\t\t\tif !group.incrementalMean {\n3156: \t\t\t\t\tnewV, newC := kahanSumInc(f, group.floatValue, group.floatKahanC)\n3157: \t\t\t\t\tif !math.IsInf(newV, 0) {\n3158: \t\t\t\t\t\t// The sum doesn't overflow, so we propagate it to the\n3159: \t\t\t\t\t\t// group struct and continue with the regular\n3160: \t\t\t\t\t\t// calculation of the mean value.\n3161: \t\t\t\t\t\tgroup.floatValue, group.floatKahanC = newV, newC\n3162: \t\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n3163: \t\t\t\t\t}\n3164: \t\t\t\t\t// If we are here, we know that the sum _would_ overflow. So\n3165: \t\t\t\t\t// instead of continue to sum up, we revert to incremental\n3166: \t\t\t\t\t// calculation of the mean value from here on.\n3167: \t\t\t\t\tgroup.incrementalMean = true\n3168: \t\t\t\t\tgroup.floatMean = group.floatValue / (group.groupCount - 1)\n3169: \t\t\t\t\tgroup.floatKahanC /= group.groupCount - 1\n3170: \t\t\t\t}\n3171: \t\t\t\tq := (group.groupCount - 1) / group.groupCount\n3172: \t\t\t\tgroup.floatMean, group.floatKahanC = kahanSumInc(\n3173: \t\t\t\t\tf/group.groupCount,\n3174: \t\t\t\t\tq*group.floatMean,\n3175: \t\t\t\t\tq*group.floatKahanC,\n3176: \t\t\t\t)\n3177: \t\t\t}\n3178: \n3179: \t\tcase parser.GROUP:\n3180: \t\t\t// Do nothing. Required to avoid the panic in `default:` below.\n3181: \n3182: \t\tcase parser.MAX:\n3183: \t\t\tif h != nil {\n3184: \t\t\t\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\"max\", e.Expr.PositionRange()))\n3185: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n3186: \t\t\t}\n3187: \t\t\tif group.floatValue < f || math.IsNaN(group.floatValue) {\n3188: \t\t\t\tgroup.floatValue = f\n3189: \t\t\t}\n3190: \n3191: \t\tcase parser.MIN:\n3192: \t\t\tif h != nil {\n3193: \t\t\t\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\"min\", e.Expr.PositionRange()))\n3194: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n3195: \t\t\t}\n3196: \t\t\tif group.floatValue > f || math.IsNaN(group.floatValue) {\n3197: \t\t\t\tgroup.floatValue = f\n3198: \t\t\t}\n3199: \n3200: \t\tcase parser.COUNT:\n3201: \t\t\tgroup.groupCount++\n3202: \n3203: \t\tcase parser.STDVAR, parser.STDDEV:\n3204: \t\t\tif h == nil { // Ignore native histograms.\n3205: \t\t\t\tgroup.groupCount++\n3206: \t\t\t\tdelta := f - group.floatMean\n3207: \t\t\t\tgroup.floatMean += delta / group.groupCount\n3208: \t\t\t\tgroup.floatValue += delta * (f - group.floatMean)\n3209: \t\t\t} else {\n3210: \t\t\t\tif op == parser.STDVAR {\n3211: \t\t\t\t\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\"stdvar\", e.Expr.PositionRange()))\n3212: \t\t\t\t} else {\n3213: \t\t\t\t\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\"stddev\", e.Expr.PositionRange()))\n3214: \t\t\t\t}\n3215: \t\t\t}\n3216: \n3217: \t\tcase parser.QUANTILE:\n3218: \t\t\tif h != nil {\n3219: \t\t\t\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\"quantile\", e.Expr.PositionRange()))\n3220: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n3221: \t\t\t}\n3222: \t\t\tgroup.heap = append(group.heap, Sample{F: f})\n3223: \n3224: \t\tdefault:\n3225: \t\t\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\"expected aggregation operator but got %q\", op))\n3226: \t\t}\n3227: \t}\n3228: \n3229: \t// Construct the output matrix from the aggregated groups.\n3230: \tnumSteps := int((ev.endTimestamp-ev.startTimestamp)/ev.interval) + 1\n3231: \n3232: \tfor ri, aggr := range groups {\n3233: \t\tif !aggr.seen {\n3234: \t\t\tcontinue\n3235: \t\t}\n3236: \t\tswitch op {\n3237: \t\tcase parser.AVG:\n3238: \t\t\tif aggr.hasFloat && aggr.hasHistogram {\n3239: \t\t\t\t// We cannot aggregate histogram sample with a float64 sample.\n3240: \t\t\t\tannos.Add(annotations.NewMixedFloatsHistogramsAggWarning(e.Expr.PositionRange()))\n3241: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n3242: \t\t\t}\n3243: \t\t\tswitch {\n3244: \t\t\tcase aggr.incompatibleHistograms:\n3245: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n3246: \t\t\tcase aggr.hasHistogram:\n3247: \t\t\t\taggr.histogramValue = aggr.histogramValue.Compact(0)\n3248: \t\t\tcase aggr.incrementalMean:\n3249: \t\t\t\taggr.floatValue = aggr.floatMean + aggr.floatKahanC\n3250: \t\t\tdefault:\n3251: \t\t\t\taggr.floatValue = aggr.floatValue/aggr.groupCount + aggr.floatKahanC/aggr.groupCount\n3252: \t\t\t}\n3253: \n3254: \t\tcase parser.COUNT:\n3255: \t\t\taggr.floatValue = aggr.groupCount\n3256: \n3257: \t\tcase parser.STDVAR:\n3258: \t\t\taggr.floatValue /= aggr.groupCount\n3259: \n3260: \t\tcase parser.STDDEV:\n3261: \t\t\taggr.floatValue = math.Sqrt(aggr.floatValue / aggr.groupCount)\n3262: \n3263: \t\tcase parser.QUANTILE:\n3264: \t\t\taggr.floatValue = quantile(q, aggr.heap)\n3265: \n3266: \t\tcase parser.SUM:\n3267: \t\t\tif aggr.hasFloat && aggr.hasHistogram {\n3268: \t\t\t\t// We cannot aggregate histogram sample with a float64 sample.\n3269: \t\t\t\tannos.Add(annotations.NewMixedFloatsHistogramsAggWarning(e.Expr.PositionRange()))\n3270: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n3271: \t\t\t}\n3272: \t\t\tswitch {\n3273: \t\t\tcase aggr.incompatibleHistograms:\n3274: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n3275: \t\t\tcase aggr.hasHistogram:\n3276: \t\t\t\taggr.histogramValue.Compact(0)\n3277: \t\t\tdefault:\n3278: \t\t\t\taggr.floatValue += aggr.floatKahanC\n3279: \t\t\t}\n3280: \t\tdefault:\n3281: \t\t\t// For other aggregations, we already have the right value.\n3282: \t\t}\n3283: \n3284: \t\tss := &outputMatrix[ri]\n3285: \t\taddToSeries(ss, enh.Ts, aggr.floatValue, aggr.histogramValue, numSteps)\n3286: \t\tss.DropName = inputMatrix[ri].DropName\n3287: \t}\n3288: \n3289: \treturn annos\n3290: }\n3291: \n3292: // aggregationK evaluates topk, bottomk, limitk, or limit_ratio at one timestep on inputMatrix.\n3293: // Output that has the same labels as the input, but just k of them per group.\n3294: // seriesToResult maps inputMatrix indexes to groups indexes.\n3295: // For an instant query, returns a Matrix in descending order for topk or ascending for bottomk, or without any order for limitk / limit_ratio.\n3296: // For a range query, aggregates output in the seriess map.\n3297: func (ev *evaluator) aggregationK(e *parser.AggregateExpr, fParam float64, inputMatrix Matrix, seriesToResult []int, groups []groupedAggregation, enh *EvalNodeHelper, seriess map[uint64]Series) (Matrix, annotations.Annotations) {\n3298: \top := e.Op\n3299: \tvar s Sample\n3300: \tvar annos annotations.Annotations\n3301: \t// Used to short-cut the loop for LIMITK if we already collected k elements for every group\n3302: \tgroupsRemaining := len(groups)\n3303: \tfor i := range groups {\n3304: \t\tgroups[i].seen = false\n3305: \t}\n3306: \t// advanceRemainingSeries discards any values at the current timestamp `ts`\n3307: \t// for the remaining input series. In range queries, if these values are not\n3308: \t// consumed now, they will no longer be accessible in the next evaluation step.\n3309: \tadvanceRemainingSeries := func(ts int64, startIdx int) {\n3310: \t\tfor i := startIdx; i < len(inputMatrix); i++ {\n3311: \t\t\t_, _, _ = ev.nextValues(ts, &inputMatrix[i])\n3312: \t\t}\n3313: \t}\n3314: \n3315: seriesLoop:\n3316: \tfor si := range inputMatrix {\n3317: \t\tf, h, ok := ev.nextValues(enh.Ts, &inputMatrix[si])\n3318: \t\tif !ok {\n3319: \t\t\tcontinue\n3320: \t\t}\n3321: \t\ts = Sample{Metric: inputMatrix[si].Metric, F: f, H: h, DropName: inputMatrix[si].DropName}\n3322: \n3323: \t\tvar k int64\n3324: \t\tvar r float64\n3325: \t\tswitch op {\n3326: \t\tcase parser.TOPK, parser.BOTTOMK, parser.LIMITK:\n3327: \t\t\tk = min(int64(fParam), int64(len(inputMatrix)))\n3328: \t\t\tif k < 1 {\n3329: \t\t\t\tif enh.Ts != ev.endTimestamp {\n3330: \t\t\t\t\tadvanceRemainingSeries(enh.Ts, si+1)\n3331: \t\t\t\t}\n3332: \t\t\t\treturn nil, annos\n3333: \t\t\t}\n3334: \t\tcase parser.LIMIT_RATIO:\n3335: \t\t\tswitch {\n3336: \t\t\tcase fParam == 0:\n3337: \t\t\t\tif enh.Ts != ev.endTimestamp {\n3338: \t\t\t\t\tadvanceRemainingSeries(enh.Ts, si+1)\n3339: \t\t\t\t}\n3340: \t\t\t\treturn nil, annos\n3341: \t\t\tcase fParam < -1.0:\n3342: \t\t\t\tr = -1.0\n3343: \t\t\tcase fParam > 1.0:\n3344: \t\t\t\tr = 1.0\n3345: \t\t\tdefault:\n3346: \t\t\t\tr = fParam\n3347: \t\t\t}\n3348: \t\t}\n3349: \n3350: \t\tgroup := &groups[seriesToResult[si]]\n3351: \t\t// Initialize this group if it's the first time we've seen it.\n3352: \t\tif !group.seen {\n3353: \t\t\t// LIMIT_RATIO is a special case, as we may not add this very sample to the heap,\n3354: \t\t\t// while we also don't know the final size of it.\n3355: \t\t\tswitch op {\n3356: \t\t\tcase parser.LIMIT_RATIO:\n3357: \t\t\t\t*group = groupedAggregation{\n3358: \t\t\t\t\tseen: true,\n3359: \t\t\t\t\theap: make(vectorByValueHeap, 0),\n3360: \t\t\t\t}\n3361: \t\t\t\tif ratiosampler.AddRatioSample(r, &s) {\n3362: \t\t\t\t\theap.Push(&group.heap, &s)\n3363: \t\t\t\t}\n3364: \t\t\tcase parser.LIMITK:\n3365: \t\t\t\t*group = groupedAggregation{\n3366: \t\t\t\t\tseen: true,\n3367: \t\t\t\t\theap: make(vectorByValueHeap, 1, k),\n3368: \t\t\t\t}\n3369: \t\t\t\tgroup.heap[0] = s\n3370: \t\t\tcase parser.TOPK:\n3371: \t\t\t\t*group = groupedAggregation{\n3372: \t\t\t\t\tseen: true,\n3373: \t\t\t\t\theap: make(vectorByValueHeap, 0, k),\n3374: \t\t\t\t}\n3375: \t\t\t\tif s.H != nil {\n3376: \t\t\t\t\tgroup.seen = false\n3377: \t\t\t\t\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\"topk\", e.PosRange))\n3378: \t\t\t\t} else {\n3379: \t\t\t\t\theap.Push(&group.heap, &s)\n3380: \t\t\t\t}\n3381: \t\t\tcase parser.BOTTOMK:\n3382: \t\t\t\t*group = groupedAggregation{\n3383: \t\t\t\t\tseen: true,\n3384: \t\t\t\t\theap: make(vectorByValueHeap, 0, k),\n3385: \t\t\t\t}\n3386: \t\t\t\tif s.H != nil {\n3387: \t\t\t\t\tgroup.seen = false\n3388: \t\t\t\t\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\"bottomk\", e.PosRange))\n3389: \t\t\t\t} else {\n3390: \t\t\t\t\theap.Push(&group.heap, &s)\n3391: \t\t\t\t}\n3392: \t\t\t}\n3393: \t\t\tcontinue\n3394: \t\t}\n3395: \n3396: \t\tswitch op {\n3397: \t\tcase parser.TOPK:\n3398: \t\t\t// We build a heap of up to k elements, with the smallest element at heap[0].\n3399: \t\t\tswitch {\n3400: \t\t\tcase s.H != nil:\n3401: \t\t\t\t// Ignore histogram sample and add info annotation.\n3402: \t\t\t\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\"topk\", e.PosRange))\n3403: \t\t\tcase int64(len(group.heap)) < k:\n3404: \t\t\t\theap.Push(&group.heap, &s)\n3405: \t\t\tcase group.heap[0].F < s.F || (math.IsNaN(group.heap[0].F) && !math.IsNaN(s.F)):\n3406: \t\t\t\t// This new element is bigger than the previous smallest element - overwrite that.\n3407: \t\t\t\tgroup.heap[0] = s\n3408: \t\t\t\tif k > 1 {\n3409: \t\t\t\t\theap.Fix(&group.heap, 0) // Maintain the heap invariant.\n3410: \t\t\t\t}\n3411: \t\t\t}\n3412: \n3413: \t\tcase parser.BOTTOMK:\n3414: \t\t\t// We build a heap of up to k elements, with the biggest element at heap[0].\n3415: \t\t\tswitch {\n3416: \t\t\tcase s.H != nil:\n3417: \t\t\t\t// Ignore histogram sample and add info annotation.\n3418: \t\t\t\tannos.Add(annotations.NewHistogramIgnoredInAggregationInfo(\"bottomk\", e.PosRange))\n3419: \t\t\tcase int64(len(group.heap)) < k:\n3420: \t\t\t\theap.Push((*vectorByReverseValueHeap)(&group.heap), &s)\n3421: \t\t\tcase group.heap[0].F > s.F || (math.IsNaN(group.heap[0].F) && !math.IsNaN(s.F)):\n3422: \t\t\t\t// This new element is smaller than the previous biggest element - overwrite that.\n3423: \t\t\t\tgroup.heap[0] = s\n3424: \t\t\t\tif k > 1 {\n3425: \t\t\t\t\theap.Fix((*vectorByReverseValueHeap)(&group.heap), 0) // Maintain the heap invariant.\n3426: \t\t\t\t}\n3427: \t\t\t}\n3428: \n3429: \t\tcase parser.LIMITK:\n3430: \t\t\tif int64(len(group.heap)) < k {\n3431: \t\t\t\theap.Push(&group.heap, &s)\n3432: \t\t\t}\n3433: \t\t\t// LIMITK optimization: early break if we've added K elem to _every_ group,\n3434: \t\t\t// especially useful for large timeseries where the user is exploring labels via e.g.\n3435: \t\t\t// limitk(10, my_metric)\n3436: \t\t\tif !group.groupAggrComplete && int64(len(group.heap)) == k {\n3437: \t\t\t\tgroup.groupAggrComplete = true\n3438: \t\t\t\tgroupsRemaining--\n3439: \t\t\t\tif groupsRemaining == 0 {\n3440: \t\t\t\t\t// Process other values in the series before breaking the loop in case of range query.\n3441: \t\t\t\t\tif enh.Ts != ev.endTimestamp {\n3442: \t\t\t\t\t\tadvanceRemainingSeries(enh.Ts, si+1)\n3443: \t\t\t\t\t}\n3444: \t\t\t\t\tbreak seriesLoop\n3445: \t\t\t\t}\n3446: \t\t\t}\n3447: \n3448: \t\tcase parser.LIMIT_RATIO:\n3449: \t\t\tif ratiosampler.AddRatioSample(r, &s) {\n3450: \t\t\t\theap.Push(&group.heap, &s)\n3451: \t\t\t}\n3452: \n3453: \t\tdefault:\n3454: \t\t\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\"expected aggregation operator but got %q\", op))\n3455: \t\t}\n3456: \t}\n3457: \n3458: \t// Construct the result from the aggregated groups.\n3459: \tnumSteps := int((ev.endTimestamp-ev.startTimestamp)/ev.interval) + 1\n3460: \tvar mat Matrix\n3461: \tif ev.endTimestamp == ev.startTimestamp {\n3462: \t\tmat = make(Matrix, 0, len(groups))\n3463: \t}\n3464: \n3465: \tadd := func(lbls labels.Labels, f float64, h *histogram.FloatHistogram, dropName bool) {\n3466: \t\t// If this could be an instant query, add directly to the matrix so the result is in consistent order.\n3467: \t\tif ev.endTimestamp == ev.startTimestamp {\n3468: \t\t\tif h != nil {\n3469: \t\t\t\tmat = append(mat, Series{Metric: lbls, Histograms: []HPoint{{T: enh.Ts, H: h}}, DropName: dropName})\n3470: \t\t\t} else {\n3471: \t\t\t\tmat = append(mat, Series{Metric: lbls, Floats: []FPoint{{T: enh.Ts, F: f}}, DropName: dropName})\n3472: \t\t\t}\n3473: \t\t} else {\n3474: \t\t\t// Otherwise the results are added into seriess elements.\n3475: \t\t\thash := lbls.Hash()\n3476: \t\t\tss, ok := seriess[hash]\n3477: \t\t\tif !ok {\n3478: \t\t\t\tss = Series{Metric: lbls, DropName: dropName}\n3479: \t\t\t}\n3480: \t\t\taddToSeries(&ss, enh.Ts, f, h, numSteps)\n3481: \t\t\tseriess[hash] = ss\n3482: \t\t}\n3483: \t}\n3484: \tfor _, aggr := range groups {\n3485: \t\tif !aggr.seen {\n3486: \t\t\tcontinue\n3487: \t\t}\n3488: \t\tswitch op {\n3489: \t\tcase parser.TOPK:\n3490: \t\t\t// The heap keeps the lowest value on top, so reverse it.\n3491: \t\t\tif len(aggr.heap) > 1 {\n3492: \t\t\t\tsort.Sort(sort.Reverse(aggr.heap))\n3493: \t\t\t}\n3494: \t\t\tfor _, v := range aggr.heap {\n3495: \t\t\t\tadd(v.Metric, v.F, v.H, v.DropName)\n3496: \t\t\t}\n3497: \n3498: \t\tcase parser.BOTTOMK:\n3499: \t\t\t// The heap keeps the highest value on top, so reverse it.\n3500: \t\t\tif len(aggr.heap) > 1 {\n3501: \t\t\t\tsort.Sort(sort.Reverse((*vectorByReverseValueHeap)(&aggr.heap)))\n3502: \t\t\t}\n3503: \t\t\tfor _, v := range aggr.heap {\n3504: \t\t\t\tadd(v.Metric, v.F, v.H, v.DropName)\n3505: \t\t\t}\n3506: \n3507: \t\tcase parser.LIMITK, parser.LIMIT_RATIO:\n3508: \t\t\tfor _, v := range aggr.heap {\n3509: \t\t\t\tadd(v.Metric, v.F, v.H, v.DropName)\n3510: \t\t\t}\n3511: \t\t}\n3512: \t}\n3513: \n3514: \treturn mat, annos\n3515: }\n3516: \n3517: // aggregationCountValues evaluates count_values on vec.\n3518: // Outputs as many series per group as there are values in the input.\n3519: func (*evaluator) aggregationCountValues(e *parser.AggregateExpr, grouping []string, valueLabel string, vec Vector, enh *EvalNodeHelper) (Vector, annotations.Annotations) {\n3520: \ttype groupCount struct {\n3521: \t\tlabels labels.Labels\n3522: \t\tcount  int\n3523: \t}\n3524: \tresult := map[uint64]*groupCount{}\n3525: \n3526: \tvar buf []byte\n3527: \tfor _, s := range vec {\n3528: \t\tenh.resetBuilder(s.Metric)\n3529: \t\tif s.H == nil {\n3530: \t\t\tenh.lb.Set(valueLabel, strconv.FormatFloat(s.F, 'f', -1, 64))\n3531: \t\t} else {\n3532: \t\t\tenh.lb.Set(valueLabel, s.H.String())\n3533: \t\t}\n3534: \t\tmetric := enh.lb.Labels()\n3535: \n3536: \t\t// Considering the count_values()\n3537: \t\t// operator is less frequently used than other aggregations, we're fine having to\n3538: \t\t// re-compute the grouping key on each step for this case.\n3539: \t\tvar groupingKey uint64\n3540: \t\tgroupingKey, buf = generateGroupingKey(metric, grouping, e.Without, buf)\n3541: \n3542: \t\tgroup, ok := result[groupingKey]\n3543: \t\t// Add a new group if it doesn't exist.\n3544: \t\tif !ok {\n3545: \t\t\tresult[groupingKey] = &groupCount{\n3546: \t\t\t\tlabels: generateGroupingLabels(enh, metric, e.Without, grouping),\n3547: \t\t\t\tcount:  1,\n3548: \t\t\t}\n3549: \t\t\tcontinue\n3550: \t\t}\n3551: \n3552: \t\tgroup.count++\n3553: \t}\n3554: \n3555: \t// Construct the result Vector from the aggregated groups.\n3556: \tfor _, aggr := range result {\n3557: \t\tenh.Out = append(enh.Out, Sample{\n3558: \t\t\tMetric: aggr.labels,\n3559: \t\t\tF:      float64(aggr.count),\n3560: \t\t})\n3561: \t}\n3562: \treturn enh.Out, nil\n3563: }\n3564: \n3565: func (ev *evaluator) cleanupMetricLabels(v parser.Value) {\n3566: \tif v.Type() == parser.ValueTypeMatrix {\n3567: \t\tmat := v.(Matrix)\n3568: \t\tfor i := range mat {\n3569: \t\t\tif mat[i].DropName {\n3570: \t\t\t\tmat[i].Metric = mat[i].Metric.DropReserved(schema.IsMetadataLabel)\n3571: \t\t\t}\n3572: \t\t}\n3573: \t\tif mat.ContainsSameLabelset() {\n3574: \t\t\tev.errorf(\"vector cannot contain metrics with the same labelset\")\n3575: \t\t}\n3576: \t} else if v.Type() == parser.ValueTypeVector {\n3577: \t\tvec := v.(Vector)\n3578: \t\tfor i := range vec {\n3579: \t\t\tif vec[i].DropName {\n3580: \t\t\t\tvec[i].Metric = vec[i].Metric.DropReserved(schema.IsMetadataLabel)\n3581: \t\t\t}\n3582: \t\t}\n3583: \t\tif vec.ContainsSameLabelset() {\n3584: \t\t\tev.errorf(\"vector cannot contain metrics with the same labelset\")\n3585: \t\t}\n3586: \t}\n3587: }\n3588: \n3589: func addToSeries(ss *Series, ts int64, f float64, h *histogram.FloatHistogram, numSteps int) {\n3590: \tif h == nil {\n3591: \t\tif ss.Floats == nil {\n3592: \t\t\tss.Floats = getFPointSlice(numSteps)\n3593: \t\t}\n3594: \t\tss.Floats = append(ss.Floats, FPoint{T: ts, F: f})\n3595: \t\treturn\n3596: \t}\n3597: \tif ss.Histograms == nil {\n3598: \t\tss.Histograms = getHPointSlice(numSteps)\n3599: \t}\n3600: \tss.Histograms = append(ss.Histograms, HPoint{T: ts, H: h})\n3601: }\n3602: \n3603: func (*evaluator) nextValues(ts int64, series *Series) (f float64, h *histogram.FloatHistogram, b bool) {\n3604: \tswitch {\n3605: \tcase len(series.Floats) > 0 && series.Floats[0].T == ts:\n3606: \t\tf = series.Floats[0].F\n3607: \t\tseries.Floats = series.Floats[1:] // Move input vectors forward\n3608: \tcase len(series.Histograms) > 0 && series.Histograms[0].T == ts:\n3609: \t\th = series.Histograms[0].H\n3610: \t\tseries.Histograms = series.Histograms[1:]\n3611: \tdefault:\n3612: \t\treturn f, h, false\n3613: \t}\n3614: \treturn f, h, true\n3615: }\n3616: \n3617: // handleAggregationError adds the appropriate annotation based on the aggregation error.\n3618: func handleAggregationError(err error, e *parser.AggregateExpr, metricName string, annos *annotations.Annotations) {\n3619: \tpos := e.Expr.PositionRange()\n3620: \tif errors.Is(err, histogram.ErrHistogramsIncompatibleSchema) {\n3621: \t\tannos.Add(annotations.NewMixedExponentialCustomHistogramsWarning(metricName, pos))\n3622: \t} else if errors.Is(err, histogram.ErrHistogramsIncompatibleBounds) {\n3623: \t\tannos.Add(annotations.NewIncompatibleCustomBucketsHistogramsWarning(metricName, pos))\n3624: \t}\n3625: }\n3626: \n3627: // handleVectorBinopError returns the appropriate annotation based on the vector binary operation error.\n3628: func handleVectorBinopError(err error, e *parser.BinaryExpr) annotations.Annotations {\n3629: \tif err == nil {\n3630: \t\treturn nil\n3631: \t}\n3632: \top := parser.ItemTypeStr[e.Op]\n3633: \tpos := e.PositionRange()\n3634: \tif errors.Is(err, annotations.PromQLInfo) || errors.Is(err, annotations.PromQLWarning) {\n3635: \t\treturn annotations.New().Add(err)\n3636: \t}\n3637: \t// TODO(NeerajGartia21): Test the exact annotation output once the testing framework can do so.\n3638: \tif errors.Is(err, histogram.ErrHistogramsIncompatibleSchema) || errors.Is(err, histogram.ErrHistogramsIncompatibleBounds) {\n3639: \t\treturn annotations.New().Add(annotations.NewIncompatibleBucketLayoutInBinOpWarning(op, pos))\n3640: \t}\n3641: \treturn nil\n3642: }\n3643: \n3644: // generateGroupingKey builds and returns the grouping key for the given metric and\n3645: // grouping labels.\n3646: func generateGroupingKey(metric labels.Labels, grouping []string, without bool, buf []byte) (uint64, []byte) {\n3647: \tif without {\n3648: \t\treturn metric.HashWithoutLabels(buf, grouping...)\n3649: \t}\n3650: \n3651: \tif len(grouping) == 0 {\n3652: \t\t// No need to generate any hash if there are no grouping labels.\n3653: \t\treturn 0, buf\n3654: \t}\n3655: \n3656: \treturn metric.HashForLabels(buf, grouping...)\n3657: }\n3658: \n3659: func generateGroupingLabels(enh *EvalNodeHelper, metric labels.Labels, without bool, grouping []string) labels.Labels {\n3660: \tenh.resetBuilder(metric)\n3661: \tswitch {\n3662: \tcase without:\n3663: \t\tenh.lb.Del(grouping...)\n3664: \t\tenh.lb.Del(labels.MetricName)\n3665: \t\treturn enh.lb.Labels()\n3666: \tcase len(grouping) > 0:\n3667: \t\tenh.lb.Keep(grouping...)\n3668: \t\treturn enh.lb.Labels()\n3669: \tdefault:\n3670: \t\treturn labels.EmptyLabels()\n3671: \t}\n3672: }\n3673: \n3674: // btos returns 1 if b is true, 0 otherwise.\n3675: func btos(b bool) float64 {\n3676: \tif b {\n3677: \t\treturn 1\n3678: \t}\n3679: \treturn 0\n3680: }\n3681: \n3682: // changesMetricSchema returns true whether the op operation changes the semantic meaning or\n3683: // schema of the metric.\n3684: func changesMetricSchema(op parser.ItemType) bool {\n3685: \tswitch op {\n3686: \tcase parser.ADD, parser.SUB, parser.DIV, parser.MUL, parser.POW, parser.MOD, parser.ATAN2:\n3687: \t\treturn true\n3688: \tdefault:\n3689: \t\treturn false\n3690: \t}\n3691: }\n3692: \n3693: // NewOriginContext returns a new context with data about the origin attached.\n3694: func NewOriginContext(ctx context.Context, data map[string]any) context.Context {\n3695: \treturn context.WithValue(ctx, QueryOrigin{}, data)\n3696: }\n3697: \n3698: func formatDate(t time.Time) string {\n3699: \treturn t.UTC().Format(\"2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z07:00\")\n3700: }\n3701: \n3702: // unwrapParenExpr does the AST equivalent of removing parentheses around a expression.\n3703: func unwrapParenExpr(e *parser.Expr) {\n3704: \tfor {\n3705: \t\tp, ok := (*e).(*parser.ParenExpr)\n3706: \t\tif !ok {\n3707: \t\t\tbreak\n3708: \t\t}\n3709: \t\t*e = p.Expr\n3710: \t}\n3711: }\n3712: \n3713: func unwrapStepInvariantExpr(e parser.Expr) parser.Expr {\n3714: \tif p, ok := e.(*parser.StepInvariantExpr); ok {\n3715: \t\treturn p.Expr\n3716: \t}\n3717: \treturn e\n3718: }\n3719: \n3720: // PreprocessExpr wraps all possible step invariant parts of the given expression with\n3721: // StepInvariantExpr. It also resolves the preprocessors, evaluates duration expressions\n3722: // into their numeric values and removes superfluous parenthesis on parameters to functions and aggregations.\n3723: func PreprocessExpr(expr parser.Expr, start, end time.Time, step time.Duration) (parser.Expr, error) {\n3724: \tdetectHistogramStatsDecoding(expr)\n3725: \n3726: \tif err := parser.Walk(&durationVisitor{step: step}, expr, nil); err != nil {\n3727: \t\treturn nil, err\n3728: \t}\n3729: \n3730: \t_, shouldWrap := preprocessExprHelper(expr, start, end)\n3731: \tif shouldWrap {\n3732: \t\treturn newStepInvariantExpr(expr), nil\n3733: \t}\n3734: \treturn expr, nil\n3735: }\n3736: \n3737: // preprocessExprHelper wraps child nodes of expr with a StepInvariantExpr,\n3738: // at the highest level within the tree that is step-invariant.\n3739: // Also resolves start() and end() on selector and subquery nodes.\n3740: // Also remove superfluous parenthesis on parameters to functions and aggregations.\n3741: // Return isStepInvariant is true when the whole subexpression is step invariant.\n3742: // Return shoudlWrap is false for cases like MatrixSelector and StringLiteral that never need to be wrapped.\n3743: func preprocessExprHelper(expr parser.Expr, start, end time.Time) (isStepInvariant, shouldWrap bool) {\n3744: \tswitch n := expr.(type) {\n3745: \tcase *parser.VectorSelector:\n3746: \t\tswitch n.StartOrEnd {\n3747: \t\tcase parser.START:\n3748: \t\t\tn.Timestamp = makeInt64Pointer(timestamp.FromTime(start))\n3749: \t\tcase parser.END:\n3750: \t\t\tn.Timestamp = makeInt64Pointer(timestamp.FromTime(end))\n3751: \t\t}\n3752: \t\treturn n.Timestamp != nil, n.Timestamp != nil\n3753: \n3754: \tcase *parser.AggregateExpr:\n3755: \t\tunwrapParenExpr(&n.Expr)\n3756: \t\tunwrapParenExpr(&n.Param)\n3757: \t\treturn preprocessExprHelper(n.Expr, start, end)\n3758: \n3759: \tcase *parser.BinaryExpr:\n3760: \t\tisInvariantLHS, shouldWrapLHS := preprocessExprHelper(n.LHS, start, end)\n3761: \t\tisInvariantRHS, shouldWrapRHS := preprocessExprHelper(n.RHS, start, end)\n3762: \t\tif isInvariantLHS && isInvariantRHS {\n3763: \t\t\treturn true, true\n3764: \t\t}\n3765: \n3766: \t\tif shouldWrapLHS {\n3767: \t\t\tn.LHS = newStepInvariantExpr(n.LHS)\n3768: \t\t}\n3769: \t\tif shouldWrapRHS {\n3770: \t\t\tn.RHS = newStepInvariantExpr(n.RHS)\n3771: \t\t}\n3772: \n3773: \t\treturn false, false\n3774: \n3775: \tcase *parser.Call:\n3776: \t\t_, ok := AtModifierUnsafeFunctions[n.Func.Name]\n3777: \t\tisStepInvariant := !ok\n3778: \t\tshouldWrap := make([]bool, len(n.Args))\n3779: \t\tfor i := range n.Args {\n3780: \t\t\tunwrapParenExpr(&n.Args[i])\n3781: \t\t\tvar argIsStepInvariant bool\n3782: \t\t\targIsStepInvariant, shouldWrap[i] = preprocessExprHelper(n.Args[i], start, end)\n3783: \t\t\tisStepInvariant = isStepInvariant && argIsStepInvariant\n3784: \t\t}\n3785: \n3786: \t\tif isStepInvariant {\n3787: \t\t\t// The function and all arguments are step invariant.\n3788: \t\t\treturn true, true\n3789: \t\t}\n3790: \n3791: \t\tfor i, isi := range shouldWrap {\n3792: \t\t\tif isi {\n3793: \t\t\t\tn.Args[i] = newStepInvariantExpr(n.Args[i])\n3794: \t\t\t}\n3795: \t\t}\n3796: \t\treturn false, false\n3797: \n3798: \tcase *parser.MatrixSelector:\n3799: \t\t// We don't need to wrap a MatrixSelector because functions over range vectors evaluate those directly.\n3800: \t\tisStepInvariant, _ := preprocessExprHelper(n.VectorSelector, start, end)\n3801: \t\treturn isStepInvariant, false\n3802: \n3803: \tcase *parser.SubqueryExpr:\n3804: \t\t// Since we adjust offset for the @ modifier evaluation,\n3805: \t\t// it gets tricky to adjust it for every subquery step.\n3806: \t\t// Hence we wrap the inside of subquery irrespective of\n3807: \t\t// @ on subquery (given it is also step invariant) so that\n3808: \t\t// it is evaluated only once w.r.t. the start time of subquery.\n3809: \t\tisInvariant, _ := preprocessExprHelper(n.Expr, start, end)\n3810: \t\tif isInvariant {\n3811: \t\t\tn.Expr = newStepInvariantExpr(n.Expr)\n3812: \t\t}\n3813: \t\tswitch n.StartOrEnd {\n3814: \t\tcase parser.START:\n3815: \t\t\tn.Timestamp = makeInt64Pointer(timestamp.FromTime(start))\n3816: \t\tcase parser.END:\n3817: \t\t\tn.Timestamp = makeInt64Pointer(timestamp.FromTime(end))\n3818: \t\t}\n3819: \t\treturn n.Timestamp != nil, n.Timestamp != nil\n3820: \n3821: \tcase *parser.ParenExpr:\n3822: \t\treturn preprocessExprHelper(n.Expr, start, end)\n3823: \n3824: \tcase *parser.UnaryExpr:\n3825: \t\treturn preprocessExprHelper(n.Expr, start, end)\n3826: \n3827: \tcase *parser.StringLiteral, *parser.NumberLiteral:\n3828: \t\treturn true, false\n3829: \t}\n3830: \n3831: \tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"found unexpected node %#v\", expr))\n3832: }\n3833: \n3834: func newStepInvariantExpr(expr parser.Expr) parser.Expr {\n3835: \treturn &parser.StepInvariantExpr{Expr: expr}\n3836: }\n3837: \n3838: // setOffsetForAtModifier modifies the offset of vector and matrix selector\n3839: // and subquery in the tree to accommodate the timestamp of @ modifier.\n3840: // The offset is adjusted w.r.t. the given evaluation time.\n3841: func setOffsetForAtModifier(evalTime int64, expr parser.Expr) {\n3842: \tgetOffset := func(ts *int64, originalOffset time.Duration, path []parser.Node) time.Duration {\n3843: \t\tif ts == nil {\n3844: \t\t\treturn originalOffset\n3845: \t\t}\n3846: \n3847: \t\tsubqOffset, _, subqTs := subqueryTimes(path)\n3848: \t\tif subqTs != nil {\n3849: \t\t\tsubqOffset += time.Duration(evalTime-*subqTs) * time.Millisecond\n3850: \t\t}\n3851: \n3852: \t\toffsetForTs := time.Duration(evalTime-*ts) * time.Millisecond\n3853: \t\toffsetDiff := offsetForTs - subqOffset\n3854: \t\treturn originalOffset + offsetDiff\n3855: \t}\n3856: \n3857: \tparser.Inspect(expr, func(node parser.Node, path []parser.Node) error {\n3858: \t\tswitch n := node.(type) {\n3859: \t\tcase *parser.VectorSelector:\n3860: \t\t\tn.Offset = getOffset(n.Timestamp, n.OriginalOffset, path)\n3861: \n3862: \t\tcase *parser.MatrixSelector:\n3863: \t\t\tvs := n.VectorSelector.(*parser.VectorSelector)\n3864: \t\t\tvs.Offset = getOffset(vs.Timestamp, vs.OriginalOffset, path)\n3865: \n3866: \t\tcase *parser.SubqueryExpr:\n3867: \t\t\tn.Offset = getOffset(n.Timestamp, n.OriginalOffset, path)\n3868: \t\t}\n3869: \t\treturn nil\n3870: \t})\n3871: }\n3872: \n3873: // detectHistogramStatsDecoding modifies the expression by setting the\n3874: // SkipHistogramBuckets field in those vector selectors for which it is safe to\n3875: // return only histogram statistics (sum and count), excluding histogram spans\n3876: // and buckets. The function can be treated as an optimization and is not\n3877: // required for correctness.\n3878: func detectHistogramStatsDecoding(expr parser.Expr) {\n3879: \tparser.Inspect(expr, func(node parser.Node, path []parser.Node) error {\n3880: \t\tn, ok := (node).(*parser.VectorSelector)\n3881: \t\tif !ok {\n3882: \t\t\treturn nil\n3883: \t\t}\n3884: \n3885: \t\tfor i := len(path) - 1; i > 0; i-- { // Walk backwards up the path.\n3886: \t\t\tcall, ok := path[i].(*parser.Call)\n3887: \t\t\tif !ok {\n3888: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n3889: \t\t\t}\n3890: \t\t\tswitch call.Func.Name {\n3891: \t\t\tcase \"histogram_count\", \"histogram_sum\", \"histogram_avg\":\n3892: \t\t\t\tn.SkipHistogramBuckets = true\n3893: \t\t\tcase \"histogram_quantile\", \"histogram_fraction\":\n3894: \t\t\t\tn.SkipHistogramBuckets = false\n3895: \t\t\tdefault:\n3896: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n3897: \t\t\t}\n3898: \t\t\tbreak\n3899: \t\t}\n3900: \t\treturn errors.New(\"stop\")\n3901: \t})\n3902: }\n3903: \n3904: func makeInt64Pointer(val int64) *int64 {\n3905: \tvalp := new(int64)\n3906: \t*valp = val\n3907: \treturn valp\n3908: }\n3909: \n3910: // RatioSampler allows unit-testing (previously: Randomizer).\n3911: type RatioSampler interface {\n3912: \t// Return this sample \"offset\" between [0.0, 1.0]\n3913: \tsampleOffset(ts int64, sample *Sample) float64\n3914: \tAddRatioSample(r float64, sample *Sample) bool\n3915: }\n3916: \n3917: // HashRatioSampler uses Hash(labels.String()) / maxUint64 as a \"deterministic\"\n3918: // value in [0.0, 1.0].\n3919: type HashRatioSampler struct{}\n3920: \n3921: var ratiosampler RatioSampler = NewHashRatioSampler()\n3922: \n3923: func NewHashRatioSampler() *HashRatioSampler {\n3924: \treturn &HashRatioSampler{}\n3925: }\n3926: \n3927: func (*HashRatioSampler) sampleOffset(_ int64, sample *Sample) float64 {\n3928: \tconst (\n3929: \t\tfloat64MaxUint64 = float64(math.MaxUint64)\n3930: \t)\n3931: \treturn float64(sample.Metric.Hash()) / float64MaxUint64\n3932: }\n3933: \n3934: func (s *HashRatioSampler) AddRatioSample(ratioLimit float64, sample *Sample) bool {\n3935: \t// If ratioLimit >= 0: add sample if sampleOffset is lesser than ratioLimit\n3936: \t//\n3937: \t// 0.0        ratioLimit                1.0\n3938: \t//  [---------|--------------------------]\n3939: \t//  [#########...........................]\n3940: \t//\n3941: \t// e.g.:\n3942: \t//   sampleOffset==0.3 && ratioLimit==0.4\n3943: \t//     0.3 < 0.4 ? --> add sample\n3944: \t//\n3945: \t// Else if ratioLimit < 0: add sample if rand() return the \"complement\" of ratioLimit>=0 case\n3946: \t// (loosely similar behavior to negative array index in other programming languages)\n3947: \t//\n3948: \t// 0.0       1+ratioLimit               1.0\n3949: \t//  [---------|--------------------------]\n3950: \t//  [.........###########################]\n3951: \t//\n3952: \t// e.g.:\n3953: \t//   sampleOffset==0.3 && ratioLimit==-0.6\n3954: \t//     0.3 >= 0.4 ? --> don't add sample\n3955: \tsampleOffset := s.sampleOffset(sample.T, sample)\n3956: \treturn (ratioLimit >= 0 && sampleOffset < ratioLimit) ||\n3957: \t\t(ratioLimit < 0 && sampleOffset >= (1.0+ratioLimit))\n3958: }\n3959: \n3960: type histogramStatsSeries struct {\n3961: \tstorage.Series\n3962: }\n3963: \n3964: func newHistogramStatsSeries(series storage.Series) *histogramStatsSeries {\n3965: \treturn &histogramStatsSeries{Series: series}\n3966: }\n3967: \n3968: func (s histogramStatsSeries) Iterator(it chunkenc.Iterator) chunkenc.Iterator {\n3969: \t// Try to reuse the iterator if we can.\n3970: \tif statsIterator, ok := it.(*HistogramStatsIterator); ok {\n3971: \t\tstatsIterator.Reset(s.Series.Iterator(statsIterator.Iterator))\n3972: \t\treturn statsIterator\n3973: \t}\n3974: \n3975: \treturn NewHistogramStatsIterator(s.Series.Iterator(it))\n3976: }\n3977: \n3978: // gatherVector gathers a Vector for ts from the series in input.\n3979: // output is used as a buffer.\n3980: // If bufHelpers and seriesHelpers are provided, seriesHelpers[i] is appended to bufHelpers for every input index i.\n3981: // The gathered Vector and bufHelper are returned.\n3982: func (ev *evaluator) gatherVector(ts int64, input Matrix, output Vector, bufHelpers, seriesHelpers []EvalSeriesHelper) (Vector, []EvalSeriesHelper) {\n3983: \toutput = output[:0]\n3984: \tfor i, series := range input {\n3985: \t\tswitch {\n3986: \t\tcase len(series.Floats) > 0 && series.Floats[0].T == ts:\n3987: \t\t\ts := series.Floats[0]\n3988: \t\t\toutput = append(output, Sample{Metric: series.Metric, F: s.F, T: ts, DropName: series.DropName})\n3989: \t\t\t// Move input vectors forward so we don't have to re-scan the same\n3990: \t\t\t// past points at the next step.\n3991: \t\t\tinput[i].Floats = series.Floats[1:]\n3992: \t\tcase len(series.Histograms) > 0 && series.Histograms[0].T == ts:\n3993: \t\t\ts := series.Histograms[0]\n3994: \t\t\toutput = append(output, Sample{Metric: series.Metric, H: s.H, T: ts, DropName: series.DropName})\n3995: \t\t\tinput[i].Histograms = series.Histograms[1:]\n3996: \t\tdefault:\n3997: \t\t\tcontinue\n3998: \t\t}\n3999: \t\tif len(seriesHelpers) > 0 {\n4000: \t\t\tbufHelpers = append(bufHelpers, seriesHelpers[i])\n4001: \t\t}\n4002: \n4003: \t\t// Don't add histogram size here because we only\n4004: \t\t// copy the pointer above, not the whole\n4005: \t\t// histogram.\n4006: \t\tev.currentSamples++\n4007: \t\tif ev.currentSamples > ev.maxSamples {\n4008: \t\t\tev.error(ErrTooManySamples(env))\n4009: \t\t}\n4010: \t}\n4011: \tev.samplesStats.UpdatePeak(ev.currentSamples)\n4012: \n4013: \treturn output, bufHelpers\n4014: }\n4015: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4@ba16cf5a7437eb3b6b71b74557b7a67e1975ad0f/-/blob/promql/engine.go%3FL2000-4014"
  }
}

{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"115: }\\n116: \\n117: // QueryEngine defines the interface for the *promql.Engine, so it can be replaced, wrapped or mocked.\\n118: type QueryEngine interface {\\n119: \\tNewInstantQuery(ctx context.Context, q storage.Queryable, opts QueryOpts, qs string, ts time.Time) (Query, error)\\n120: \\tNewRangeQuery(ctx context.Context, q storage.Queryable, opts QueryOpts, qs string, start, end time.Time, interval time.Duration) (Query, error)\\n121: }\\n122: \\n123: var _ QueryLogger = (*logging.JSONFileLogger)(nil)\\n124: \\n125: // QueryLogger is an interface that can be used to log all the queries logged\\n126: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4@ba16cf5a7437eb3b6b71b74557b7a67e1975ad0f/-/blob/promql/engine.go%3FL115-125\"}",
